content.content:
1	I have enough research to compose the complete technical design document. I'll proceed directly to writing it since this is a single-shot deliverable that doesn't benefit from task tracking overhead.
2	
3	---
4	
5	# Marketify — Technical Architecture Document (v0.1)
6	
7	**Scope:** Backend, data, APIs, workflows. UI/UX is out of scope (handled by sibling agent).
8	**Stack:** Expo + React Native + Reanimated + Supabase (Postgres, Storage, Edge Functions, Realtime) + TikTok + Instagram/Meta APIs.
9	**Author's posture:** Opinionated. Where the PRD is silent, I pick a default and justify it. Override any call in review.
10	
11	---
12	
13	## 1. PRD Gap Analysis
14	
15	Each gap lists: **Q** (question), **Why it matters**, **→ Decision**.
16	
17	### 1.1 Identity & Auth
18	
19	| # | Gap | Why it matters | Decision |
20	|---|---|---|---|
21	| 1 | "No auth" contradicts needing to fetch private creator metrics from TikTok/IG. Without OAuth we cannot call `user.info.stats` or Instagram Graph insights. | Core value prop (pre-conditions on follower/view counts) depends on real metrics. | **Hybrid:** no email/password app auth, but **require TikTok OAuth and/or Instagram OAuth at onboarding as the "proof of handle"**. OAuth access_token + refresh_token IS the session identity. Username is derived from the verified TikTok/IG handle. Device-scoped anon `session_id` cookie is persisted locally. A creator must link at least one platform to do anything meaningful. (See §3e for full recommendation.) |
22	| 2 | Username uniqueness + collisions | Two users can claim `@bulutyazgan` on different platforms. | **Primary key = internal UUID.** Display identifier = `{platform}:{handle}`. Uniqueness is `(platform, platform_user_id)` from OAuth, NOT the handle string (handles can change). |
23	| 3 | Can a single user be both creator and lister? | PRD says "chosen at signup." | **One role per account in MVP.** A person who wants both creates two accounts. Keeps RLS and UX simple. Revisit in v2. |
24	| 4 | Lister auth — listers don't have TikTok "creator" metrics to verify. | How do we trust a company is a company? | **MVP: self-declared, unverified.** Lister signs up with email + org name + username. We accept this at face value. Payment/KYC is post-MVP. |
25	
26	### 1.2 Listing / Campaign Lifecycle
27	
28	| # | Gap | Why it matters | Decision |
29	|---|---|---|---|
30	| 5 | Listing states beyond active/inactive (draft, closed, archived, expired)? | Listers need to save drafts; finished campaigns need to stop showing. | States: `draft → active → (paused | closed | expired) → archived`. `paused` = inactive, reversible. `closed` = fulfilled/manually ended, terminal. `expired` = past end_date, terminal. |
31	| 6 | Do listings have end dates / expiration? | Prevents stale campaigns cluttering feed. | **Yes — optional `end_date`.** If null, runs until manually closed. Edge function `expire_listings` runs daily via `pg_cron`. |
32	| 7 | Submission cap per campaign (max approved submissions)? | A bounty for "10 videos" behaves differently from an open call. | Add `max_submissions` (nullable = unlimited). When `approved_submissions_count >= max_submissions`, listing auto-transitions to `closed`, pending applications stay visitable but cannot be approved. |
33	| 8 | Applications cap per creator per listing? | Prevents spam reapplications. | **One active application per (creator, listing) pair.** Creator may reapply after rejection/cancellation only if listing is still active AND they pass eligibility again. Enforced by partial unique index (see schema). |
34	| 9 | "Requirements or price change cancels pending applications" — what exactly counts? | Ambiguous in PRD. | **Listing versioning.** Any change to price, pre-conditions, post-conditions, sample videos, or max_submissions bumps `version`. All applications in state `pending` on prior version → auto-`cancelled_listing_edit`. Approved applications and submissions are NOT cancelled (the creator already has a contract). Title/description text edits do NOT bump version. |
35	| 10 | Can lister edit post-conditions without cancelling? | PRD says only requirements/price. | **No — post-condition changes DO bump version and cancel pending applications.** Rationale: post-conditions define what the creator agrees to deliver. Changing them mid-queue is bait-and-switch. |
36	
37	### 1.3 Applications & Submissions
38	
39	| # | Gap | Why it matters | Decision |
40	|---|---|---|---|
41	| 11 | Creator withdraws application? | Creators change their mind. | **Yes.** Add `withdrawn` state. Only from `pending`. |
42	| 12 | What if metrics drop below threshold between apply and submission? | Gamed by applying when metrics are fresh, then ghosting real work. | **Re-check at approve-time.** If below threshold when lister tries to approve, approval API returns 409 `INELIGIBLE_NOW` and lister can decide to override (one-click override allowed, logged). **No re-check at submission-time** — once approved, the creator's eligibility is locked. |
43	| 13 | Multiple videos per submission — one URL or batched? | A campaign may require a set of videos. | **Submission has 1..N video URLs.** Lister's post-conditions include `min_videos` (default 1). Submission is reviewed as a unit. |
44	| 14 | Resubmission after rejection? | Creators may need to fix and retry. | Add `revision_requested` state. Flow: `pending → revision_requested → pending` (up to 2 revisions; `revision_count` on submission row). After that, reject is final. |
45	| 15 | Creator reapplies after rejection/cancellation? | See #8. | After `rejected` or `cancelled_listing_edit`: allowed if listing still active. After `withdrawn`: allowed immediately. After lister hits "block creator" (post-MVP): never. |
46	| 16 | Disputes (creator says submitted, lister says no) | Hard case; immutable log needed. | Write all status transitions to an append-only `events` table. Expose support email in-app. No in-app arbitration in MVP. |
47	| 17 | Expiration of pending applications/submissions | Listers can ghost. | `applications`: auto-expire 14 days after creation if still `pending`. `submissions`: auto-expire 7 days after creation if still `pending`. Both via `pg_cron`. Configurable on listing in v2. |
48	
49	### 1.4 Money
50	
51	| # | Gap | Why it matters | Decision |
52	|---|---|---|---|
53	| 18 | Does MVP actually handle payment? | PRD says "price" and "bounty" — ambiguous. | **No real money in MVP.** `price` is display-only ("advertised bounty"). Payment is off-platform (lister contacts creator post-approval). Add a `payment_status` column to submissions for future. Flag this as v2. |
54	
55	### 1.5 Notifications
56	
57	| # | Gap | Why it matters | Decision |
58	|---|---|---|---|
59	| 19 | Which events notify? Which channels? | PRD says "notified" with no spec. | **Events:** application approved/rejected/cancelled; submission approved/rejected/revision_requested; listing version changed (for applicants); metrics refresh failed. **Channels MVP:** in-app notification row + Expo Push (via Edge Function). Email out of scope. |
60	| 20 | Realtime for lister seeing new submission? | UX question. | **Supabase Realtime subscriptions** on `submissions` filtered by `listing.lister_id`. Fallback polling on app resume. |
61	
62	### 1.6 Metrics / External APIs
63	
64	| # | Gap | Why it matters | Decision |
65	|---|---|---|---|
66	| 21 | Metric refresh rate limits | Creator could DDOS our API budget. | **Server-side throttle:** manual refresh once per 6 hours per (user, platform). Background refresh daily via `pg_cron`. |
67	| 22 | Handling API failures | Expired tokens, rate limit, account deleted. | `metric_snapshots.status = fresh | stale | refreshing | failed`. Show "metrics outdated" badge if `fetched_at < now() - 7 days`. After 3 failed refreshes, mark link `failed_fetch` and prompt re-OAuth. |
68	| 23 | Deleted / private / removed video at submission time | Creator submits, then deletes video. | Validate URL via oEmbed at submission time (reject if 404). Re-validate via Edge Function on a nightly sweep for `approved` submissions — flag but don't change state. |
69	| 24 | Post-submission performance verification | Lister wants to reward high-performing videos (v2 feature). | Out of MVP scope, but store `video_external_id` so we can later call `GET /v2/video/query/` (TikTok) or `GET /<IG_MEDIA_ID>?fields=insights` (IG) to fetch view/like counts. |
70	
71	### 1.7 Security / Abuse
72	
73	| # | Gap | Why | Decision |
74	|---|---|---|---|
75	| 25 | Lister posts campaign that's spam/illegal | Marketplace trust. | Manual admin review post-MVP. MVP: all listings live immediately; add `report_listing` button → admin table. |
76	| 26 | Creator submits same video to 10 listings | Double-dipping. | Enforce `UNIQUE(submission.video_url)` globally? No — same video might legitimately match two campaigns. Instead: flag and surface to lister. No hard block MVP. |
77	
78	---
79	
80	## 2. State Machines
81	
82	Notation: `transition [actor: user|lister|system|time]`.
83	
84	### 2.1 Listing
85	
86	```
87	(init) ──create[lister]──> draft
88	draft ──publish[lister]──> active
89	active ──pause[lister]──> paused
90	paused ──resume[lister]──> active
91	active ──close[lister]──> closed
92	active ──end_date_passed[time]──> expired
93	active ──max_submissions_reached[system]──> closed
94	closed ──archive[lister]──> archived
95	expired ──archive[lister|time:30d]──> archived
96	paused ──archive[lister]──> archived
97	
98	Side-effect on any edit that mutates price/pre-cond/post-cond/samples/max_submissions while active:
99	  version++ AND trigger cascade (see §2.3)
100	```
101	
102	Terminal states: `archived`.
103	
104	### 2.2 Application
105	
106	```
107	(init) ──submit[creator, gated by eligibility]──> pending
108	pending ──approve[lister]──> approved
109	pending ──reject[lister]──> rejected
110	pending ──withdraw[creator]──> withdrawn
111	pending ──listing_version_bump[system]──> cancelled_listing_edit
112	pending ──14d_no_action[time]──> expired
113	pending ──listing_closed_or_archived[system]──> cancelled_listing_closed
114	
115	approved ──submission_created[system]──> (stays approved; submission owns downstream state)
116	approved ──listing_archived[system]──> cancelled_listing_closed  // rare edge case
117	```
118	
119	Terminal: `approved` (once submission exists), `rejected`, `withdrawn`, `cancelled_*`, `expired`.
120	
121	### 2.3 Submission
122	
123	```
124	(init, requires application.status=approved) ──create[creator]──> pending
125	pending ──approve[lister]──> approved
126	pending ──reject[lister]──> rejected
127	pending ──request_revision[lister]──> revision_requested
128	revision_requested ──resubmit[creator]──> pending   // max 2 revisions
129	pending ──7d_no_action[time]──> expired
130	```
131	
132	Terminal: `approved`, `rejected`, `expired`.
133	
134	### 2.4 Social Link
135	
136	```
137	(init) ──oauth_complete[creator]──> linked
138	linked ──refresh_success[system|creator]──> linked
139	linked ──refresh_fail x3[system]──> failed_fetch
140	failed_fetch ──reauth[creator]──> linked
141	linked ──unlink[creator]──> unlinked
142	```
143	
144	### 2.5 Metric Snapshot
145	
146	```
147	(init, on link) ──fetch_queued[system]──> refreshing
148	refreshing ──success[system]──> fresh
149	refreshing ──fail[system]──> failed
150	fresh ──age > 24h[time]──> stale
151	stale ──fetch_queued[creator_manual | system_daily]──> refreshing
152	failed ──retry[system|creator]──> refreshing
153	```
154	
155	Rule: eligibility filtering uses `fresh` OR `stale` (not `failed`). Display warning if `stale`.
156	
157	---
158	
159	## 3. Best-Practice Research (context7-verified)
160	
161	### 3a. TikTok — Creator Metrics
162	
163	**Source:** `/websites/developers_tiktok_doc` — `https://developers.tiktok.com/doc/tiktok-api-v2-get-user-info`, `https://developers.tiktok.com/doc/tiktok-api-scopes`.
164	
165	- **Endpoint:** `GET https://open.tiktokapis.com/v2/user/info/` with `fields=follower_count,following_count,likes_count,video_count,username,is_verified,display_name`.
166	- **Required scopes:**
167	  - `user.info.basic` (open_id, display_name, avatar) — auto-granted with Login Kit.
168	  - `user.info.profile` (bio, verified, deep link) — optional.
169	  - `user.info.stats` — **REQUIRED for follower_count, likes_count, video_count**.
170	  - `video.list` — required to page `POST /v2/video/list/` and derive avg views from last N videos.
171	- **Auth flow:** OAuth 2.0 via Login Kit. We get `access_token` (short-lived) + `refresh_token` (typically 365 days). Must refresh before expiry.
172	- **Video stats per video:** `view_count`, `like_count`, `comment_count`, `share_count` available on Video Object (`/v2/video/list/` with `fields=id,view_count,like_count,...`). This is how we compute `avg_views_last_n`.
173	- **Rate limits:** TikTok documents per-app QPS on each endpoint (varies; typically 10 QPS per app for `/v2/user/info/`). We MUST not fan-out concurrent refreshes; use a queue.
174	- **Research API** (`/research/user/info/`) surfaces `follower_count` publicly but is gated behind a researcher approval process — **not available to us in MVP**.
175	- **oEmbed** (`https://www.tiktok.com/oembed?url=...`) is public and unauthenticated but returns ONLY embed HTML + thumbnail + author — **no follower count, no view count**. Useful for rendering submitted videos, not for eligibility.
176	
177	**Conclusion:** No path to real follower/view counts without TikTok OAuth on the creator side. "No auth, just handles" from PRD is infeasible for pre-conditions.
178	
179	### 3b. Instagram — Creator Metrics
180	
181	**Source:** `/websites/developers_facebook_instagram-platform` — `https://developers.facebook.com/docs/instagram-platform/instagram-graph-api/reference/ig-user/business_discovery`, `https://developers.facebook.com/docs/instagram-platform/insights`.
182	
183	- **`business_discovery`** is the only Graph API path that returns follower_count/media_count for *another* account by username. Important limitations:
184	  - The *querying* account must be an Instagram Business or Creator account.
185	  - The *target* account must also be Business or Creator (personal accounts are invisible to this API).
186	  - Requires permissions `instagram_basic`, `instagram_manage_insights`, `pages_read_engagement` on the querying user's access token.
187	  - Public-looking but NOT actually unauthenticated.
188	- **Direct account (own metrics):** `GET /<IG_USER_ID>?fields=followers_count,media_count,username,biography` — Business/Creator accounts only.
189	- **Media insights:** `GET /<IG_MEDIA_ID>?fields=insights.metric(views,likes,comments,shares,...)`. Note per-changelog: `impressions`, `plays`, and `clips_replays_count` deprecated April 2025; use `views` going forward.
190	- **Instagram Basic Display API:** deprecated by Meta for new apps; do not use.
191	- **oEmbed** (`/instagram_oembed`) — public thumbnail + author only; requires app access token; no metrics.
192	
193	**Conclusion:** For IG we need the creator to log in via Instagram OAuth **AND** they must have a Business/Creator account (switch is free and in-app on IG). Personal IG accounts = we cannot fetch metrics, period.
194	
195	### 3c. Displaying a Submitted TikTok Video
196	
197	- Use oEmbed (`GET https://www.tiktok.com/oembed?url=<share_url>`) → returns `html`, `thumbnail_url`, `author_name`, `title`. Renderable via WebView.
198	- Alternative: construct `embed_link = https://www.tiktok.com/embed/v2/<video_id>` and load in WebView directly.
199	- **Performance verification:** requires the creator's OAuth token AND `video.list` scope, then `POST /v2/video/list/` to find the video and read `view_count`. Out of MVP scope.
200	
201	### 3d. Displaying a Submitted Instagram Reel
202	
203	- `GET /instagram_oembed?url=<reel_url>&access_token=<app_token>` → thumbnail + attribution. No embed HTML as full-featured as TikTok.
204	- Recommended: parse the reel shortcode from URL (`/reel/{shortcode}/` or `/p/{shortcode}/`), display thumbnail from oEmbed, and deep-link to Instagram app on tap.
205	- **Performance verification:** the creator must have linked their IG Business account AND we need the `IG_MEDIA_ID`. Resolve via `ig_hashtag_search` / the creator's own `/me/media` — out of MVP scope.
206	
207	### 3e. MVP Feasibility with "No Auth, No Verification"
208	
209	**I am overriding the PRD on this point.** The constraint as written is incompatible with the core value proposition.
210	
211	**Recommended MVP path:**
212	
213	1. **No app-level auth (email/password).** Correct, keep this.
214	2. **REQUIRE TikTok OAuth and/or Instagram OAuth at creator onboarding.** At least one. Treat the OAuth account as the identity. No handle is typed by hand.
215	3. **Self-reported handles rejected entirely.** Metrics come from OAuth calls only.
216	4. **Listers sign up with username + email** (no social OAuth required — they aren't being filtered on metrics).
217	5. For IG specifically, require Business/Creator conversion in onboarding flow (show a 3-step guide). If creator only has TikTok, they see only TikTok-gated listings.
218	
219	Rationale: a marketplace built on unverified follower counts is a fraud vector on day one. Listers pay (or will pay) for access to creators with real audiences. Fake metrics = churn. OAuth is free and a one-time friction.
220	
221	**Scope cut alternative** (if the above is rejected): launch as "discovery only" — no pre-conditions, creators browse all listings, apply manually, lister eyeballs the handle. Don't ship pre-conditions at all in v1. (See §8.)
222	
223	---
224	
225	## 4. Supabase Database Schema
226	
227	Applied rules from `supabase-postgres-best-practices`: RLS enabled on all public tables; FKs indexed; partial indexes for hot filters; `security_invoker` on views; `security definer` functions in a private schema; composite indexes ordered by selectivity.
228	
229	### 4.1 Tables Overview
230	
231	| Table | Purpose |
232	|---|---|
233	| `users` | Unified account row; role-gated. |
234	| `creator_profiles` | 1:1 extension for creator-specific fields. |
235	| `lister_profiles` | 1:1 extension for lister-specific fields. |
236	| `social_links` | OAuth-linked TikTok/IG accounts per user. |
237	| `metric_snapshots` | Historized metric data per social_link. Latest-flagged. |
238	| `listings` | Core campaign row. Mutable fields live here. |
239	| `listing_versions` | Immutable snapshots of versioned fields. Applications FK to a version. |
240	| `listing_conditions` | Pre + post conditions, structured rows tied to a listing_version. |
241	| `sample_videos` | Sample video URLs per listing_version. |
242	| `applications` | Creator → listing_version. |
243	| `submissions` | Final deliverables linked to an approved application. |
244	| `submission_videos` | N video URLs per submission. |
245	| `notifications` | In-app notification inbox. |
246	| `push_tokens` | Expo push tokens per user. |
247	| `events` | Append-only audit log. |
248	| `oauth_states` | CSRF/state tokens for OAuth callbacks (short-lived). |
249	
250	### 4.2 Enums
251	
252	```sql
253	create type user_role as enum ('creator', 'lister');
254	create type platform as enum ('tiktok', 'instagram');
255	create type listing_status as enum ('draft', 'active', 'paused', 'closed', 'expired', 'archived');
256	create type application_status as enum (
257	  'pending', 'approved', 'rejected', 'withdrawn',
258	  'cancelled_listing_edit', 'cancelled_listing_closed', 'expired'
259	);
260	create type submission_status as enum (
261	  'pending', 'approved', 'rejected', 'revision_requested', 'expired'
262	);
263	create type social_link_status as enum ('linked', 'unlinked', 'failed_fetch');
264	create type metric_status as enum ('fresh', 'stale', 'refreshing', 'failed');
265	create type condition_kind as enum ('pre', 'post');
266	create type condition_metric as enum (
267	  'min_followers', 'min_avg_views_last_n', 'min_total_likes',
268	  'min_videos_posted', 'verified_only',
269	  'post_min_video_duration_sec', 'post_max_video_duration_sec',
270	  'post_min_video_count', 'post_must_mention', 'post_family_friendly',
271	  'post_must_tag_account'
272	);
273	create type notification_kind as enum (
274	  'application_approved', 'application_rejected', 'application_cancelled',
275	  'submission_received', 'submission_approved', 'submission_rejected',
276	  'submission_revision_requested', 'listing_version_changed',
277	  'metrics_refresh_failed'
278	);
279	```
280	
281	### 4.3 Denormalization Notes
282	
283	- `creator_profiles.latest_follower_count_tiktok`, `latest_follower_count_instagram`, `latest_avg_views_tiktok`, `latest_avg_views_instagram`, `metrics_fetched_at` — denormalized from `metric_snapshots` for **O(1) eligibility filtering** across thousands of listings. Kept in sync by trigger.
284	- `listings.current_version_id` — FK to `listing_versions`, denormalized to avoid an extra join on every feed query.
285	- `listings.active_pending_applications_count`, `approved_submissions_count` — counters, trigger-maintained.
286	- `applications.listing_version_id` — denormalized so cancellation logic becomes a single UPDATE when version changes.
287	
288	### 4.4 RLS Model (without Supabase Auth)
289	
290	Since we have no `auth.uid()`, we'll use a custom JWT issued by our `auth_signin` edge function (signed with project JWT secret, same verification path Supabase uses). JWT payload includes:
291	```json
292	{ "sub": "<user_id>", "role": "creator|lister", "session_id": "<uuid>" }
293	```
294	RLS policies read `auth.jwt() ->> 'sub'` as the user id. **`sub` is stored in `app_metadata`-equivalent claims we control (signed by us), not user-editable — safe for authz decisions.** We explicitly do NOT use `user_metadata` for authorization (per Supabase security checklist).
295	
296	### 4.5 Indexes — Key Decisions
297	
298	- Feed query: `listings WHERE status='active' ORDER BY created_at DESC` — partial index on `(status, created_at DESC) WHERE status='active'`.
299	- Eligibility pre-filter: denormalized creator metrics compared client-side after server returns filterable listings; server supplies `min_required_followers` column per listing row (computed — see §6) indexed as `btree`.
300	- Application uniqueness: `UNIQUE (listing_id, creator_id) WHERE status='pending' OR status='approved'` — partial unique.
301	- Notifications inbox: `(user_id, read_at NULLS FIRST, created_at DESC)`.
302	
303	### 4.6 Triggers / Functions
304	
305	All in private schema `app_private` (security-definer path).
306	
307	| Function | Trigger |
308	|---|---|
309	| `app_private.handle_listing_mutation()` | BEFORE UPDATE on `listings` — if any versioned field changed, INSERT into `listing_versions`, set `current_version_id`, and queue application cascade. |
310	| `app_private.cascade_cancel_pending_apps(listing_id, reason)` | Called by trigger + edge functions. Updates applications + emits notifications. |
311	| `app_private.denorm_creator_metrics()` | AFTER INSERT on `metric_snapshots` where `is_latest=true` — pushes to `creator_profiles`. |
312	| `app_private.expire_stale()` | Called by `pg_cron` daily. Expires pending applications (14d) + pending submissions (7d) + past-end_date listings. |
313	| `app_private.auto_close_on_max_submissions()` | AFTER UPDATE on `submissions` when status → `approved` — if count >= max_submissions, close listing. |
314	| `app_private.emit_event()` | Helper called by every state-changing function; inserts into `events`. |
315	
316	### 4.7 Full Migration SQL
317	
318	```sql
319	-- =========================================================
320	-- 0. Extensions & schemas
321	-- =========================================================
322	create extension if not exists "pgcrypto";
323	create extension if not exists "pg_cron";
324	
325	create schema if not exists app_private;
326	revoke all on schema app_private from anon, authenticated;
327	
328	-- =========================================================
329	-- 1. Enums
330	-- =========================================================
331	create type user_role as enum ('creator', 'lister');
332	create type platform as enum ('tiktok', 'instagram');
333	create type listing_status as enum ('draft', 'active', 'paused', 'closed', 'expired', 'archived');
334	create type application_status as enum (
335	  'pending', 'approved',

I have enough research to compose the complete technical design document. I'll proceed directly to writing it since this is a single-shot deliverable that doesn't benefit from task tracking overhead.

---

# Marketify — Technical Architecture Document (v0.1)

**Scope:** Backend, data, APIs, workflows. UI/UX is out of scope (handled by sibling agent).
**Stack:** Expo + React Native + Reanimated + Supabase (Postgres, Storage, Edge Functions, Realtime) + TikTok + Instagram/Meta APIs.
**Author's posture:** Opinionated. Where the PRD is silent, I pick a default and justify it. Override any call in review.

---

## 1. PRD Gap Analysis

Each gap lists: **Q** (question), **Why it matters**, **→ Decision**.

### 1.1 Identity & Auth

| # | Gap | Why it matters | Decision |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | "No auth" contradicts needing to fetch private creator metrics from TikTok/IG. Without OAuth we cannot call `user.info.stats` or Instagram Graph insights. | Core value prop (pre-conditions on follower/view counts) depends on real metrics. | **Hybrid:** no email/password app auth, but **require TikTok OAuth and/or Instagram OAuth at onboarding as the "proof of handle"**. OAuth access_token + refresh_token IS the session identity. Username is derived from the verified TikTok/IG handle. Device-scoped anon `session_id` cookie is persisted locally. A creator must link at least one platform to do anything meaningful. (See §3e for full recommendation.) |
| 2 | Username uniqueness + collisions | Two users can claim `@bulutyazgan` on different platforms. | **Primary key = internal UUID.** Display identifier = `{platform}:{handle}`. Uniqueness is `(platform, platform_user_id)` from OAuth, NOT the handle string (handles can change). |
| 3 | Can a single user be both creator and lister? | PRD says "chosen at signup." | **One role per account in MVP.** A person who wants both creates two accounts. Keeps RLS and UX simple. Revisit in v2. |
| 4 | Lister auth — listers don't have TikTok "creator" metrics to verify. | How do we trust a company is a company? | **MVP: self-declared, unverified.** Lister signs up with email + org name + username. We accept this at face value. Payment/KYC is post-MVP. |

### 1.2 Listing / Campaign Lifecycle

| # | Gap | Why it matters | Decision |
|---|---|---|---|
| 5 | Listing states beyond active/inactive (draft, closed, archived, expired)? | Listers need to save drafts; finished campaigns need to stop showing. | States: `draft → active → (paused | closed | expired) → archived`. `paused` = inactive, reversible. `closed` = fulfilled/manually ended, terminal. `expired` = past end_date, terminal. |
| 6 | Do listings have end dates / expiration? | Prevents stale campaigns cluttering feed. | **Yes — optional `end_date`.** If null, runs until manually closed. Edge function `expire_listings` runs daily via `pg_cron`. |
| 7 | Submission cap per campaign (max approved submissions)? | A bounty for "10 videos" behaves differently from an open call. | Add `max_submissions` (nullable = unlimited). When `approved_submissions_count >= max_submissions`, listing auto-transitions to `closed`, pending applications stay visitable but cannot be approved. |
| 8 | Applications cap per creator per listing? | Prevents spam reapplications. | **One active application per (creator, listing) pair.** Creator may reapply after rejection/cancellation only if listing is still active AND they pass eligibility again. Enforced by partial unique index (see schema). |
| 9 | "Requirements or price change cancels pending applications" — what exactly counts? | Ambiguous in PRD. | **Listing versioning.** Any change to price, pre-conditions, post-conditions, sample videos, or max_submissions bumps `version`. All applications in state `pending` on prior version → auto-`cancelled_listing_edit`. Approved applications and submissions are NOT cancelled (the creator already has a contract). Title/description text edits do NOT bump version. |
| 10 | Can lister edit post-conditions without cancelling? | PRD says only requirements/price. | **No — post-condition changes DO bump version and cancel pending applications.** Rationale: post-conditions define what the creator agrees to deliver. Changing them mid-queue is bait-and-switch. |

### 1.3 Applications & Submissions

| # | Gap | Why it matters | Decision |
|---|---|---|---|
| 11 | Creator withdraws application? | Creators change their mind. | **Yes.** Add `withdrawn` state. Only from `pending`. |
| 12 | What if metrics drop below threshold between apply and submission? | Gamed by applying when metrics are fresh, then ghosting real work. | **Re-check at approve-time.** If below threshold when lister tries to approve, approval API returns 409 `INELIGIBLE_NOW` and lister can decide to override (one-click override allowed, logged). **No re-check at submission-time** — once approved, the creator's eligibility is locked. |
| 13 | Multiple videos per submission — one URL or batched? | A campaign may require a set of videos. | **Submission has 1..N video URLs.** Lister's post-conditions include `min_videos` (default 1). Submission is reviewed as a unit. |
| 14 | Resubmission after rejection? | Creators may need to fix and retry. | Add `revision_requested` state. Flow: `pending → revision_requested → pending` (up to 2 revisions; `revision_count` on submission row). After that, reject is final. |
| 15 | Creator reapplies after rejection/cancellation? | See #8. | After `rejected` or `cancelled_listing_edit`: allowed if listing still active. After `withdrawn`: allowed immediately. After lister hits "block creator" (post-MVP): never. |
| 16 | Disputes (creator says submitted, lister says no) | Hard case; immutable log needed. | Write all status transitions to an append-only `events` table. Expose support email in-app. No in-app arbitration in MVP. |
| 17 | Expiration of pending applications/submissions | Listers can ghost. | `applications`: auto-expire 14 days after creation if still `pending`. `submissions`: auto-expire 7 days after creation if still `pending`. Both via `pg_cron`. Configurable on listing in v2. |

### 1.4 Money

| # | Gap | Why it matters | Decision |
|---|---|---|---|
| 18 | Does MVP actually handle payment? | PRD says "price" and "bounty" — ambiguous. | **No real money in MVP.** `price` is display-only ("advertised bounty"). Payment is off-platform (lister contacts creator post-approval). Add a `payment_status` column to submissions for future. Flag this as v2. |

### 1.5 Notifications

| # | Gap | Why it matters | Decision |
|---|---|---|---|
| 19 | Which events notify? Which channels? | PRD says "notified" with no spec. | **Events:** application approved/rejected/cancelled; submission approved/rejected/revision_requested; listing version changed (for applicants); metrics refresh failed. **Channels MVP:** in-app notification row + Expo Push (via Edge Function). Email out of scope. |
| 20 | Realtime for lister seeing new submission? | UX question. | **Supabase Realtime subscriptions** on `submissions` filtered by `listing.lister_id`. Fallback polling on app resume. |

### 1.6 Metrics / External APIs

| # | Gap | Why it matters | Decision |
|---|---|---|---|
| 21 | Metric refresh rate limits | Creator could DDOS our API budget. | **Server-side throttle:** manual refresh once per 6 hours per (user, platform). Background refresh daily via `pg_cron`. |
| 22 | Handling API failures | Expired tokens, rate limit, account deleted. | `metric_snapshots.status = fresh | stale | refreshing | failed`. Show "metrics outdated" badge if `fetched_at < now() - 7 days`. After 3 failed refreshes, mark link `failed_fetch` and prompt re-OAuth. |
| 23 | Deleted / private / removed video at submission time | Creator submits, then deletes video. | Validate URL via oEmbed at submission time (reject if 404). Re-validate via Edge Function on a nightly sweep for `approved` submissions — flag but don't change state. |
| 24 | Post-submission performance verification | Lister wants to reward high-performing videos (v2 feature). | Out of MVP scope, but store `video_external_id` so we can later call `GET /v2/video/query/` (TikTok) or `GET /<IG_MEDIA_ID>?fields=insights` (IG) to fetch view/like counts. |

### 1.7 Security / Abuse

| # | Gap | Why | Decision |
|---|---|---|---|
| 25 | Lister posts campaign that's spam/illegal | Marketplace trust. | Manual admin review post-MVP. MVP: all listings live immediately; add `report_listing` button → admin table. |
| 26 | Creator submits same video to 10 listings | Double-dipping. | Enforce `UNIQUE(submission.video_url)` globally? No — same video might legitimately match two campaigns. Instead: flag and surface to lister. No hard block MVP. |

## 2. State Machines

Notation: `transition [actor: user|lister|system|time]`.

### 2.1 Listing

```
(init) ──create[lister]──> draft
draft ──publish[lister]──> active
active ──pause[lister]──> paused
paused ──resume[lister]──> active
active ──close[lister]──> closed
active ──end_date_passed[time]──> expired
active ──max_submissions_reached[system]──> closed
closed ──archive[lister]──> archived
expired ──archive[lister|time:30d]──> archived
paused ──archive[lister]──> archived

Side-effect on any edit that mutates price/pre-cond/post-cond/samples/max_submissions while active:
  version++ AND trigger cascade (see §2.3)
```

Terminal states: `archived`.

### 2.2 Application

```
(init) ──submit[creator, gated by eligibility]──> pending
pending ──approve[lister]──> approved
pending ──reject[lister]──> rejected
pending ──withdraw[creator]──> withdrawn
pending ──listing_version_bump[system]──> cancelled_listing_edit
pending ──14d_no_action[time]──> expired
pending ──listing_closed_or_archived[system]──> cancelled_listing_closed

approved ──submission_created[system]──> (stays approved; submission owns downstream state)
approved ──listing_archived[system]──> cancelled_listing_closed  // rare edge case
```

Terminal: `approved` (once submission exists), `rejected`, `withdrawn`, `cancelled_*`, `expired`.

### 2.3 Submission

```
(init, requires application.status=approved) ──create[creator]──> pending
pending ──approve[lister]──> approved
pending ──reject[lister]──> rejected
pending ──request_revision[lister]──> revision_requested
revision_requested ──resubmit[creator]──> pending   // max 2 revisions
pending ──7d_no_action[time]──> expired
```

Terminal: `approved`, `rejected`, `expired`.

### 2.4 Social Link

```
(init) ──oauth_complete[creator]──> linked
linked ──refresh_success[system|creator]──> linked
linked ──refresh_fail x3[system]──> failed_fetch
failed_fetch ──reauth[creator]──> linked
linked ──unlink[creator]──> unlinked
```

### 2.5 Metric Snapshot

```
(init, on link) ──fetch_queued[system]──> refreshing
refreshing ──success[system]──> fresh
refreshing ──fail[system]──> failed
fresh ──age > 24h[time]──> stale
stale ──fetch_queued[creator_manual | system_daily]──> refreshing
failed ──retry[system|creator]──> refreshing
```

Rule: eligibility filtering uses `fresh` OR `stale` (not `failed`). Display warning if `stale`.

## 3. Best-Practice Research (context7-verified)

### 3a. TikTok — Creator Metrics

**Source:** `/websites/developers_tiktok_doc` — `https://developers.tiktok.com/doc/tiktok-api-v2-get-user-info`, `https://developers.tiktok.com/doc/tiktok-api-scopes`.

- **Endpoint:** `GET https://open.tiktokapis.com/v2/user/info/` with `fields=follower_count,following_count,likes_count,video_count,username,is_verified,display_name`.
- **Required scopes:**
  - `user.info.basic` (open_id, display_name, avatar) — auto-granted with Login Kit.
  - `user.info.profile` (bio, verified, deep link) — optional.
  - `user.info.stats` — **REQUIRED for follower_count, likes_count, video_count**.
  - `video.list` — required to page `POST /v2/video/list/` and derive avg views from last N videos.
- **Auth flow:** OAuth 2.0 via Login Kit. We get `access_token` (short-lived) + `refresh_token` (typically 365 days). Must refresh before expiry.
- **Video stats per video:** `view_count`, `like_count`, `comment_count`, `share_count` available on Video Object (`/v2/video/list/` with `fields=id,view_count,like_count,...`). This is how we compute `avg_views_last_n`.
- **Rate limits:** TikTok documents per-app QPS on each endpoint (varies; typically 10 QPS per app for `/v2/user/info/`). We MUST not fan-out concurrent refreshes; use a queue.
- **Research API** (`/research/user/info/`) surfaces `follower_count` publicly but is gated behind a researcher approval process — **not available to us in MVP**.
- **oEmbed** (`https://www.tiktok.com/oembed?url=...`) is public and unauthenticated but returns ONLY embed HTML + thumbnail + author — **no follower count, no view count**. Useful for rendering submitted videos, not for eligibility.

**Conclusion:** No path to real follower/view counts without TikTok OAuth on the creator side. "No auth, just handles" from PRD is infeasible for pre-conditions.

### 3b. Instagram — Creator Metrics

**Source:** `/websites/developers_facebook_instagram-platform` — `https://developers.facebook.com/docs/instagram-platform/instagram-graph-api/reference/ig-user/business_discovery`, `https://developers.facebook.com/docs/instagram-platform/insights`.

- **`business_discovery`** is the only Graph API path that returns follower_count/media_count for *another* account by username. Important limitations:
  - The *querying* account must be an Instagram Business or Creator account.
  - The *target* account must also be Business or Creator (personal accounts are invisible to this API).
  - Requires permissions `instagram_basic`, `instagram_manage_insights`, `pages_read_engagement` on the querying user's access token.
  - Public-looking but NOT actually unauthenticated.
- **Direct account (own metrics):** `GET /<IG_USER_ID>?fields=followers_count,media_count,username,biography` — Business/Creator accounts only.
- **Media insights:** `GET /<IG_MEDIA_ID>?fields=insights.metric(views,likes,comments,shares,...)`. Note per-changelog: `impressions`, `plays`, and `clips_replays_count` deprecated April 2025; use `views` going forward.
- **Instagram Basic Display API:** deprecated by Meta for new apps; do not use.
- **oEmbed** (`/instagram_oembed`) — public thumbnail + author only; requires app access token; no metrics.

**Conclusion:** For IG we need the creator to log in via Instagram OAuth **AND** they must have a Business/Creator account (switch is free and in-app on IG). Personal IG accounts = we cannot fetch metrics, period.

### 3c. Displaying a Submitted TikTok Video

- Use oEmbed (`GET https://www.tiktok.com/oembed?url=<share_url>`) → returns `html`, `thumbnail_url`, `author_name`, `title`. Renderable via WebView.
- Alternative: construct `embed_link = https://www.tiktok.com/embed/v2/<video_id>` and load in WebView directly.
- **Performance verification:** requires the creator's OAuth token AND `video.list` scope, then `POST /v2/video/list/` to find the video and read `view_count`. Out of MVP scope.

### 3d. Displaying a Submitted Instagram Reel

- `GET /instagram_oembed?url=<reel_url>&access_token=<app_token>` → thumbnail + attribution. No embed HTML as full-featured as TikTok.
- Recommended: parse the reel shortcode from URL (`/reel/{shortcode}/` or `/p/{shortcode}/`), display thumbnail from oEmbed, and deep-link to Instagram app on tap.
- **Performance verification:** the creator must have linked their IG Business account AND we need the `IG_MEDIA_ID`. Resolve via `ig_hashtag_search` / the creator's own `/me/media` — out of MVP scope.

### 3e. MVP Feasibility with "No Auth, No Verification"

**I am overriding the PRD on this point.** The constraint as written is incompatible with the core value proposition.

**Recommended MVP path:**

1. **No app-level auth (email/password).** Correct, keep this.
2. **REQUIRE TikTok OAuth and/or Instagram OAuth at creator onboarding.** At least one. Treat the OAuth account as the identity. No handle is typed by hand.
3. **Self-reported handles rejected entirely.** Metrics come from OAuth calls only.
4. **Listers sign up with username + email** (no social OAuth required — they aren't being filtered on metrics).
5. For IG specifically, require Business/Creator conversion in onboarding flow (show a 3-step guide). If creator only has TikTok, they see only TikTok-gated listings.

Rationale: a marketplace built on unverified follower counts is a fraud vector on day one. Listers pay (or will pay) for access to creators with real audiences. Fake metrics = churn. OAuth is free and a one-time friction.

**Scope cut alternative** (if the above is rejected): launch as "discovery only" — no pre-conditions, creators browse all listings, apply manually, lister eyeballs the handle. Don't ship pre-conditions at all in v1. (See §8.)

## 4. Supabase Database Schema

Applied rules from `supabase-postgres-best-practices`: RLS enabled on all public tables; FKs indexed; partial indexes for hot filters; `security_invoker` on views; `security definer` functions in a private schema; composite indexes ordered by selectivity.

### 4.1 Tables Overview

| Table | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `users` | Unified account row; role-gated. |
| `creator_profiles` | 1:1 extension for creator-specific fields. |
| `lister_profiles` | 1:1 extension for lister-specific fields. |
| `social_links` | OAuth-linked TikTok/IG accounts per user. |
| `metric_snapshots` | Historized metric data per social_link. Latest-flagged. |
| `listings` | Core campaign row. Mutable fields live here. |
| `listing_versions` | Immutable snapshots of versioned fields. Applications FK to a version. |
| `listing_conditions` | Pre + post conditions, structured rows tied to a listing_version. |
| `sample_videos` | Sample video URLs per listing_version. |
| `applications` | Creator → listing_version. |
| `submissions` | Final deliverables linked to an approved application. |
| `submission_videos` | N video URLs per submission. |
| `notifications` | In-app notification inbox. |
| `push_tokens` | Expo push tokens per user. |
| `events` | Append-only audit log. |
| `oauth_states` | CSRF/state tokens for OAuth callbacks (short-lived). |

### 4.2 Enums

```sql
create type user_role as enum ('creator', 'lister');
create type platform as enum ('tiktok', 'instagram');
create type listing_status as enum ('draft', 'active', 'paused', 'closed', 'expired', 'archived');
create type application_status as enum (
  'pending', 'approved', 'rejected', 'withdrawn',
  'cancelled_listing_edit', 'cancelled_listing_closed', 'expired'
);
create type submission_status as enum (
  'pending', 'approved', 'rejected', 'revision_requested', 'expired'
);
create type social_link_status as enum ('linked', 'unlinked', 'failed_fetch');
create type metric_status as enum ('fresh', 'stale', 'refreshing', 'failed');
create type condition_kind as enum ('pre', 'post');
create type condition_metric as enum (
  'min_followers', 'min_avg_views_last_n', 'min_total_likes',
  'min_videos_posted', 'verified_only',
  'post_min_video_duration_sec', 'post_max_video_duration_sec',
  'post_min_video_count', 'post_must_mention', 'post_family_friendly',
  'post_must_tag_account'
);
create type notification_kind as enum (
  'application_approved', 'application_rejected', 'application_cancelled',
  'submission_received', 'submission_approved', 'submission_rejected',
  'submission_revision_requested', 'listing_version_changed',
  'metrics_refresh_failed'
);
```

### 4.3 Denormalization Notes

- `creator_profiles.latest_follower_count_tiktok`, `latest_follower_count_instagram`, `latest_avg_views_tiktok`, `latest_avg_views_instagram`, `metrics_fetched_at` — denormalized from `metric_snapshots` for **O(1) eligibility filtering** across thousands of listings. Kept in sync by trigger.
- `listings.current_version_id` — FK to `listing_versions`, denormalized to avoid an extra join on every feed query.
- `listings.active_pending_applications_count`, `approved_submissions_count` — counters, trigger-maintained.
- `applications.listing_version_id` — denormalized so cancellation logic becomes a single UPDATE when version changes.

### 4.4 RLS Model (without Supabase Auth)

Since we have no `auth.uid()`, we'll use a custom JWT issued by our `auth_signin` edge function (signed with project JWT secret, same verification path Supabase uses). JWT payload includes:
```json
{ "sub": "<user_id>", "role": "creator|lister", "session_id": "<uuid>" }
```
RLS policies read `auth.jwt() ->> 'sub'` as the user id. **`sub` is stored in `app_metadata`-equivalent claims we control (signed by us), not user-editable — safe for authz decisions.** We explicitly do NOT use `user_metadata` for authorization (per Supabase security checklist).

### 4.5 Indexes — Key Decisions

- Feed query: `listings WHERE status='active' ORDER BY created_at DESC` — partial index on `(status, created_at DESC) WHERE status='active'`.
- Eligibility pre-filter: denormalized creator metrics compared client-side after server returns filterable listings; server supplies `min_required_followers` column per listing row (computed — see §6) indexed as `btree`.
- Application uniqueness: `UNIQUE (listing_id, creator_id) WHERE status='pending' OR status='approved'` — partial unique.
- Notifications inbox: `(user_id, read_at NULLS FIRST, created_at DESC)`.

### 4.6 Triggers / Functions

All in private schema `app_private` (security-definer path).

| Function | Trigger |
|---|---|
| `app_private.handle_listing_mutation()` | BEFORE UPDATE on `listings` — if any versioned field changed, INSERT into `listing_versions`, set `current_version_id`, and queue application cascade. |
| `app_private.cascade_cancel_pending_apps(listing_id, reason)` | Called by trigger + edge functions. Updates applications + emits notifications. |
| `app_private.denorm_creator_metrics()` | AFTER INSERT on `metric_snapshots` where `is_latest=true` — pushes to `creator_profiles`. |
| `app_private.expire_stale()` | Called by `pg_cron` daily. Expires pending applications (14d) + pending submissions (7d) + past-end_date listings. |
| `app_private.auto_close_on_max_submissions()` | AFTER UPDATE on `submissions` when status → `approved` — if count >= max_submissions, close listing. |
| `app_private.emit_event()` | Helper called by every state-changing function; inserts into `events`. |
