146	create index listings_lister_idx on public.listings (lister_id, status);
147	
148	create table public.listing_versions (
149	  id                uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
150	  listing_id        uuid not null references public.listings(id) on delete cascade,
151	  version_number    integer not null,
152	  price_cents       integer not null,
153	  currency          text not null,
154	  max_submissions   integer,
155	  snapshot          jsonb not null,         -- full snapshot for audit
156	  created_at        timestamptz not null default now(),
157	  unique (listing_id, version_number)
158	);
159	alter table public.listings
160	  add constraint listings_current_version_fk
161	  foreign key (current_version_id) references public.listing_versions(id);
162	
163	-- =========================================================
164	-- 8. listing_conditions (pre + post, data-driven)
165	-- =========================================================
166	create table public.listing_conditions (
167	  id                   uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
168	  listing_version_id   uuid not null references public.listing_versions(id) on delete cascade,
169	  kind                 condition_kind not null,
170	  metric               condition_metric not null,
171	  platform             platform,                 -- null if not platform-specific
172	  operator             text not null default 'gte' check (operator in ('gte','lte','eq','contains','bool')),
173	  numeric_threshold    numeric,
174	  text_threshold       text,
175	  bool_threshold       boolean,
176	  created_at           timestamptz not null default now()
177	);
178	create index on public.listing_conditions (listing_version_id, kind);
179	
180	-- =========================================================
181	-- 9. sample_videos
182	-- =========================================================
183	create table public.sample_videos (
184	  id                   uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
185	  listing_version_id   uuid not null references public.listing_versions(id) on delete cascade,
186	  platform             platform not null,
187	  url                  text not null,
188	  caption              text,
189	  sort_order           smallint not null default 0
190	);
191	create index on public.sample_videos (listing_version_id);
192	
193	-- =========================================================
194	-- 10. applications
195	-- =========================================================
196	create table public.applications (
197	  id                   uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
198	  listing_id           uuid not null references public.listings(id) on delete restrict,
199	  listing_version_id   uuid not null references public.listing_versions(id) on delete restrict,
200	  creator_id           uuid not null references public.users(id) on delete restrict,
201	  status               application_status not null default 'pending',
202	  cover_note           text,
203	  created_at           timestamptz not null default now(),
204	  updated_at           timestamptz not null default now(),
205	  decided_at           timestamptz,
206	  decision_note        text
207	);
208	create index on public.applications (listing_id, status);
209	create index on public.applications (creator_id, status, created_at desc);
210	create unique index applications_open_uniq
211	  on public.applications (listing_id, creator_id)
212	  where status in ('pending','approved');
213	
214	-- =========================================================
215	-- 11. submissions
216	-- =========================================================
217	create table public.submissions (
218	  id                uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
219	  application_id    uuid not null references public.applications(id) on delete restrict,
220	  status            submission_status not null default 'pending',
221	  cover_note        text,
222	  revision_count    smallint not null default 0,
223	  created_at        timestamptz not null default now(),
224	  updated_at        timestamptz not null default now(),
225	  decided_at        timestamptz,
226	  decision_note     text
227	);
228	create unique index submissions_open_uniq
229	  on public.submissions (application_id)
230	  where status in ('pending','revision_requested','approved');
231	create index on public.submissions (status, created_at desc);
232	
233	create table public.submission_videos (
234	  id              uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
235	  submission_id   uuid not null references public.submissions(id) on delete cascade,
236	  platform        platform not null,
237	  url             text not null,
238	  external_id     text,           -- parsed from URL
239	  oembed_cached   jsonb,
240	  last_validated_at timestamptz,
241	  sort_order      smallint not null default 0
242	);
243	create index on public.submission_videos (submission_id);
244	
245	-- =========================================================
246	-- 12. notifications + push_tokens
247	-- =========================================================
248	create table public.notifications (
249	  id          uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
250	  user_id     uuid not null references public.users(id) on delete cascade,
251	  kind        notification_kind not null,
252	  payload     jsonb not null,
253	  read_at     timestamptz,
254	  created_at  timestamptz not null default now()
255	);
256	create index notifications_inbox_idx
257	  on public.notifications (user_id, created_at desc);
258	create index notifications_unread_idx
259	  on public.notifications (user_id) where read_at is null;
260	
261	create table public.push_tokens (
262	  id          uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
263	  user_id     uuid not null references public.users(id) on delete cascade,
264	  expo_token  text not null unique,
265	  platform    text not null check (platform in ('ios','android')),
266	  created_at  timestamptz not null default now(),
267	  last_seen_at timestamptz not null default now()
268	);
269	
270	-- =========================================================
271	-- 13. events (append-only audit log)
272	-- =========================================================
273	create table public.events (
274	  id          bigserial primary key,
275	  actor_id    uuid,
276	  entity      text not null,    -- 'listing'|'application'|'submission'|...
277	  entity_id   uuid not null,
278	  action      text not null,
279	  old_state   jsonb,
280	  new_state   jsonb,
281	  created_at  timestamptz not null default now()
282	);
283	create index on public.events (entity, entity_id, created_at desc);
284	
285	-- =========================================================
286	-- 14. oauth_states (short-lived CSRF tokens)
287	-- =========================================================
288	create table public.oauth_states (
289	  state       text primary key,
290	  platform    platform not null,
291	  user_id     uuid references public.users(id) on delete cascade, -- nullable for signup
292	  code_verifier text,
293	  created_at  timestamptz not null default now(),
294	  expires_at  timestamptz not null default (now() + interval '10 minutes')
295	);
296	create index on public.oauth_states (expires_at);
297	
298	-- =========================================================
299	-- 15. RLS — enable on all public tables
300	-- =========================================================
301	alter table public.users enable row level security;
302	alter table public.creator_profiles enable row level security;
303	alter table public.lister_profiles enable row level security;
304	alter table public.social_links enable row level security;
305	alter table public.metric_snapshots enable row level security;
306	alter table public.listings enable row level security;
307	alter table public.listing_versions enable row level security;
308	alter table public.listing_conditions enable row level security;
309	alter table public.sample_videos enable row level security;
310	alter table public.applications enable row level security;
311	alter table public.submissions enable row level security;
312	alter table public.submission_videos enable row level security;
313	alter table public.notifications enable row level security;
314	alter table public.push_tokens enable row level security;
315	alter table public.events enable row level security;
316	alter table public.oauth_states enable row level security;
317	
318	-- Helper: returns current user id from custom JWT sub claim
319	create or replace function public.current_user_id() returns uuid
320	language sql stable as $$
321	  select nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'sub', '')::uuid
322	$$;
323	
324	create or replace function public.current_user_role() returns user_role
325	language sql stable as $$
326	  select (auth.jwt() ->> 'role')::user_role
327	$$;
328	
329	-- users: self-read, self-update limited
330	create policy users_self_read on public.users
331	  for select using (id = public.current_user_id());
332	create policy users_self_update on public.users
333	  for update using (id = public.current_user_id())
334	  with check (id = public.current_user_id());
335	
336	-- creator_profiles: owner full, public read of display fields via view
337	create policy cp_self_all on public.creator_profiles
338	  for all using (user_id = public.current_user_id())
339	  with check (user_id = public.current_user_id());
340	-- listers need to see creator profiles of their applicants — via a security_invoker view
341	create view public.v_creator_public with (security_invoker = true) as
342	  select user_id, display_name, country,
343	         tiktok_follower_count, instagram_follower_count,
344	         tiktok_avg_views_last_10, instagram_avg_views_last_10,
345	         metrics_fetched_at
346	  from public.creator_profiles;
347	
348	create policy cp_lister_read_applicants on public.creator_profiles
349	  for select using (
350	    public.current_user_role() = 'lister'
351	    and exists (
352	      select 1 from public.applications a
353	      join public.listings l on l.id = a.listing_id
354	      where a.creator_id = creator_profiles.user_id
355	        and l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()
356	    )
357	  );
358	
359	-- listings: anyone authenticated reads active/paused/closed; owner reads all; owner writes
360	create policy listings_public_read on public.listings
361	  for select using (status in ('active','paused','closed','expired'));
362	create policy listings_owner_all on public.listings
363	  for all using (lister_id = public.current_user_id())
364	  with check (lister_id = public.current_user_id());
365	
366	create policy lv_public_read on public.listing_versions
367	  for select using (
368	    exists (select 1 from public.listings l
369	            where l.id = listing_versions.listing_id
370	              and (l.status in ('active','paused','closed','expired')
371	                   or l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()))
372	  );
373	
374	create policy lc_public_read on public.listing_conditions
375	  for select using (
376	    exists (select 1 from public.listing_versions lv
377	            join public.listings l on l.id = lv.listing_id
378	            where lv.id = listing_conditions.listing_version_id
379	              and (l.status in ('active','paused','closed','expired')
380	                   or l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()))
381	  );
382	
383	create policy sv_public_read on public.sample_videos
384	  for select using (
385	    exists (select 1 from public.listing_versions lv
386	            join public.listings l on l.id = lv.listing_id
387	            where lv.id = sample_videos.listing_version_id
388	              and (l.status in ('active','paused','closed','expired')
389	                   or l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()))
390	  );
391	
392	-- applications: creator sees own; lister sees their listing's
393	create policy apps_creator_read on public.applications
394	  for select using (creator_id = public.current_user_id());
395	create policy apps_lister_read on public.applications
396	  for select using (
397	    exists (select 1 from public.listings l
398	            where l.id = applications.listing_id
399	              and l.lister_id = public.current_user_id())
400	  );
401	-- writes go through RPCs (no direct insert/update from client)
402	
403	-- submissions: creator of the parent app + lister of parent listing
404	create policy subs_creator_read on public.submissions
405	  for select using (
406	    exists (select 1 from public.applications a
407	            where a.id = submissions.application_id
408	              and a.creator_id = public.current_user_id())
409	  );
410	create policy subs_lister_read on public.submissions
411	  for select using (
412	    exists (select 1 from public.applications a
413	            join public.listings l on l.id = a.listing_id
414	            where a.id = submissions.application_id
415	              and l.lister_id = public.current_user_id())
416	  );
417	
418	create policy sv_rw on public.submission_videos
419	  for select using (
420	    exists (select 1 from public.submissions s
421	            where s.id = submission_videos.submission_id
422	            and (exists (select 1 from public.applications a
423	                         where a.id = s.application_id and a.creator_id = public.current_user_id())
424	                 or exists (select 1 from public.applications a
425	                            join public.listings l on l.id = a.listing_id
426	                            where a.id = s.application_id and l.lister_id = public.current_user_id())))
427	  );
428	
429	-- social_links + metric_snapshots: owner only
430	create policy sl_owner on public.social_links
431	  for all using (user_id = public.current_user_id())
432	  with check (user_id = public.current_user_id());
433	create policy ms_owner on public.metric_snapshots
434	  for select using (
435	    exists (select 1 from public.social_links sl
436	            where sl.id = metric_snapshots.social_link_id
437	              and sl.user_id = public.current_user_id())
438	  );
439	
440	-- notifications: owner
441	create policy notif_owner on public.notifications
442	  for all using (user_id = public.current_user_id())
443	  with check (user_id = public.current_user_id());
444	create policy push_owner on public.push_tokens
445	  for all using (user_id = public.current_user_id())
446	  with check (user_id = public.current_user_id());
447	
448	-- events: no direct reads from clients (server/admin only via service_role)
449	create policy events_none on public.events for select using (false);
450	-- oauth_states: none from clients
451	create policy os_none on public.oauth_states for all using (false) with check (false);
452	
453	-- =========================================================
454	-- 16. Triggers / functions
455	-- =========================================================
456	create or replace function app_private.bump_listing_version()
457	returns trigger language plpgsql security definer as $$
458	declare
459	  versioned_changed boolean;
460	  new_version_id uuid;
461	begin
462	  versioned_changed := (
463	    old.price_cents is distinct from new.price_cents
464	    or old.currency is distinct from new.currency
465	    or old.max_submissions is distinct from new.max_submissions
466	  );
467	  if versioned_changed and new.status = 'active' then
468	    new.version_number := old.version_number + 1;
469	    insert into public.listing_versions(listing_id, version_number, price_cents, currency, max_submissions, snapshot)
470	      values (new.id, new.version_number, new.price_cents, new.currency, new.max_submissions,
471	              to_jsonb(new))
472	      returning id into new_version_id;
473	    new.current_version_id := new_version_id;
474	
475	    update public.applications
476	      set status = 'cancelled_listing_edit', updated_at = now()
477	      where listing_id = new.id and status = 'pending';
478	
479	    insert into public.notifications(user_id, kind, payload)
480	      select creator_id, 'listing_version_changed',
481	             jsonb_build_object('listing_id', new.id, 'new_version', new.version_number)
482	      from public.applications
483	      where listing_id = new.id and status = 'cancelled_listing_edit';
484	  end if;
485	  return new;
486	end $$;
487	
488	create trigger trg_bump_listing_version
489	  before update on public.listings
490	  for each row execute function app_private.bump_listing_version();
491	
492	create or replace function app_private.denorm_metrics()
493	returns trigger language plpgsql security definer as $$
494	declare
495	  sl public.social_links%rowtype;
496	begin
497	  if new.is_latest then
498	    -- mark older snapshots not latest
499	    update public.metric_snapshots
500	      set is_latest = false
501	      where social_link_id = new.social_link_id and id <> new.id and is_latest;
502	
503	    select * into sl from public.social_links where id = new.social_link_id;
504	    if sl.platform = 'tiktok' then
505	      update public.creator_profiles
506	        set tiktok_follower_count = new.follower_count,
507	            tiktok_avg_views_last_10 = new.avg_views_last_10,
508	            tiktok_total_likes = new.total_likes,
509	            tiktok_video_count = new.video_count,
510	            tiktok_is_verified = new.is_verified,
511	            metrics_fetched_at = new.fetched_at,
512	            updated_at = now()
513	      where user_id = sl.user_id;
514	    elsif sl.platform = 'instagram' then
515

file.content:
'rejected', 'withdrawn',
  'cancelled_listing_edit', 'cancelled_listing_closed', 'expired'
);
create type submission_status as enum (
  'pending', 'approved', 'rejected', 'revision_requested', 'expired'
);
create type social_link_status as enum ('linked', 'unlinked', 'failed_fetch');
create type metric_status as enum ('fresh', 'stale', 'refreshing', 'failed');
create type condition_kind as enum ('pre', 'post');
create type condition_metric as enum (
  'min_followers', 'min_avg_views_last_n', 'min_total_likes',
  'min_videos_posted', 'verified_only',
  'post_min_video_duration_sec', 'post_max_video_duration_sec',
  'post_min_video_count', 'post_must_mention', 'post_family_friendly',
  'post_must_tag_account'
);
create type notification_kind as enum (
  'application_approved', 'application_rejected', 'application_cancelled',
  'submission_received', 'submission_approved', 'submission_rejected',
  'submission_revision_requested', 'listing_version_changed',
  'metrics_refresh_failed'
);

-- =========================================================
-- 2. users
-- =========================================================
create table public.users (
  id             uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  role           user_role not null,
  username       citext not null unique,   -- display handle, unique case-insensitively
  email          citext unique,            -- nullable for creators (OAuth-only)
  created_at     timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at     timestamptz not null default now(),
  deleted_at     timestamptz
);
create index on public.users (role) where deleted_at is null;

-- =========================================================
-- 3. creator_profiles (denormalized for fast filtering)
-- =========================================================
create table public.creator_profiles (
  user_id                            uuid primary key references public.users(id) on delete cascade,
  display_name                       text,
  bio                                text,
  country                            text,
  -- denormalized latest metrics (nullable until first fetch)
  tiktok_follower_count              integer,
  tiktok_avg_views_last_10           integer,
  tiktok_total_likes                 bigint,
  tiktok_video_count                 integer,
  tiktok_is_verified                 boolean,
  instagram_follower_count           integer,
  instagram_avg_views_last_10        integer,
  instagram_media_count              integer,
  metrics_fetched_at                 timestamptz,
  created_at                         timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at                         timestamptz not null default now()
);
-- indexes so eligibility pre-filter on lister side is cheap
create index on public.creator_profiles (tiktok_follower_count);
create index on public.creator_profiles (instagram_follower_count);

-- =========================================================
-- 4. lister_profiles
-- =========================================================
create table public.lister_profiles (
  user_id      uuid primary key references public.users(id) on delete cascade,
  org_name     text not null,
  website_url  text,
  logo_path    text,          -- Supabase Storage path
  description  text,
  created_at   timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at   timestamptz not null default now()
);

-- =========================================================
-- 5. social_links
-- =========================================================
create table public.social_links (
  id                    uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id               uuid not null references public.users(id) on delete cascade,
  platform              platform not null,
  platform_user_id      text not null,        -- TikTok open_id / IG user id
  handle                text not null,        -- denormalized handle at link time
  status                social_link_status not null default 'linked',
  access_token_enc      text,                 -- encrypted via pgsodium in v2; TEXT for MVP
  refresh_token_enc     text,
  token_expires_at      timestamptz,
  scopes                text[],
  last_refresh_at       timestamptz,
  fail_count            smallint not null default 0,
  created_at            timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at            timestamptz not null default now(),
  unique (platform, platform_user_id)
);
create unique index social_links_user_platform_uniq
  on public.social_links (user_id, platform) where status <> 'unlinked';

-- =========================================================
-- 6. metric_snapshots (historized)
-- =========================================================
create table public.metric_snapshots (
  id                uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  social_link_id    uuid not null references public.social_links(id) on delete cascade,
  status            metric_status not null,
  follower_count    integer,
  following_count   integer,
  total_likes       bigint,
  video_count       integer,
  avg_views_last_10 integer,
  is_verified       boolean,
  raw_payload       jsonb,                    -- raw API response for forensics
  fetched_at        timestamptz not null default now(),
  is_latest         boolean not null default true,
  error_message     text
);
create index on public.metric_snapshots (social_link_id, fetched_at desc);
create unique index metric_snapshots_latest
  on public.metric_snapshots (social_link_id) where is_latest;

-- =========================================================
-- 7. listings + versioning
-- =========================================================
create table public.listings (
  id                               uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  lister_id                        uuid not null references public.users(id) on delete restrict,
  status                           listing_status not null default 'draft',
  title                            text not null,
  description                      text,
  -- versioned fields live ALSO on listing_versions for historical integrity
  price_cents                      integer not null check (price_cents >= 0),
  currency                         text not null default 'USD',
  max_submissions                  integer,                 -- nullable = unlimited
  approved_submissions_count       integer not null default 0,
  active_pending_applications_count integer not null default 0,
  end_date                         timestamptz,
  current_version_id               uuid,                    -- FK set after row exists
  version_number                   integer not null default 1,
  created_at                       timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at                       timestamptz not null default now(),
  published_at                     timestamptz,
  closed_at                        timestamptz
);
create index listings_feed_idx
  on public.listings (status, created_at desc) where status = 'active';
create index listings_lister_idx on public.listings (lister_id, status);

create table public.listing_versions (
  id                uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  listing_id        uuid not null references public.listings(id) on delete cascade,
  version_number    integer not null,
  price_cents       integer not null,
  currency          text not null,
  max_submissions   integer,
  snapshot          jsonb not null,         -- full snapshot for audit
  created_at        timestamptz not null default now(),
  unique (listing_id, version_number)
);
alter table public.listings
  add constraint listings_current_version_fk
  foreign key (current_version_id) references public.listing_versions(id);

-- =========================================================
-- 8. listing_conditions (pre + post, data-driven)
-- =========================================================
create table public.listing_conditions (
  id                   uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  listing_version_id   uuid not null references public.listing_versions(id) on delete cascade,
  kind                 condition_kind not null,
  metric               condition_metric not null,
  platform             platform,                 -- null if not platform-specific
  operator             text not null default 'gte' check (operator in ('gte','lte','eq','contains','bool')),
  numeric_threshold    numeric,
  text_threshold       text,
  bool_threshold       boolean,
  created_at           timestamptz not null default now()
);
create index on public.listing_conditions (listing_version_id, kind);

-- =========================================================
-- 9. sample_videos
-- =========================================================
create table public.sample_videos (
  id                   uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  listing_version_id   uuid not null references public.listing_versions(id) on delete cascade,
  platform             platform not null,
  url                  text not null,
  caption              text,
  sort_order           smallint not null default 0
);
create index on public.sample_videos (listing_version_id);

-- =========================================================
-- 10. applications
-- =========================================================
create table public.applications (
  id                   uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  listing_id           uuid not null references public.listings(id) on delete restrict,
  listing_version_id   uuid not null references public.listing_versions(id) on delete restrict,
  creator_id           uuid not null references public.users(id) on delete restrict,
  status               application_status not null default 'pending',
  cover_note           text,
  created_at           timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at           timestamptz not null default now(),
  decided_at           timestamptz,
  decision_note        text
);
create index on public.applications (listing_id, status);
create index on public.applications (creator_id, status, created_at desc);
create unique index applications_open_uniq
  on public.applications (listing_id, creator_id)
  where status in ('pending','approved');

-- =========================================================
-- 11. submissions
-- =========================================================
create table public.submissions (
  id                uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  application_id    uuid not null references public.applications(id) on delete restrict,
  status            submission_status not null default 'pending',
  cover_note        text,
  revision_count    smallint not null default 0,
  created_at        timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at        timestamptz not null default now(),
  decided_at        timestamptz,
  decision_note     text
);
create unique index submissions_open_uniq
  on public.submissions (application_id)
  where status in ('pending','revision_requested','approved');
create index on public.submissions (status, created_at desc);

create table public.submission_videos (
  id              uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  submission_id   uuid not null references public.submissions(id) on delete cascade,
  platform        platform not null,
  url             text not null,
  external_id     text,           -- parsed from URL
  oembed_cached   jsonb,
  last_validated_at timestamptz,
  sort_order      smallint not null default 0
);
create index on public.submission_videos (submission_id);

-- =========================================================
-- 12. notifications + push_tokens
-- =========================================================
create table public.notifications (
  id          uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id     uuid not null references public.users(id) on delete cascade,
  kind        notification_kind not null,
  payload     jsonb not null,
  read_at     timestamptz,
  created_at  timestamptz not null default now()
);
create index notifications_inbox_idx
  on public.notifications (user_id, created_at desc);
create index notifications_unread_idx
  on public.notifications (user_id) where read_at is null;

create table public.push_tokens (
  id          uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id     uuid not null references public.users(id) on delete cascade,
  expo_token  text not null unique,
  platform    text not null check (platform in ('ios','android')),
  created_at  timestamptz not null default now(),
  last_seen_at timestamptz not null default now()
);

-- =========================================================
-- 13. events (append-only audit log)
-- =========================================================
create table public.events (
  id          bigserial primary key,
  actor_id    uuid,
  entity      text not null,    -- 'listing'|'application'|'submission'|...
  entity_id   uuid not null,
  action      text not null,
  old_state   jsonb,
  new_state   jsonb,
  created_at  timestamptz not null default now()
);
create index on public.events (entity, entity_id, created_at desc);

-- =========================================================
-- 14. oauth_states (short-lived CSRF tokens)
-- =========================================================
create table public.oauth_states (
  state       text primary key,
  platform    platform not null,
  user_id     uuid references public.users(id) on delete cascade, -- nullable for signup
  code_verifier text,
  created_at  timestamptz not null default now(),
  expires_at  timestamptz not null default (now() + interval '10 minutes')
);
create index on public.oauth_states (expires_at);

-- =========================================================
-- 15. RLS — enable on all public tables
-- =========================================================
alter table public.users enable row level security;
alter table public.creator_profiles enable row level security;
alter table public.lister_profiles enable row level security;
alter table public.social_links enable row level security;
alter table public.metric_snapshots enable row level security;
alter table public.listings enable row level security;
alter table public.listing_versions enable row level security;
alter table public.listing_conditions enable row level security;
alter table public.sample_videos enable row level security;
alter table public.applications enable row level security;
alter table public.submissions enable row level security;
alter table public.submission_videos enable row level security;
alter table public.notifications enable row level security;
alter table public.push_tokens enable row level security;
alter table public.events enable row level security;
alter table public.oauth_states enable row level security;

-- Helper: returns current user id from custom JWT sub claim
create or replace function public.current_user_id() returns uuid
language sql stable as $$
  select nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'sub', '')::uuid
$$;

create or replace function public.current_user_role() returns user_role
language sql stable as $$
  select (auth.jwt() ->> 'role')::user_role
$$;

-- users: self-read, self-update limited
create policy users_self_read on public.users
  for select using (id = public.current_user_id());
create policy users_self_update on public.users
  for update using (id = public.current_user_id())
  with check (id = public.current_user_id());

-- creator_profiles: owner full, public read of display fields via view
create policy cp_self_all on public.creator_profiles
  for all using (user_id = public.current_user_id())
  with check (user_id = public.current_user_id());
-- listers need to see creator profiles of their applicants — via a security_invoker view
create view public.v_creator_public with (security_invoker = true) as
  select user_id, display_name, country,
         tiktok_follower_count, instagram_follower_count,
         tiktok_avg_views_last_10, instagram_avg_views_last_10,
         metrics_fetched_at
  from public.creator_profiles;

create policy cp_lister_read_applicants on public.creator_profiles
  for select using (
    public.current_user_role() = 'lister'
    and exists (
      select 1 from public.applications a
      join public.listings l on l.id = a.listing_id
      where a.creator_id = creator_profiles.user_id
        and l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()
    )
  );

-- listings: anyone authenticated reads active/paused/closed; owner reads all; owner writes
create policy listings_public_read on public.listings
  for select using (status in ('active','paused','closed','expired'));
create policy listings_owner_all on public.listings
  for all using (lister_id = public.current_user_id())
  with check (lister_id = public.current_user_id());

create policy lv_public_read on public.listing_versions
  for select using (
    exists (select 1 from public.listings l
            where l.id = listing_versions.listing_id
              and (l.status in ('active','paused','closed','expired')
                   or l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()))
  );

create policy lc_public_read on public.listing_conditions
  for select using (
    exists (select 1 from public.listing_versions lv
            join public.listings l on l.id = lv.listing_id
            where lv.id = listing_conditions.listing_version_id
              and (l.status in ('active','paused','closed','expired')
                   or l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()))
  );

create policy sv_public_read on public.sample_videos
  for select using (
    exists (select 1 from public.listing_versions lv
            join public.listings l on l.id = lv.listing_id
            where lv.id = sample_videos.listing_version_id
              and (l.status in ('active','paused','closed','expired')
                   or l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()))
  );

-- applications: creator sees own; lister sees their listing's
create policy apps_creator_read on public.applications
  for select using (creator_id = public.current_user_id());
create policy apps_lister_read on public.applications
  for select using (
    exists (select 1 from public.listings l
            where l.id = applications.listing_id
              and l.lister_id = public.current_user_id())
  );
-- writes go through RPCs (no direct insert/update from client)

-- submissions: creator of the parent app + lister of parent listing
create policy subs_creator_read on public.submissions
  for select using (
    exists (select 1 from public.applications a
            where a.id = submissions.application_id
              and a.creator_id = public.current_user_id())
  );
create policy subs_lister_read on public.submissions
  for select using (
    exists (select 1 from public.applications a
            join public.listings l on l.id = a.listing_id
            where a.id = submissions.application_id
              and l.lister_id = public.current_user_id())
  );

create policy sv_rw on public.submission_videos
  for select using (
    exists (select 1 from public.submissions s
            where s.id = submission_videos.submission_id
            and (exists (select 1 from public.applications a
                         where a.id = s.application_id and a.creator_id = public.current_user_id())
                 or exists (select 1 from public.applications a
                            join public.listings l on l.id = a.listing_id
                            where a.id = s.application_id and l.lister_id = public.current_user_id())))
  );

-- social_links + metric_snapshots: owner only
create policy sl_owner on public.social_links
  for all using (user_id = public.current_user_id())
  with check (user_id = public.current_user_id());
create policy ms_owner on public.metric_snapshots
  for select using (
    exists (select 1 from public.social_links sl
            where sl.id = metric_snapshots.social_link_id
              and sl.user_id = public.current_user_id())
  );

-- notifications: owner
create policy notif_owner on public.notifications
  for all using (user_id = public.current_user_id())
  with check (user_id = public.current_user_id());
create policy push_owner on public.push_tokens
  for all using (user_id = public.current_user_id())
  with check (user_id = public.current_user_id());

-- events: no direct reads from clients (server/admin only via service_role)
create policy events_none on public.events for select using (false);
-- oauth_states: none from clients
create policy os_none on public.oauth_states for all using (false) with check (false);

-- 16. Triggers / functions

create or replace function app_private.bump_listing_version()
returns trigger language plpgsql security definer as $$
declare
  versioned_changed boolean;
  new_version_id uuid;
begin
  versioned_changed := (
    old.price_cents is distinct from new.price_cents
    or old.currency is distinct from new.currency
    or old.max_submissions is distinct from new.max_submissions
  );
  if versioned_changed and new.status = 'active' then
    new.version_number := old.version_number + 1;
    insert into public.listing_versions(listing_id, version_number, price_cents, currency, max_submissions, snapshot)
      values (new.id, new.version_number, new.price_cents, new.currency, new.max_submissions,
              to_jsonb(new))
      returning id into new_version_id;
    new.current_version_id := new_version_id;

    update public.applications
      set status = 'cancelled_listing_edit', updated_at = now()
      where listing_id = new.id and status = 'pending';

    insert into public.notifications(user_id, kind, payload)
      select creator_id, 'listing_version_changed',
             jsonb_build_object('listing_id', new.id, 'new_version', new.version_number)
      from public.applications
      where listing_id = new.id and status = 'cancelled_listing_edit';
  end if;
  return new;
end $$;

create trigger trg_bump_listing_version
  before update on public.listings
  for each row execute function app_private.bump_listing_version();

62	
63	## 5. API / Edge Function Spec Sheet
64	
65	**Transport:**
66	- **Direct table reads** via `postgrest` (Supabase Data API) for anything RLS-safe (feed browse, notifications inbox).
67	- **Edge Functions** for anything that (a) calls an external API, (b) performs multi-table writes atomically, (c) enforces server-side business logic, or (d) issues JWTs.
68	- **Realtime channels** on `applications`, `submissions`, `notifications`.
69	
70	Roles below refer to custom-JWT `role` claim.
71	
72	### 5.1 Auth & Identity
73	
74	#### `POST /auth/signup-creator`
75	Create creator account, redirect to TikTok or IG OAuth.
76	```ts
77	interface Req { role: 'creator'; initial_platform: 'tiktok' | 'instagram'; username?: string; }
78	interface Res { auth_url: string; state: string; } // 302-style payload
79	```
80	Side effects: inserts row into `oauth_states`. No user row yet.
81	Errors: `400 INVALID_PLATFORM`.
82	
83	#### `POST /auth/signup-lister`
84	```ts
85	interface Req { role: 'lister'; username: string; email: string; org_name: string; website_url?: string; }
86	interface Res { user_id: string; jwt: string; }
87	```
88	Side effects: `users` + `lister_profiles` insert. Issues custom JWT.
89	Errors: `409 USERNAME_TAKEN`, `409 EMAIL_TAKEN`.
90	
91	#### `GET /auth/oauth-callback/:platform`
92	Handle redirect from TikTok/IG. Exchange code → tokens → user_info → find-or-create user.
93	```ts
94	interface Query { code: string; state: string; }
95	interface Res { jwt: string; user_id: string; is_new: boolean; }
96	```
97	Side effects: `users`+`creator_profiles`+`social_links` upsert; enqueues initial metric fetch; emits event; deletes `oauth_states` row.
98	Errors: `400 INVALID_STATE`, `401 OAUTH_FAILED`, `409 ACCOUNT_LINKED_TO_OTHER_USER`.
99	
100	#### `POST /auth/signout`
101	```ts
102	interface Req { session_id: string; }
103	interface Res { ok: true; }
104	```
105	Since deleting a user does NOT invalidate JWTs (per Supabase security note), we keep a `session_id` claim and a server-side `revoked_sessions` set (redis/KV or `app_private.revoked_sessions` table) checked by an edge middleware.
106	
107	### 5.2 Social Accounts & Metrics
108	
109	#### `POST /social/link-start`
110	Start linking an additional platform to an existing user.
111	```ts
112	interface Req { platform: 'tiktok' | 'instagram'; }
113	interface Res { auth_url: string; state: string; }
114	```
115	Auth: creator.
116	
117	#### `POST /social/unlink`
118	```ts
119	interface Req { social_link_id: string; }
120	interface Res { ok: true; }
121	```
122	Auth: creator (owner). Errors: `409 LAST_LINK` if this is their only linked platform.
123	
124	#### `POST /metrics/refresh`
125	Manually trigger metric refresh. Rate-limited.
126	```ts
127	interface Req { social_link_id: string; }
128	interface Res { snapshot_id: string; status: 'queued' | 'already_fresh'; }
129	```
130	Side effects: writes `metric_snapshots` (status=refreshing) and kicks background task. Idempotency: second call within 6 h returns `already_fresh`.
131	Errors: `429 RATE_LIMIT` (includes `retry_after`), `401 REAUTH_REQUIRED`, `502 PROVIDER_ERROR`.
132	
133	#### Internal cron: `metrics-refresh-all`
134	Daily at 03:00 UTC. Picks links with `fetched_at < now() - 24h` in batches of 100, QPS-capped.
135	
136	### 5.3 Listings — Browse (Creator)
137	
138	#### `GET /rest/v1/listings?status=eq.active...` (PostgREST direct)
139	Feed query with filters. Client passes creator's denormalized metrics as query params for eligibility pre-filter, but the **authoritative** eligibility check runs in `/listings/:id/eligibility` before apply.
140	
141	#### `GET /listings/:id` (Edge Function)
142	```ts
143	interface Res {
144	  listing: Listing; // active current_version joined
145	  conditions: ListingCondition[];
146	  sample_videos: SampleVideo[];
147	  eligibility: {
148	    eligible: boolean;
149	    failed_conditions: Array<{ metric, required, actual }>;
150	    has_active_application: boolean;
151	  };
152	}
153	```
154	Auth: creator or lister (lister sees their own).
155	
156	### 5.4 Listings — Manage (Lister)
157	
158	#### `POST /listings` — create
159	```ts
160	interface Req {
161	  title: string; description?: string;
162	  price_cents: number; currency?: string;
163	  max_submissions?: number | null;
164	  end_date?: string | null;
165	  pre_conditions: ListingConditionInput[];
166	  post_conditions: ListingConditionInput[];
167	  sample_videos: { platform: Platform; url: string; caption?: string; }[];
168	  publish: boolean; // false => draft
169	}
170	interface Res { listing_id: string; version_id: string; }
171	```
172	Side effects: `listings` + `listing_versions` + `listing_conditions` + `sample_videos` in a txn.
173	
174	#### `PATCH /listings/:id` — update
175	```ts
176	interface Req { /* any subset of create */ }
177	interface Res { listing_id: string; version_id: string; cascaded_applications: number; }
178	```
179	Side effects: if versioned fields change, trigger cancels pending applications and sends notifications. Returns count of cascaded apps so UI can confirm.
180	
181	#### `POST /listings/:id/status`
182	```ts
183	interface Req { action: 'publish' | 'pause' | 'resume' | 'close' | 'archive'; }
184	interface Res { ok: true; new_status: ListingStatus; }
185	```
186	Auth: lister (owner). Server validates state transition.
187	
188	### 5.5 Applications
189	
190	#### `POST /applications`
191	```ts
192	interface Req { listing_id: string; cover_note?: string; }
193	interface Res { application_id: string; listing_version_id: string; }
194	```
195	Idempotency: server checks partial-unique index; on collision returns existing `application_id`.
196	Side effects: re-evaluates eligibility on server; creates row; notifies lister (`application_received`).
197	Errors: `403 INELIGIBLE`, `409 ALREADY_APPLIED`, `409 LISTING_NOT_ACTIVE`.
198	
199	#### `POST /applications/:id/withdraw` — creator
200	#### `POST /applications/:id/approve` — lister
201	Includes re-check of eligibility (may return `409 INELIGIBLE_NOW` with `override_allowed=true`).
202	```ts
203	interface Req { override_ineligible?: boolean; decision_note?: string; }
204	```
205	#### `POST /applications/:id/reject` — lister
206	```ts
207	interface Req { decision_note?: string; }
208	```
209	
210	### 5.6 Submissions
211	
212	#### `POST /applications/:id/submissions`
213	```ts
214	interface Req { videos: { platform: Platform; url: string }[]; cover_note?: string; }
215	interface Res { submission_id: string; }
216	```
217	Side effects: validates each URL via oEmbed (422 if unreachable); creates submission + submission_videos; notifies lister (`submission_received`).
218	Errors: `403 APPLICATION_NOT_APPROVED`, `409 SUBMISSION_EXISTS`, `422 INVALID_VIDEO_URL`.
219	
220	#### `POST /submissions/:id/approve` — lister
221	#### `POST /submissions/:id/reject` — lister
222	#### `POST /submissions/:id/request-revision` — lister (only up to 2 times)
223	```ts
224	interface Req { decision_note?: string; }
225	```
226	#### `POST /submissions/:id/resubmit` — creator (when status=revision_requested)
227	```ts
228	interface Req { videos: { platform: Platform; url: string }[]; cover_note?: string; }
229	```
230	
231	### 5.7 Notifications
232	
233	#### `GET /rest/v1/notifications` — PostgREST direct read (RLS-scoped)
234	#### `POST /notifications/mark-read`
235	```ts
236	interface Req { ids: string[] | 'all'; }
237	interface Res { updated: number; }
238	```
239	#### **Realtime** — subscribe to `notifications` table filtered by `user_id=eq.<me>`. Client uses `supabase.channel('notif').on('postgres_changes', ...)`.
240	#### `POST /push/register`
241	```ts
242	interface Req { expo_token: string; platform: 'ios' | 'android'; }
243	interface Res { ok: true; }
244	```
245	
246	### 5.8 Realtime Channels (summary)
247	
248	| Channel | Who | Events |
249	|---|---|---|
250	| `notifications:user_id=<me>` | both | INSERT |
251	| `applications:listing_id=<id>` | lister | INSERT, UPDATE |
252	| `submissions:listing_id=<id>` | lister | INSERT, UPDATE |
253	| `applications:creator_id=<me>` | creator | UPDATE (approval/rejection) |
254	| `submissions:creator_id=<me>` | creator | UPDATE |
255	
256	### 5.9 Webhooks
257	
258	- **TikTok**: no webhook support for Display API metrics. Must poll.
259	- **Instagram**: webhooks exist for mentions/comments but not for follower_count changes. Skip in MVP.
260	
261	---
262	
263	## 6. Eligibility Evaluation Engine
264	
265	### 6.1 Condition Schema (already in §4.7)
266	
267	A pre-condition is a row: `(metric, platform, operator, numeric_threshold | text_threshold | bool_threshold)`.
268	
269	Catalog:
270	| metric | platform | operator | threshold type |
271	|---|---|---|---|
272	| `min_followers` | tiktok \| instagram | gte | numeric |
273	| `min_avg_views_last_n` | tiktok \| instagram | gte | numeric |
274	| `min_total_likes` | tiktok | gte | numeric |
275	| `min_videos_posted` | tiktok \| instagram | gte | numeric |
276	| `verified_only` | tiktok \| instagram | bool | boolean |
277	
278	### 6.2 Evaluation Function Signature
279	
280	```ts
281	type Metric = 'min_followers' | 'min_avg_views_last_n' | 'min_total_likes' | 'min_videos_posted' | 'verified_only';
282	interface Condition { metric: Metric; platform: 'tiktok' | 'instagram'; operator: 'gte' | 'bool'; threshold: number | boolean; }
283	interface CreatorMetrics {
284	  tiktok: { follower_count?: number; avg_views_last_10?: number; total_likes?: number; video_count?: number; is_verified?: boolean; fetched_at?: string } | null;
285	  instagram: { follower_count?: number; avg_views_last_10?: number; media_count?: number; fetched_at?: string } | null;
286	}
287	interface EligibilityResult { eligible: boolean; failed: Array<{condition: Condition; actual: number | boolean | null}>; stale_platforms: string[]; }
288	
289	function evaluate(conditions: Condition[], metrics: CreatorMetrics): EligibilityResult { ... }
290	```
291	
292	### 6.3 Where It Runs
293	
294	- **Client-side (fast UI):** on app resume we fetch `creator_profiles` row once into a context. Feed query returns listings with their `pre_conditions` inlined; client filters locally. Gives instant "eligible only" toggle without a round-trip per listing.
295	- **Server-side (authoritative):** `POST /applications` runs the exact same TypeScript function on Deno (Edge Function), reading the same denormalized metrics from `creator_profiles`. No trust in client filter.
296	- **At approve-time (re-check):** `POST /applications/:id/approve` re-runs evaluation using current metrics, NOT the metrics at apply-time. (Decision #12.)
297	
298	### 6.4 Scaling Feed to 1000+ Listings
299	
300	- Denormalize `min_followers_tiktok` and `min_followers_instagram` onto `listings` as generated columns (MAX across pre-conditions per platform). Add indexes.
301	- Feed query:
302	  ```sql
303	  select * from public.listings
304	  where status = 'active'
305	    and (min_followers_tiktok is null or min_followers_tiktok <= $creator_tt_followers)
306	    and (min_followers_ig is null or min_followers_ig <= $creator_ig_followers)
307	  order by created_at desc
308	  limit 50;
309	  ```
310	- More complex conditions (avg views, video count) evaluated client-side after this first-pass server filter trims to hundreds.
311	- **No need for full-text search or ElasticSearch in MVP.** Postgres btree indexes suffice.
312

423	
424	## 8. MVP Scope Cuts
425	
426	Sorted by "most likely to ship day one" impact.
427	
428	1. **No payment system.** `price` is a display string. Off-platform handshake. Cut: Stripe Connect, KYC, escrow, disputes-with-money. Defer to v2.
429	
430	2. **OAuth required even though "no auth" is the PRD wish.** Keeping this full-scope is non-negotiable per §3e. But we DO cut email/password signup entirely — OAuth is the ONLY way in for creators. (Listers use email/username.)
431	
432	3. **Instagram metrics gated behind Business/Creator account requirement.** If the creator has a personal IG account, we show: "Instagram metrics require a free conversion to Creator account. Continue with TikTok only?" — do NOT build a workaround. Defer any personal-IG support.
433	
434	4. **No post-submission performance tracking in v1.** We store the video URL and embed it. We do NOT track view_count growth, ROI, etc. Cut this; sell it in v2 as "Marketify Analytics."
435	
436	5. **No lister dashboard analytics.** Just list views of their campaigns + applications + submissions. No charts, no funnel. Cut.
437	
438	6. **No admin moderation console.** Reports go into a table, admin reads it via Supabase Studio. No in-app admin tooling.
439	
440	7. **No revision-request flow in v1.** Cut `revision_requested` state; lister either approves or rejects. Reduces submission state machine from 5 states to 3. (Easy to add back; the submission table already has the column.)
441	   - *Tradeoff: worse creator experience when close-but-not-quite. Watch and re-add quickly.*
442	
443	8. **No push notifications in v1.** In-app inbox only + Realtime. Expo Push adds provisioning friction (APNs cert, FCM key). Re-add in v1.1 once core loop works.
444	
445	9. **Application/submission expiration timers: DO NOT cut.** Cheap to ship, keeps the system clean.
446	
447	10. **Listing versioning + cascade-cancel: DO NOT cut.** This is the single trickiest business rule and is core to trust. Ship it.
448	
449	11. **Metrics auto-refresh cron: DO NOT cut.** Required to keep eligibility honest. Ship it.
450	
451	12. **Eligibility pre-filter client-side + server enforcement: DO NOT cut.** Core UX.
452	
453	13. **Sample videos: simplify.** Store URL only; don't ship upload-to-Supabase-Storage. Use external URLs (TikTok/IG). Cut Storage integration for samples.
454	
455	14. **Lister can "close" manually; no auto-close-on-max_submissions in v1.** Cut the trigger path. Add back once we see real usage patterns. (Tradeoff: listers must remember to close.)
456	
457	15. **No creator blocklist, no lister blocklist.** Report-only.
458	
459	16. **No in-app chat.** Out of scope.
460	
461	---

463	## Appendix A: Open Questions for Product
464	
465	These decisions were made unilaterally above and merit product review before build:
466	
467	1. **OAuth mandatory for creators** (§3e, §8.2) — overrides PRD "no auth."
468	2. **No real money in MVP** (§1.18) — confirm with business.
469	3. **Post-condition changes DO cancel pending applications** (§1.10) — stricter than PRD literal text.
470	4. **14d application / 7d submission expiration** (§1.17) — numbers are my guess.
471	5. **Revision flow cut from v1** (§8.7) — user-facing impact.
472	6. **One role per account** (§1.3) — forces double-signup for creator-listers.
473	7. **Submission re-use across listings allowed** (§1.26) — flagged but not blocked.

file.content:
update public.creator_profiles
        set instagram_follower_count = new.follower_count,
            instagram_avg_views_last_10 = new.avg_views_last_10,
            instagram_media_count = new.video_count,
            metrics_fetched_at = new.fetched_at,
            updated_at = now()
      where user_id = sl.user_id;
    end if;
  end if;
  return new;
end $$;

create trigger trg_denorm_metrics
  after insert on public.metric_snapshots
  for each row execute function app_private.denorm_metrics();

create or replace function app_private.submission_side_effects()
returns trigger language plpgsql security definer as $$
declare
  lst public.listings%rowtype;
begin
  if tg_op = 'UPDATE' and new.status = 'approved' and old.status <> 'approved' then
    select l.* into lst
      from public.listings l
      join public.applications a on a.listing_id = l.id
      where a.id = new.application_id;
    update public.listings
      set approved_submissions_count = approved_submissions_count + 1
      where id = lst.id;
    if lst.max_submissions is not null
       and lst.approved_submissions_count + 1 >= lst.max_submissions then
      update public.listings
        set status = 'closed', closed_at = now()
        where id = lst.id;
    end if;
  end if;
  return new;
end $$;

create trigger trg_submission_side_effects
  after update on public.submissions
  for each row execute function app_private.submission_side_effects();

-- Daily cron jobs (schedules configurable)
select cron.schedule('expire-stale', '0 * * * *', $$
  update public.applications
    set status = 'expired', updated_at = now()
    where status = 'pending' and created_at < now() - interval '14 days';
  update public.submissions
    set status = 'expired', updated_at = now()
    where status = 'pending' and created_at < now() - interval '7 days';
  update public.listings
    set status = 'expired'
    where status = 'active' and end_date is not null and end_date < now();
  update public.metric_snapshots
    set status = 'stale'
    where status = 'fresh' and fetched_at < now() - interval '24 hours' and is_latest;
$$);
```

## 5. API / Edge Function Spec Sheet

**Transport:**
- **Direct table reads** via `postgrest` (Supabase Data API) for anything RLS-safe (feed browse, notifications inbox).
- **Edge Functions** for anything that (a) calls an external API, (b) performs multi-table writes atomically, (c) enforces server-side business logic, or (d) issues JWTs.
- **Realtime channels** on `applications`, `submissions`, `notifications`.

Roles below refer to custom-JWT `role` claim.

### 5.1 Auth & Identity

#### `POST /auth/signup-creator`
Create creator account, redirect to TikTok or IG OAuth.
```ts
interface Req { role: 'creator'; initial_platform: 'tiktok' | 'instagram'; username?: string; }
interface Res { auth_url: string; state: string; } // 302-style payload
```
Side effects: inserts row into `oauth_states`. No user row yet.
Errors: `400 INVALID_PLATFORM`.

#### `POST /auth/signup-lister`
```ts
interface Req { role: 'lister'; username: string; email: string; org_name: string; website_url?: string; }
interface Res { user_id: string; jwt: string; }
```
Side effects: `users` + `lister_profiles` insert. Issues custom JWT.
Errors: `409 USERNAME_TAKEN`, `409 EMAIL_TAKEN`.

#### `GET /auth/oauth-callback/:platform`
Handle redirect from TikTok/IG. Exchange code → tokens → user_info → find-or-create user.
```ts
interface Query { code: string; state: string; }
interface Res { jwt: string; user_id: string; is_new: boolean; }
```
Side effects: `users`+`creator_profiles`+`social_links` upsert; enqueues initial metric fetch; emits event; deletes `oauth_states` row.
Errors: `400 INVALID_STATE`, `401 OAUTH_FAILED`, `409 ACCOUNT_LINKED_TO_OTHER_USER`.

#### `POST /auth/signout`
```ts
interface Req { session_id: string; }
interface Res { ok: true; }
```
Since deleting a user does NOT invalidate JWTs (per Supabase security note), we keep a `session_id` claim and a server-side `revoked_sessions` set (redis/KV or `app_private.revoked_sessions` table) checked by an edge middleware.

### 5.2 Social Accounts & Metrics

#### `POST /social/link-start`
Start linking an additional platform to an existing user.
```ts
interface Req { platform: 'tiktok' | 'instagram'; }
interface Res { auth_url: string; state: string; }
```
Auth: creator.

#### `POST /social/unlink`
```ts
interface Req { social_link_id: string; }
interface Res { ok: true; }
```
Auth: creator (owner). Errors: `409 LAST_LINK` if this is their only linked platform.

#### `POST /metrics/refresh`
Manually trigger metric refresh. Rate-limited.
```ts
interface Req { social_link_id: string; }
interface Res { snapshot_id: string; status: 'queued' | 'already_fresh'; }
```
Side effects: writes `metric_snapshots` (status=refreshing) and kicks background task. Idempotency: second call within 6 h returns `already_fresh`.
Errors: `429 RATE_LIMIT` (includes `retry_after`), `401 REAUTH_REQUIRED`, `502 PROVIDER_ERROR`.

#### Internal cron: `metrics-refresh-all`
Daily at 03:00 UTC. Picks links with `fetched_at < now() - 24h` in batches of 100, QPS-capped.

### 5.3 Listings — Browse (Creator)

#### `GET /rest/v1/listings?status=eq.active...` (PostgREST direct)
Feed query with filters. Client passes creator's denormalized metrics as query params for eligibility pre-filter, but the **authoritative** eligibility check runs in `/listings/:id/eligibility` before apply.

#### `GET /listings/:id` (Edge Function)
```ts
interface Res {
  listing: Listing; // active current_version joined
  conditions: ListingCondition[];
  sample_videos: SampleVideo[];
  eligibility: {
    eligible: boolean;
    failed_conditions: Array<{ metric, required, actual }>;
    has_active_application: boolean;
  };
}
```
Auth: creator or lister (lister sees their own).

### 5.4 Listings — Manage (Lister)

#### `POST /listings` — create
```ts
interface Req {
  title: string; description?: string;
  price_cents: number; currency?: string;
  max_submissions?: number | null;
  end_date?: string | null;
  pre_conditions: ListingConditionInput[];
  post_conditions: ListingConditionInput[];
  sample_videos: { platform: Platform; url: string; caption?: string; }[];
  publish: boolean; // false => draft
}
interface Res { listing_id: string; version_id: string; }
```
Side effects: `listings` + `listing_versions` + `listing_conditions` + `sample_videos` in a txn.

#### `PATCH /listings/:id` — update
```ts
interface Req { /* any subset of create */ }
interface Res { listing_id: string; version_id: string; cascaded_applications: number; }
```
Side effects: if versioned fields change, trigger cancels pending applications and sends notifications. Returns count of cascaded apps so UI can confirm.

#### `POST /listings/:id/status`
```ts
interface Req { action: 'publish' | 'pause' | 'resume' | 'close' | 'archive'; }
interface Res { ok: true; new_status: ListingStatus; }
```
Auth: lister (owner). Server validates state transition.

### 5.5 Applications

#### `POST /applications`
```ts
interface Req { listing_id: string; cover_note?: string; }
interface Res { application_id: string; listing_version_id: string; }
```
Idempotency: server checks partial-unique index; on collision returns existing `application_id`.
Side effects: re-evaluates eligibility on server; creates row; notifies lister (`application_received`).
Errors: `403 INELIGIBLE`, `409 ALREADY_APPLIED`, `409 LISTING_NOT_ACTIVE`.

#### `POST /applications/:id/withdraw` — creator
#### `POST /applications/:id/approve` — lister
Includes re-check of eligibility (may return `409 INELIGIBLE_NOW` with `override_allowed=true`).
```ts
interface Req { override_ineligible?: boolean; decision_note?: string; }
```
#### `POST /applications/:id/reject` — lister
```ts
interface Req { decision_note?: string; }
```

### 5.6 Submissions

#### `POST /applications/:id/submissions`
```ts
interface Req { videos: { platform: Platform; url: string }[]; cover_note?: string; }
interface Res { submission_id: string; }
```
Side effects: validates each URL via oEmbed (422 if unreachable); creates submission + submission_videos; notifies lister (`submission_received`).
Errors: `403 APPLICATION_NOT_APPROVED`, `409 SUBMISSION_EXISTS`, `422 INVALID_VIDEO_URL`.

#### `POST /submissions/:id/approve` — lister
#### `POST /submissions/:id/reject` — lister
#### `POST /submissions/:id/request-revision` — lister (only up to 2 times)
```ts
interface Req { decision_note?: string; }
```
#### `POST /submissions/:id/resubmit` — creator (when status=revision_requested)
```ts
interface Req { videos: { platform: Platform; url: string }[]; cover_note?: string; }
```

### 5.7 Notifications

#### `GET /rest/v1/notifications` — PostgREST direct read (RLS-scoped)
#### `POST /notifications/mark-read`
```ts
interface Req { ids: string[] | 'all'; }
interface Res { updated: number; }
```
#### **Realtime** — subscribe to `notifications` table filtered by `user_id=eq.<me>`. Client uses `supabase.channel('notif').on('postgres_changes', ...)`.
#### `POST /push/register`
```ts
interface Req { expo_token: string; platform: 'ios' | 'android'; }
interface Res { ok: true; }
```

### 5.8 Realtime Channels (summary)

| Channel | Who | Events |
|---|---|---|
| `notifications:user_id=<me>` | both | INSERT |
| `applications:listing_id=<id>` | lister | INSERT, UPDATE |
| `submissions:listing_id=<id>` | lister | INSERT, UPDATE |
| `applications:creator_id=<me>` | creator | UPDATE (approval/rejection) |
| `submissions:creator_id=<me>` | creator | UPDATE |

### 5.9 Webhooks

- **TikTok**: no webhook support for Display API metrics. Must poll.
- **Instagram**: webhooks exist for mentions/comments but not for follower_count changes. Skip in MVP.

## 6. Eligibility Evaluation Engine

### 6.1 Condition Schema (already in §4.7)

A pre-condition is a row: `(metric, platform, operator, numeric_threshold | text_threshold | bool_threshold)`.

Catalog:
| metric | platform | operator | threshold type |
|---|---|---|---|
| `min_followers` | tiktok \| instagram | gte | numeric |
| `min_avg_views_last_n` | tiktok \| instagram | gte | numeric |
| `min_total_likes` | tiktok | gte | numeric |
| `min_videos_posted` | tiktok \| instagram | gte | numeric |
| `verified_only` | tiktok \| instagram | bool | boolean |

### 6.2 Evaluation Function Signature

```ts
type Metric = 'min_followers' | 'min_avg_views_last_n' | 'min_total_likes' | 'min_videos_posted' | 'verified_only';
interface Condition { metric: Metric; platform: 'tiktok' | 'instagram'; operator: 'gte' | 'bool'; threshold: number | boolean; }
interface CreatorMetrics {
  tiktok: { follower_count?: number; avg_views_last_10?: number; total_likes?: number; video_count?: number; is_verified?: boolean; fetched_at?: string } | null;
  instagram: { follower_count?: number; avg_views_last_10?: number; media_count?: number; fetched_at?: string } | null;
}
interface EligibilityResult { eligible: boolean; failed: Array<{condition: Condition; actual: number | boolean | null}>; stale_platforms: string[]; }

function evaluate(conditions: Condition[], metrics: CreatorMetrics): EligibilityResult { ... }
```

### 6.3 Where It Runs

- **Client-side (fast UI):** on app resume we fetch `creator_profiles` row once into a context. Feed query returns listings with their `pre_conditions` inlined; client filters locally. Gives instant "eligible only" toggle without a round-trip per listing.
- **Server-side (authoritative):** `POST /applications` runs the exact same TypeScript function on Deno (Edge Function), reading the same denormalized metrics from `creator_profiles`. No trust in client filter.
- **At approve-time (re-check):** `POST /applications/:id/approve` re-runs evaluation using current metrics, NOT the metrics at apply-time. (Decision #12.)

### 6.4 Scaling Feed to 1000+ Listings

- Denormalize `min_followers_tiktok` and `min_followers_instagram` onto `listings` as generated columns (MAX across pre-conditions per platform). Add indexes.
- Feed query:
  ```sql
  select * from public.listings
  where status = 'active'
    and (min_followers_tiktok is null or min_followers_tiktok <= $creator_tt_followers)
    and (min_followers_ig is null or min_followers_ig <= $creator_ig_followers)
  order by created_at desc
  limit 50;
  ```
- More complex conditions (avg views, video count) evaluated client-side after this first-pass server filter trims to hundreds.
- **No need for full-text search or ElasticSearch in MVP.** Postgres btree indexes suffice.

 │                                                │─INSERT notification(lister)>│
 │<─{application_id}──────────────────────────────│                             │
 │                                                                              │
Realtime: lister receives INSERT on applications channel
```

### 7.3 Lister Edit Listing → Cascade

```
Lister app       EdgeFn(listings update)       DB (trigger)              Affected creators
 │─PATCH /listings/:id price────────>│                                     │
 │                                    │─UPDATE listings price_cents────────>│
 │                                    │                   trg_bump_version │
 │                                    │                   ├─INSERT listing_versions
 │                                    │                   ├─UPDATE applications SET status=cancelled_listing_edit
 │                                    │                   └─INSERT notifications
 │<─{version_id, cascaded=12}─────────│                                     │
                                                                   Realtime push to each creator
```

### 7.4 Creator Submit → Lister Review

```
Creator app       EdgeFn(submissions)          oEmbed (TikTok/IG)     DB              Lister app
 │─POST /applications/:id/submissions────>│                                             │
 │                                         │─validate URLs via oEmbed─>│                │
 │                                         │<─200/404─────────────────│                │
 │                                         │─INSERT submissions + videos──────>│        │
 │                                         │─INSERT notification(lister)──────>│        │
 │<─{submission_id}────────────────────────│                                  │ Realtime├─>lister UI updates
 │                                                                             │         │
 │                                            Lister reviews, approves         │         │
 │                                         <─POST /submissions/:id/approve─────────────│
 │                                         │─UPDATE submissions status=approved>│        │
 │                                         │         (trigger auto_close_on_max)│        │
 │                                         │─INSERT notification(creator)──────>│        │
 │ Realtime update ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────│        │
```

### 7.5 Metrics Refresh (Rate Limited + Failure)

```
Creator app    EdgeFn(metrics/refresh)    Rate limiter (edge KV)    Platform API     DB
 │─POST /metrics/refresh sl_id─>│                                                      │
 │                                │─check last_refresh_at > 6h?──>│                    │
 │                                │<─allow/deny──────────────────│                    │
 │  (denied)                                                                            │
 │<─429 {retry_after}─────────────│                                                      │
 │  (allowed)                                                                            │
 │                                │─INSERT snapshot status=refreshing─────────────>DB   │
 │                                │─GET /v2/user/info/────────────>│                    │
 │                                │<─200 or 401 or 5xx─────────────│                    │
 │  (success)                                                                            │
 │                                │─UPDATE snapshot status=fresh + data──────────>DB    │
 │                                │  (trigger denorm → creator_profiles)                │
 │<─{snapshot_id, status=fresh}──│                                                      │
 │  (401 → token expired)                                                                │
 │                                │─attempt refresh_token─────────>│                    │
 │                                │<─new access_token or fail──────│                    │
 │                                │  if fail: UPDATE snapshot failed, sl.fail_count++   │
 │                                │  if fail_count>=3: sl.status=failed_fetch           │
 │                                │  INSERT notification metrics_refresh_failed         │
 │<─{error: REAUTH_REQUIRED}──────│                                                      │
```

---

## 8. MVP Scope Cuts

Sorted by "most likely to ship day one" impact.

1. **No payment system.** `price` is a display string. Off-platform handshake. Cut: Stripe Connect, KYC, escrow, disputes-with-money. Defer to v2.

2. **OAuth required even though "no auth" is the PRD wish.** Keeping this full-scope is non-negotiable per §3e. But we DO cut email/password signup entirely — OAuth is the ONLY way in for creators. (Listers use email/username.)

3. **Instagram metrics gated behind Business/Creator account requirement.** If the creator has a personal IG account, we show: "Instagram metrics require a free conversion to Creator account. Continue with TikTok only?" — do NOT build a workaround. Defer any personal-IG support.

4. **No post-submission performance tracking in v1.** We store the video URL and embed it. We do NOT track view_count growth, ROI, etc. Cut this; sell it in v2 as "Marketify Analytics."

5. **No lister dashboard analytics.** Just list views of their campaigns + applications + submissions. No charts, no funnel. Cut.

6. **No admin moderation console.** Reports go into a table, admin reads it via Supabase Studio. No in-app admin tooling.

7. **No revision-request flow in v1.** Cut `revision_requested` state; lister either approves or rejects. Reduces submission state machine from 5 states to 3. (Easy to add back; the submission table already has the column.)
   - *Tradeoff: worse creator experience when close-but-not-quite. Watch and re-add quickly.*

8. **No push notifications in v1.** In-app inbox only + Realtime. Expo Push adds provisioning friction (APNs cert, FCM key). Re-add in v1.1 once core loop works.

9. **Application/submission expiration timers: DO NOT cut.** Cheap to ship, keeps the system clean.

10. **Listing versioning + cascade-cancel: DO NOT cut.** This is the single trickiest business rule and is core to trust. Ship it.

11. **Metrics auto-refresh cron: DO NOT cut.** Required to keep eligibility honest. Ship it.

12. **Eligibility pre-filter client-side + server enforcement: DO NOT cut.** Core UX.

13. **Sample videos: simplify.** Store URL only; don't ship upload-to-Supabase-Storage. Use external URLs (TikTok/IG). Cut Storage integration for samples.

14. **Lister can "close" manually; no auto-close-on-max_submissions in v1.** Cut the trigger path. Add back once we see real usage patterns. (Tradeoff: listers must remember to close.)

15. **No creator blocklist, no lister blocklist.** Report-only.

16. **No in-app chat.** Out of scope.

---

## Appendix A: Open Questions for Product

These decisions were made unilaterally above and merit product review before build:

1. **OAuth mandatory for creators** (§3e, §8.2) — overrides PRD "no auth."
2. **No real money in MVP** (§1.18) — confirm with business.
3. **Post-condition changes DO cancel pending applications** (§1.10) — stricter than PRD literal text.
4. **14d application / 7d submission expiration** (§1.17) — numbers are my guess.
5. **Revision flow cut from v1** (§8.7) — user-facing impact.
6. **One role per account** (§1.3) — forces double-signup for creator-listers.
7. **Submission re-use across listings allowed** (§1.26) — flagged but not blocked.

---

**End of document.** Ready for review. When cuts are finalized, I can generate the first migration file via `supabase migration new marketify_init` and deploy skeleton edge functions.
