message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user wants to build "Marketify," a mobile app for the creator marketing industry connecting creators with companies posting bounty-like campaigns. Stack: Expo + React Native + Reanimated + Supabase + originally TikTok/Instagram APIs. The app has two roles (creator | lister) chosen at signup, with pre-conditions (follower count, avg views) and post-conditions (content rules) per campaign. The user first asked me to dispatch two subagents in parallel (UI agent with ui-ux-pro-max skill + technical agent) to analyze the PRD, then synthesize their feedback into a polished product plan. After reviewing my synthesis, the user asked me to walk through 7 open questions to resolve all product decisions. The user answered all 7 questions, including a major pivot on Q1: instead of TikTok/Instagram OAuth, use **Apify scrapers** (TikTok Profile Scraper, Instagram Followers Count Scraper, Instagram Reel Scraper) to fetch metrics via handle input only. I must now update all three docs to reflect the resolved decisions.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Expo + React Native + Reanimated v3 (UI thread worklets, gesture handler)
   - Supabase: Postgres, Edge Functions (Deno), Realtime, Storage, pg_cron, RLS
   - Custom JWT auth (no Supabase Auth) — signed by us, sub=user_id, role, session_id
   - **Apify** scraping platform via `apify-client` NPM package
   - Apify actors: `tiktok-profile-scraper` (clockworks), `instagram-follower-count-scraper`, `instagram-reel-scraper`
   - Apify sync pattern: `client.actor(slug).call(input, {waitSecs: 60})` → `{defaultDatasetId}`
   - Apify async pattern: `.start()` with webhooks → our `/webhooks/apify` endpoint
   - Listing versioning with cascade-cancel trigger (bump_listing_version Postgres function)
   - Eligibility evaluation engine: generated columns `min_followers_tiktok/ig` on listings for O(log n) feed filter
   - Denormalized creator metrics on `creator_profiles` for fast eligibility check
   - Partial unique indexes for application uniqueness
   - oEmbed for video rendering (TikTok + IG)
   - Neubrutalism design language (ui-ux-pro-max skill catalog)
   - context7 MCP for library documentation

3. Files and Code Sections:
   
   - **`/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/docs/design.md`** (created, NOT YET UPDATED for Apify pivot)
     - Full UI/UX design document
     - Neubrutalism style, pink #EC4899 / orange #F97316 palette, Clash Display + Satoshi fonts
     - Full screen map for creator + lister roles
     - Text-described wireframes for 6 critical screens
     - Component library inventory
     - **STILL NEEDS:** Onboarding section (§2.1) updated — change "Connect TikTok OAuth" screens to "Enter your TikTok/Instagram handle" screens. Remove IG Business/Creator account requirement copy.
   
   - **`/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/docs/tech-architecture.md`** (v0.2 — just fully rewritten)
     - Complete Apify-based architecture replacing OAuth
     - Removed `oauth_states` table
     - Modified `social_links` table:
     ```sql
     create table public.social_links (
       id                      uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
       user_id                 uuid not null references public.users(id) on delete cascade,
       platform                platform not null,
       handle                  citext not null,
       status                  social_link_status not null default 'linked',
       last_scrape_run_id      text,
       last_scrape_attempt_at  timestamptz,
       last_scrape_error       text,
       fail_count              smallint not null default 0,
       created_at              timestamptz not null default now(),
       updated_at              timestamptz not null default now(),
       unique (platform, handle)
     );
     ```
     - Added `apify_run_id` to `metric_snapshots` with partial unique index for webhook idempotency
     - Removed `expired` state from listings, removed `revision_requested` from submissions
     - Removed application/submission expiration cron (Q5=C)
     - New endpoint `POST /webhooks/apify` — receives Apify webhook, fetches dataset, updates snapshot
     - New endpoints: `POST /social/add-handle`, `POST /social/update-handle`
     - Simplified `POST /auth/signup-creator`:
     ```ts
     interface Req {
       username: string;
       tiktok_handle?: string;
       instagram_handle?: string;
       display_name?: string;
       country?: string;
     }
     interface Res {
       user_id: string;
       jwt: string;
       social_links: { id: string; platform: Platform; handle: string; status: 'linked' }[];
       initial_scrape_queued: boolean;
     }
     ```
     - Rewrote sequence diagrams 7.1 (signup+Apify) and 7.5 (metrics refresh)
     - Updated scope cuts: removed IG Business account requirement, added "no handle ownership verification"
     - Marked all 7 open questions resolved in Appendix A
   
   - **`/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/docs/product-plan.md`** (created, NOT YET UPDATED for Apify pivot)
     - Still references OAuth-for-creators as Open Question #1
     - Still references IG Business/Creator requirement
     - Still mentions 14d/7d expiration timers (now Q5=C, no auto-expire)
     - Still mentions revision flow as "to decide" (now cut)
     - **STILL NEEDS:** §2 rewrite for Apify pivot, §3.1/3.2 scope updates, §4 decisions table, §6 build sequence (no OAuth steps), §7 move to resolved

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **Technical agent output too large:** The Task returned 69.4KB which exceeded token limits when reading. Fixed by using Python to split the JSON file into 3 parts at `/tmp/tech_part_{1,2,3}.md` and reading each chunk separately.
   - **User feedback on Q1:** User rejected my OAuth recommendation, saying "I did not know metric API's for these platforms required a business acount and auth. Let's change strategies, our new API is going to be apify." This was a major course-correction — the user named specific Apify actors with specific behaviors (Instagram Followers Count Scraper, Instagram Reel Scraper for last 10 reels' videoViewCount, TikTok Profile Scraper for profile + last 10 videos' playCount). I pivoted the entire tech architecture accordingly.

5. Problem Solving:
   - **PRD "no auth" vs metrics requirement:** Originally both agents converged on requiring OAuth for real metrics. After user chose Apify, the PRD's original "no auth, just handles" vision became feasible since Apify scrapes public pages without OAuth.
   - **Parallel agent dispatch:** Used `superpowers:dispatching-parallel-agents` skill to run UI + technical analysis concurrently, each with self-contained prompts since they don't share my conversation context.
   - **Apify API pattern research:** Used context7 (`/apify/apify-client-js`) to verify actor.call(input, {waitSecs}) sync pattern and webhook receiver pattern for async runs.

6. All user messages:
   - **Initial PRD + request:** "we are going to create an app for creator marketing industry: 'marketify'..." — full PRD with creator/lister flows, pre/post-conditions, component statuses, stack. Ended with "create and run 2 subagents in parallel...1. UI agent...2. technical expert agent...analyze their feedback and produce a polished product plan."
   - **"Ask me the 7 questions and lets resolve it together"**
   - **Seven answers:** "Q1: C - I did not know metric API's for these platforms required a business acount and auth. Let's change strategies, our new API is going to be apify: * Instagram: Step 1 — Scrape the follower count Use the Instagram Followers Count Scraper...Step 2 — Scrape the last 10 reels + view counts Use the Instagram Reel Scraper...TikTok: The TikTok Profile Scraper handles everything at once...Q2: A, Q3: A, Q4: A, Q5: C, Q6: A, Q7: A"
   - **"Tool loaded."** (confirming the ToolSearch loaded context7 tools)
   - **Summary request** (current message)

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Update `design.md` §2.1 (Onboarding screens) to replace OAuth flow with handle input flow. Remove IG Business/Creator account conversion requirement copy.
   - Update `product-plan.md`:
     - §2 (The One Non-Negotiable Decision) — rewrite around Apify pivot, no longer "non-negotiable override"
     - §3.1/3.2 (scope) — remove OAuth steps, remove IG Business requirement, remove expiration cron from scope
     - §4 (Key Technical Decisions) — update all rows with resolved values
     - §6 (Build Sequence) — replace OAuth steps with Apify client + webhook receiver steps
     - §7 (Open Questions) — mark all 7 resolved, move to appendix

8. Current Work:
   I had just completed a full rewrite of `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/docs/tech-architecture.md` to version v0.2, incorporating the Apify pivot (Q1=C) and all other Q2-Q7 answers. The Write call returned successfully. My next planned action before the summary interrupt was to update `design.md` via targeted Edits (Onboarding section only) and then rewrite `product-plan.md` for the Apify pivot. The last message before the summary request was my Write tool success confirmation for tech-architecture.md.

9. Optional Next Step:
   Continue updating the remaining two docs to reflect the Apify pivot. Specifically:
   
   1. Edit `design.md` §2.1 Auth/Setup table — change the creator onboarding screens from OAuth-based ("Connect Socials (Creator)" → "OAuth Callback" → "Initial Metric Fetch") to handle-based ("Enter Handles (Creator)" → "Initial Metric Fetch" where the fetch is via Apify). Remove the line about IG Business/Creator requirement.
   
   2. Rewrite relevant sections of `product-plan.md` to reflect resolved decisions:
      - Replace §2 entirely (was "The One Non-Negotiable Decision" about OAuth) with a shorter "Identity model: Apify-powered" section
      - Update §3.1 (IN scope) build list — replace "OAuth sign-in (TikTok + Instagram)" with "Handle-based signup + Apify scrape on link"
      - Update §3.2 (OUT) scope table — remove row about personal IG support (no longer blocked), remove revision flow row (already cut)
      - Update §4 (Key Technical Decisions) table — all 7 questions now have final answers
      - Update §6 (Build Sequence) — replace steps 3/4 (TikTok OAuth + Instagram OAuth) with a single "Apify client integration + webhook receiver" step, and step 5 becomes "Signup flows (creator with handles, lister with email)"
      - Update §7 — all 7 questions resolved, move to "Resolved Decisions" section
   
   Direct quote from my last substantive response establishing the scope: "Big pivot on Q1 — Apify scraping completely changes the auth story (the PRD's 'no auth, just handles' is actually feasible now). Let me verify the Apify integration pattern via context7, then update all three docs." I updated tech-architecture.md; design.md and product-plan.md remain to be updated per that stated plan.

If you need specific details from before compaction (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/bulutyazgan/.claude/projects/-Users-bulutyazgan-Desktop-marketify/a3145df1-fe82-4ebe-b63a-e1544dc9b353.jsonl

# Marketify — Polished Product Plan

*Synthesis of the initial PRD, the UI/UX design analysis (`design.md`), and the technical architecture analysis (`tech-architecture.md`). This is the canonical plan; source docs are the authoritative detail.*

---

## 0. TL;DR

A mobile marketplace (Expo + React Native + Supabase) where creators claim bounties from companies. Two roles, chosen at onboarding. Creators connect TikTok and/or Instagram via OAuth; listers sign up with a username and email. Listers post campaigns with pre-conditions (follower/view thresholds) and post-conditions (content rules). Creators see only eligible campaigns by default, apply, get approved, film, submit a video URL, get reviewed.

**Design language:** Disciplined Neubrutalism — hard shadows, 2px ink borders, pink/orange creator-economy palette, Reanimated-driven micro-interactions.

**One non-negotiable override of the initial PRD:** "no auth" is infeasible for the eligibility feature as stated. TikTok/Instagram OAuth is required for creators. This is explained in §2.

---

## 1. The Core User Loop

```
CREATOR                              LISTER
────────                             ──────
OAuth TikTok/IG ──┐                  Create campaign (wizard)
                  │                          │
(metrics fetched) │                          ↓
                  │                  Publish → status=active
                  ↓                          │
Browse feed ←─────────────────────────┘ (filter: eligible only)
   │
   ↓
Tap campaign → eligibility evaluated live
   │
   ↓ (if eligible)
Apply (optional pitch) ───────────────────► Lister inbox (Realtime)
                                             │
                                             ↓
                              Approve / Reject application
   (Realtime push to creator) ◄──────────────┘
   │
   ↓ (if approved)
Film → paste video URL ───────────────────► Lister review (video embed + post-condition checklist)
                                             │
                                             ↓
                              Approve / Reject submission
   (Realtime push to creator) ◄──────────────┘
```

Two cross-cutting mechanics that the PRD surfaced:

- **Listing versioning + auto-cancel.** Editing price, pre-conditions, post-conditions, sample videos, or max_submissions bumps a version and cascades `cancelled_listing_edit` to all pending applications, with notifications.
- **Metrics refresh.** Creators can't edit their numbers. Pulled via TikTok/IG API at sign-up, refreshed daily by cron, or manually pulled once per 6 hours per platform via pull-to-refresh.

---

## 2. The One Non-Negotiable Decision

> **PRD said:** *"no auth in the initial product, the users just select their role and create an account with a username and social media links."*
>
> **Reality, per context7 research:** real follower/view counts for TikTok require the `user.info.stats` scope (OAuth). Instagram follower counts require a Graph API call from a Business/Creator account token. There is **no unauthenticated public endpoint** that returns these. oEmbed only returns thumbnails.

**Recommendation (both agents aligned):**

- **No app-level email/password auth.** Correct; keep.
- **Creators sign in via TikTok OAuth and/or Instagram OAuth.** At least one platform is required. The OAuth token IS the session. No hand-typed handles.
- **Listers sign up with username + email + org name.** They're not filtered on metrics; no OAuth needed.
- For Instagram specifically, require Business/Creator account conversion (free, in-app, ~30 seconds). If a creator has only a personal IG, we show: *"Instagram metrics need a free Creator account. Continue with TikTok only?"*

**Fallback if rejected:** ship v1 as "discovery-only" — no pre-conditions at all, creators self-declare handles, listers eyeball applicants manually. Removes the marketplace's central value prop but is honest given the PRD constraint. **Not recommended.**

This is **Open Question #1** — user must confirm before build starts.

---

## 3. Scope — What Ships in v1

### 3.1 IN (build this first)

**Foundations**
- Supabase project + full schema (`docs/tech-architecture.md` §4.7)
- Custom JWT auth (signed by us, RLS-aware)
- Design tokens + base component library (Neubrutalism primitives)

**Creator**
- OAuth sign-in (TikTok + Instagram)
- Metric fetch on link + 6h-throttled manual refresh + daily cron
- Campaign feed with client-side eligibility filter (`Eligible only` toggle)
- Campaign detail with live eligibility evaluation
- Apply flow with server-side eligibility re-check
- My Applications (pending / approved / rejected / cancelled)
- Submission composer (paste URL → oEmbed validate → self-affirm post-conditions)
- My Submissions (pending / approved / rejected)
- Profile (linked socials, metrics, pull-to-refresh)
- In-app notifications inbox

**Lister**
- Username/email signup
- 5-step create-campaign wizard
- My Campaigns (active / inactive)
- Edit campaign with cascade-cancel confirmation (*"This will cancel N applications"*)
- Applications inbox (with creator metrics surfaced)
- Application review (approve with optional re-eligibility override, reject)
- Submissions inbox
- Submission review (embedded video + post-condition checklist + feedback)
- In-app notifications inbox

**Cross-cutting**
- Listing versioning + cascade trigger (Postgres trigger, §4.7)
- Expiration cron (applications 14d, submissions 7d, listings `end_date`)
- Realtime subscriptions (notifications, applications, submissions)
- Append-only `events` audit table

### 3.2 OUT — explicitly cut from v1

| # | Cut | Why | Revisit |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Payment / escrow / Stripe | `price` is display-only; off-platform handshake | v2 |
| 2 | Post-submission performance tracking (view count, ROI) | Requires creator OAuth + repeated API calls | v2 as "Marketify Analytics" |
| 3 | Push notifications | APNs/FCM provisioning friction | v1.1 |
| 4 | Revision-request flow on submissions | Reduces state machine complexity; column reserved | v1.1 |
| 5 | Personal IG account support | Graph API requires Business/Creator | v2 if user demand |
| 6 | In-app admin moderation UI | Reports logged to table, admin via Supabase Studio | v2 |
| 7 | Lister analytics dashboard | Just counts in v1 | v2 |
| 8 | Creator/lister blocklists | Report-only | v2 |
| 9 | In-app chat | Out of scope | v2 |
| 10 | Auto-close-on-max-submissions | Listers close manually; re-add when usage clarifies | v1.1 |
| 11 | Sample-video upload to Storage | External URLs only | v2 |
| 12 | Dark mode | Neubrutalism translates but doubles QA | v2 |
| 13 | A single user being both creator and lister on one account | One role per account | v2 |

---

## 4. Key Technical Decisions

Surfaced from the tech doc's PRD gap analysis. User should confirm these before build:

| # | Decision | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | OAuth required for creators (§2) | Only path to real metrics |
| 2 | Listing versioning bumps on price, pre-conditions, **post-conditions**, sample videos, max_submissions | Changing post-conditions mid-queue is bait-and-switch |
| 3 | One role per account | Simplifies RLS + UX; creator-listers make two accounts |
| 4 | Applications expire 14d pending; submissions expire 7d pending | Keeps pipeline clean; numbers are a guess, adjust from data |
| 5 | Metric refresh: manual cap = 1 per 6h per platform; cron refreshes daily | Balances TikTok/IG rate limits vs freshness |
| 6 | Submission re-use across campaigns allowed (flagged, not blocked) | Same video may legitimately satisfy two campaigns |
| 7 | Eligibility re-checked at lister's approve-time (not at submit-time) | Prevents "metrics gaming"; approval locks creator in |
| 8 | Generated columns `min_followers_tiktok/ig` on `listings` for O(log n) feed filtering | Scales to 1000s of listings on btree index |

---

## 5. Design System Summary

Full details in `docs/design.md`. Highlights:

- **Style:** Neubrutalism (hard shadows, 2px ink borders, 12px card radius, 999px pill radius)
- **Palette:** Pink primary `#EC4899`, Orange CTA `#F97316`, warm canvas `#FFF9F2`, four semantic status colors with soft-bg variants
- **Typography:** Clash Display (display/heading), Satoshi (body/caption), JetBrains Mono (URLs/handles)
- **Motion:** Reanimated 3 worklets. Three spring presets (soft/snappy/bouncy). Press-collapse shadows on every pressable. Celebration burst on approval. Shake on rejection. `useReducedMotion` respected everywhere.
- **Icons:** Lucide React Native only. No emoji.
- **Tabs:** 4 per role. Creator = Discover / Applied / Submitted / Profile. Lister = Home / Campaigns / Inbox / Profile. Lister has a FAB for create-campaign.

---

## 6. Build Sequence (12 steps)

Each step ends with something demonstrable. Numbered for checkpointing.

1. **Supabase project + migration.** Apply the full schema from tech-architecture.md §4.7. Add seed data (one lister, one active listing, one creator). Verify RLS blocks cross-user reads.
2. **Custom JWT edge function + signup-lister endpoint.** Listers can create accounts and get a JWT. Round-trip tested with curl.
3. **TikTok OAuth flow end-to-end.** `/auth/signup-creator` → TikTok consent → `/auth/oauth-callback/tiktok` → user + social_link rows + initial metric snapshot written. Displayed in Supabase Studio.
4. **Instagram OAuth flow.** Same shape. Include the Business/Creator gate in the onboarding copy.
5. **Metric fetch worker.** Background fetch of `user.info.stats` + `video.list` → populate `metric_snapshots` → trigger denorm. `/metrics/refresh` edge function with 6h throttle + `pg_cron` daily job.
6. **Design tokens + primitive component library.** Colors, typography, spacing, `ButtonPrimary`, `ButtonSecondary`, `Chip`, `StatusPill`, `CampaignCard`, `SkeletonCard`, `BottomSheet`, `Toast`. Each with a Storybook-style preview screen.
7. **Creator feed + eligibility filter.** Direct PostgREST query with generated-column filters. Client-side fine-grained eligibility. Pull-to-refresh.
8. **Campaign detail + apply flow.** `/listings/:id` edge function returns listing + conditions + live eligibility. Apply modal → `POST /applications` with server re-check. Toast on success.
9. **Create-campaign wizard (lister).** 5 steps → `POST /listings`. Draft + publish. Review step shows creator-side preview.
10. **Applications inbox + review (lister) + My Applications (creator).** Lister approves/rejects. Realtime updates on both sides. Eligibility re-check on approve with override dialog.
11. **Submission composer + review.** Creator pastes URL → oEmbed validation → submit. Lister embeds video (WebView), checks post-conditions, approves/rejects. Status change animations wired.
12. **Notifications + polishing pass.** In-app inbox, bell badge, deep-link routing, listing edit cascade UX (`"cancel 12 apps?"` modal), empty states, error states, reduced-motion audit.

**Target velocity:** a solo dev with this stack can land steps 1–4 in week 1, 5–7 in week 2, 8–10 in week 3, 11–12 in week 4. Four-week MVP is realistic.

---

## 7. Open Questions Requiring Product Decisions

Before step 1, user should confirm or override:

1. **OAuth-for-creators override** (§2). Biggest call.
2. **No payment in v1** (§3.2 row 1). Is a bounty without money a viable pitch to listers?
3. **Post-condition edits cancel pending applications** (§4 row 2). Stricter than PRD literal text.
4. **Revision flow cut** (§3.2 row 4). Lose creator goodwill on close-calls.
5. **14-day / 7-day expiration defaults** (§4 row 4). Gut numbers.
6. **One role per account** (§4 row 3). Forces creator-listers into two accounts.
7. **Design style: Neubrutalism** (§5). Bold choice; wants explicit buy-in.

---

## 8. Reference Artifacts

- **`docs/design.md`** — full UI/UX language, screen catalog, wireframes, component inventory, motion spec.
- **`docs/tech-architecture.md`** — PRD gap analysis, state machines, context7-verified API research, complete Supabase schema + RLS + triggers + cron, edge-function spec sheet, eligibility engine, sequence diagrams, v1 scope cuts.
- **`docs/product-plan.md`** (this file) — synthesis + decisions + build sequence.

All three are living docs. As decisions land in §7, update the relevant source doc rather than tracking deltas here.

skills.name: superpowers:dispatching-parallel-agents
skills.content:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/bulutyazgan/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/superpowers/5.0.7/skills/dispatching-parallel-agents

# Dispatching Parallel Agents

## Overview

You delegate tasks to specialized agents with isolated context. By precisely crafting their instructions and context, you ensure they stay focused and succeed at their task. They should never inherit your session's context or history — you construct exactly what they need. This also preserves your own context for coordination work.

When you have multiple unrelated failures (different test files, different subsystems, different bugs), investigating them sequentially wastes time. Each investigation is independent and can happen in parallel.

**Core principle:** Dispatch one agent per independent problem domain. Let them work concurrently.

## When to Use

```dot
digraph when_to_use {
    "Multiple failures?" [shape=diamond];
    "Are they independent?" [shape=diamond];
    "Single agent investigates all" [shape=box];
    "One agent per problem domain" [shape=box];
    "Can they work in parallel?" [shape=diamond];
    "Sequential agents" [shape=box];
    "Parallel dispatch" [shape=box];

    "Multiple failures?" -> "Are they independent?" [label="yes"];
    "Are they independent?" -> "Single agent investigates all" [label="no - related"];
    "Are they independent?" -> "Can they work in parallel?" [label="yes"];
    "Can they work in parallel?" -> "Parallel dispatch" [label="yes"];
    "Can they work in parallel?" -> "Sequential agents" [label="no - shared state"];
}
```

**Use when:**
- 3+ test files failing with different root causes
- Multiple subsystems broken independently
- Each problem can be understood without context from others
- No shared state between investigations

**Don't use when:**
- Failures are related (fix one might fix others)
- Need to understand full system state
- Agents would interfere with each other

## The Pattern

### 1. Identify Independent Domains

Group failures by what's broken:
- File A tests: Tool approval flow
- File B tests: Batch completion behavior
- File C tests: Abort functionality

Each domain is independent - fixing tool approval doesn't affect abort tests.

### 2. Create Focused Agent Tasks

Each agent gets:
- **Specific scope:** One test file or subsystem
- **Clear goal:** Make these tests pass
- **Constraints:** Don't change other code
- **Expected output:** Summary of what you found and fixed

### 3. Dispatch in Parallel

```typescript
// In Claude Code / AI environment
Task("Fix agent-tool-abort.test.ts failures")
Task("Fix batch-completion-behavior.test.ts failures")
Task("Fix tool-approval-race-conditions.test.ts failures")
// All three run concurrently
```

### 4. Review and Integrate

When agents return:
- Read each summary
- Verify fixes don't conflict
- Run full test suite
- Integrate all changes

## Agent Prompt Structure

Good agent prompts are:
1. **Focused** - One clear problem domain
2. **Self-contained** - All context needed to understand the problem
3. **Specific about output** - What should the agent return?

```markdown
Fix the 3 failing tests in src/agents/agent-tool-abort.test.ts:

1. "should abort tool with partial output capture" - expects 'interrupted at' in message
2. "should handle mixed completed and aborted tools" - fast tool aborted instead of completed
3. "should properly track pendingToolCount" - expects 3 results but gets 0

These are timing/race condition issues. Your task:

1. Read the test file and understand what each test verifies
2. Identify root cause - timing issues or actual bugs?
3. Fix by:
   - Replacing arbitrary timeouts with event-based waiting
   - Fixing bugs in abort implementation if found
   - Adjusting test expectations if testing changed behavior

Do NOT just increase timeouts - find the real issue.

Return: Summary of what you found and what you fixed.
```

## Common Mistakes

**❌ Too broad:** "Fix all the tests" - agent gets lost
**✅ Specific:** "Fix agent-tool-abort.test.ts" - focused scope

**❌ No context:** "Fix the race condition" - agent doesn't know where
**✅ Context:** Paste the error messages and test names

**❌ No constraints:** Agent might refactor everything
**✅ Constraints:** "Do NOT change production code" or "Fix tests only"

**❌ Vague output:** "Fix it" - you don't know what changed
**✅ Specific:** "Return summary of root cause and changes"

## When NOT to Use

**Related failures:** Fixing one might fix others - investigate together first
**Need full context:** Understanding requires seeing entire system
**Exploratory debugging:** You don't know what's broken yet
**Shared state:** Agents would interfere (editing same files, using same resources)

## Real Example from Session

**Scenario:** 6 test failures across 3 files after major refactoring

**Failures:**
- agent-tool-abort.test.ts: 3 failures (timing issues)
- batch-completion-behavior.test.ts: 2 failures (tools not executing)
- tool-approval-race-conditions.test.ts: 1 failure (execution count = 0)

**Decision:** Independent domains - abort logic separate from batch completion separate from race conditions

**Dispatch:**
```
Agent 1 → Fix agent-tool-abort.test.ts
Agent 2 → Fix batch-completion-behavior.test.ts
Agent 3 → Fix tool-approval-race-conditions.test.ts
```

**Results:**
- Agent 1: Replaced timeouts with event-based waiting
- Agent 2: Fixed event structure bug (threadId in wrong place)
- Agent 3: Added wait for async tool execution to complete

**Integration:** All fixes independent, no conflicts, full suite green

**Time saved:** 3 problems solved in parallel vs sequentially

## Key Benefits

1. **Parallelization** - Multiple investigations happen simultaneously
2. **Focus** - Each agent has narrow scope, less context to track
3. **Independence** - Agents don't interfere with each other
4. **Speed** - 3 problems solved in time of 1

## Verification

After agents return:
1. **Review each summary** - Understand what changed
2. **Check for conflicts** - Did agents edit same code?
3. **Run full suite** - Verify all fixes work together
4. **Spot check** - Agents can make systematic errors

## Real-World Impact

From debugging session (2025-10-03):
- 6 failures across 3 files
- 3 agents dispatched in parallel
- All investigations completed concurrently
- All fixes integrated successfully
- Zero conflicts between agent changes

## plugin:context7:context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

<EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>
You have superpowers.

**Below is the full content of your 'superpowers:using-superpowers' skill - your introduction to using skills. For all other skills, use the 'Skill' tool:**

---
name: using-superpowers
description: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions
---

<SUBAGENT-STOP>
If you were dispatched as a subagent to execute a specific task, skip this skill.
</SUBAGENT-STOP>

<EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>
If you think there is even a 1% chance a skill might apply to what you are doing, you ABSOLUTELY MUST invoke the skill.

IF A SKILL APPLIES TO YOUR TASK, YOU DO NOT HAVE A CHOICE. YOU MUST USE IT.

This is not negotiable. This is not optional. You cannot rationalize your way out of this.
</EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>

## Instruction Priority

Superpowers skills override default system prompt behavior, but **user instructions always take precedence**:

1. **User's explicit instructions** (CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, AGENTS.md, direct requests) — highest priority
2. **Superpowers skills** — override default system behavior where they conflict
3. **Default system prompt** — lowest priority

If CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, or AGENTS.md says "don't use TDD" and a skill says "always use TDD," follow the user's instructions. The user is in control.

## How to Access Skills

**In Claude Code:** Use the `Skill` tool. When you invoke a skill, its content is loaded and presented to you—follow it directly. Never use the Read tool on skill files.

**In Copilot CLI:** Use the `skill` tool. Skills are auto-discovered from installed plugins. The `skill` tool works the same as Claude Code's `Skill` tool.

**In Gemini CLI:** Skills activate via the `activate_skill` tool. Gemini loads skill metadata at session start and activates the full content on demand.

**In other environments:** Check your platform's documentation for how skills are loaded.

## Platform Adaptation

Skills use Claude Code tool names. Non-CC platforms: see `references/copilot-tools.md` (Copilot CLI), `references/codex-tools.md` (Codex) for tool equivalents. Gemini CLI users get the tool mapping loaded automatically via GEMINI.md.

# Using Skills

## The Rule

**Invoke relevant or requested skills BEFORE any response or action.** Even a 1% chance a skill might apply means that you should invoke the skill to check. If an invoked skill turns out to be wrong for the situation, you don't need to use it.

```dot
digraph skill_flow {
    "User message received" [shape=doublecircle];
    "About to EnterPlanMode?" [shape=doublecircle];
    "Already brainstormed?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke brainstorming skill" [shape=box];
    "Might any skill apply?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke Skill tool" [shape=box];
    "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'" [shape=box];
    "Has checklist?" [shape=diamond];
    "Create TodoWrite todo per item" [shape=box];
    "Follow skill exactly" [shape=box];
    "Respond (including clarifications)" [shape=doublecircle];

    "About to EnterPlanMode?" -> "Already brainstormed?";
    "Already brainstormed?" -> "Invoke brainstorming skill" [label="no"];
    "Already brainstormed?" -> "Might any skill apply?" [label="yes"];
    "Invoke brainstorming skill" -> "Might any skill apply?";

    "User message received" -> "Might any skill apply?";
    "Might any skill apply?" -> "Invoke Skill tool" [label="yes, even 1%"];
    "Might any skill apply?" -> "Respond (including clarifications)" [label="definitely not"];
    "Invoke Skill tool" -> "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'";
    "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'" -> "Has checklist?";
    "Has checklist?" -> "Create TodoWrite todo per item" [label="yes"];
    "Has checklist?" -> "Follow skill exactly" [label="no"];
    "Create TodoWrite todo per item" -> "Follow skill exactly";
}
```

## Red Flags

These thoughts mean STOP—you're rationalizing:

| Thought | Reality |
|---------|---------|
| "This is just a simple question" | Questions are tasks. Check for skills. |
| "I need more context first" | Skill check comes BEFORE clarifying questions. |
| "Let me explore the codebase first" | Skills tell you HOW to explore. Check first. |
| "I can check git/files quickly" | Files lack conversation context. Check for skills. |
| "Let me gather information first" | Skills tell you HOW to gather information. |
| "This doesn't need a formal skill" | If a skill exists, use it. |
| "I remember this skill" | Skills evolve. Read current version. |
| "This doesn't count as a task" | Action = task. Check for skills. |
| "The skill is overkill" | Simple things become complex. Use it. |
| "I'll just do this one thing first" | Check BEFORE doing anything. |
| "This feels productive" | Undisciplined action wastes time. Skills prevent this. |
| "I know what that means" | Knowing the concept ≠ using the skill. Invoke it. |

## Skill Priority

When multiple skills could apply, use this order:

1. **Process skills first** (brainstorming, debugging) - these determine HOW to approach the task
2. **Implementation skills second** (frontend-design, mcp-builder) - these guide execution

"Let's build X" → brainstorming first, then implementation skills.
"Fix this bug" → debugging first, then domain-specific skills.

## Skill Types

**Rigid** (TDD, debugging): Follow exactly. Don't adapt away discipline.

**Flexible** (patterns): Adapt principles to context.

The skill itself tells you which.

## User Instructions

Instructions say WHAT, not HOW. "Add X" or "Fix Y" doesn't mean skip workflows.


</EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>

content.content:
1	# Marketify — UI/UX Design Document
2	
3	*Mobile app for the creator marketing industry. Expo + React Native + Reanimated + Supabase.*
4	
5	---
6	
7	## 1. Design Language
8	
9	### 1.1 Chosen Style
10	
11	**Neubrutalism** as the primary language, with disciplined restraint for mobile comfort.
12	
13	**Why it fits Marketify:**
14	- The marketplace/directory pattern (Vibrant & Block-based) is the top match; Neubrutalism is its most purposeful expression — bold borders, hard offset shadows, flat pop colors.
15	- The pattern rewards scannability — hard black outlines on CampaignCards make bounties feel like physical "cards on a board."
16	- WCAG AAA by construction (high contrast), Excellent performance — critical for mid-tier Android.
17	- Differentiated. TikTok is dark-glassmorphic, Instagram is quiet gradient, every campaign-management tool (Grin, Aspire) is corporate-blue SaaS. Neubrutalism carves out a clear creator-native identity.
18	
19	**Disciplines to keep it mobile-friendly (not a desktop stunt):**
20	- Borders are 2px, not 4px.
21	- Shadows are 3px offset max, never 6–8px.
22	- Corner radius is 12px on cards, 999px on pills — not 0. Pure 0 feels brittle on touch.
23	- No intentionally "broken" layouts. Brutalism's anti-design aesthetic is skipped in favor of its craft elements only.
24	
25	### 1.2 Color Palette
26	
27	Base palette anchored on the **"Creator Economy Platform"** colors (pink + orange CTA), with semantic statuses added.
28	
29	| Role | Token | Hex | Usage |
30	|---|---|---|---|
31	| Primary | `--primary` | `#EC4899` | Brand, active tab icon, primary surfaces, logo mark |
32	| Primary Soft | `--primary-soft` | `#FDF2F8` | Primary-tinted backgrounds, selected chip bg |
33	| Primary Deep | `--primary-deep` | `#831843` | Primary text on soft bg, pressed states |
34	| Accent (CTA) | `--cta` | `#F97316` | All primary action buttons, "Apply", "Submit", "Create Campaign" |
35	| Accent Deep | `--cta-deep` | `#9A3412` | CTA pressed state |
36	| Ink (text) | `--ink` | `#0F172A` | All body and heading text, hard borders |
37	| Ink-70 | `--ink-70` | `#475569` | Secondary text, captions |
38	| Ink-40 | `--ink-40` | `#94A3B8` | Tertiary / disabled text |
39	| Surface | `--surface` | `#FFFFFF` | Card bodies |
40	| Canvas | `--canvas` | `#FFF9F2` | App background (warm off-white — matches orange CTA undertone) |
41	| Hairline | `--hairline` | `#E5E7EB` | Dividers inside cards |
42	| **Success (approved)** | `--success` | `#16A34A` | Approved submissions, approved applications, eligible checks |
43	| Success Soft | `--success-soft` | `#DCFCE7` | Approved pill bg |
44	| **Danger (rejected)** | `--danger` | `#DC2626` | Rejected submissions, rejected applications |
45	| Danger Soft | `--danger-soft` | `#FEE2E2` | Rejected pill bg, ineligibility warning |
46	| **Warning (pending)** | `--warning` | `#F59E0B` | Pending status, waiting-for-review states |
47	| Warning Soft | `--warning-soft` | `#FEF3C7` | Pending pill bg |
48	| **Cancelled** | `--cancelled` | `#6B7280` | Auto-cancelled applications |
49	| Cancelled Soft | `--cancelled-soft` | `#F3F4F6` | Cancelled pill bg |
50	| Shadow | `--shadow` | `#0F172A` | Neubrutalist hard shadow (always ink, never tinted) |
51	
52	**Dark mode:** deferred for MVP. Neubrutalism translates cleanly (invert canvas → `#0B0B0F`, keep shadow as a white 1px border with a `rgba(0,0,0,0.4)` offset), but it doubles visual QA.
53	
54	### 1.3 Font Pairing
55	
56	**"Startup Bold"** — `Clash Display` for headings, `Satoshi` for body. Both via `expo-font` (Fontshare CDN or self-host). Fallbacks: `Outfit` for headings, `Rubik` for body.
57	
58	| Role | Font | Weight | Size (mobile) |
59	|---|---|---|---|
60	| Display (screen titles, campaign titles) | Clash Display | 600–700 | 28 / 22 |
61	| Heading | Clash Display | 600 | 20 |
62	| Subhead | Satoshi | 600 | 17 |
63	| Body | Satoshi | 500 | 15 |
64	| Caption | Satoshi | 500 | 13 |
65	| Micro (pills, chip labels) | Satoshi | 700 | 11 (uppercase, `letter-spacing: 0.08em`) |
66	| Monospace (URLs, handles @xxx) | JetBrains Mono | 500 | 14 |
67	
68	**Usage rules:**
69	- Display font is *only* for titles and hero metric numbers. Never stack two Clash Display elements in the same card.
70	- User-generated content (campaign descriptions) bumps to 16px.
71	- Line-height: 1.55 body, 1.15 display.
72	- Line-length: max 65 characters → content width caps at ~340px on a 390px screen.
73	
74	### 1.4 Motion Language (Reanimated)
75	
76	**Spring presets** (define in `animations/springs.ts`):
77	```
78	soft:    { damping: 20, stiffness: 180, mass: 1 }   // cards, chips
79	snappy:  { damping: 18, stiffness: 260, mass: 1 }   // tab switch, pill transitions
80	bouncy:  { damping: 12, stiffness: 220, mass: 1 }   // status change celebration
81	```
82	
83	**Timing presets:**
84	```
85	micro:  150ms  ease-out     // press feedback
86	short:  220ms  ease-out     // enter/exit
87	medium: 320ms  cubic-bezier(0.2, 0.8, 0.2, 1)  // screen transitions
88	```
89	
90	**Motion rules:**
91	1. **Screen transitions** — stack push uses native `slide_from_right` (expo-router default). Modals use `modal` presentation with a spring slide-up (snappy).
92	2. **Press feedback** — every pressable scales to 0.97 on `pressIn` (spring.soft), returns on `pressOut`. Use `Pressable` + `useAnimatedStyle` with `useSharedValue`. Never use `TouchableOpacity`.
93	3. **Status change (pending → approved/rejected)** — StatusPill morphs color with a 320ms color interpolation + one-shot bouncy scale (1 → 1.12 → 1). Approved gets confetti-lite: 8 small squares in primary/cta/success burst upward from the pill, fade over 800ms. Rejected gets a gentle left-right shake (3 cycles, ±4px, 120ms each). Both respect `prefers-reduced-motion`.
94	4. **Pull-to-refresh on metrics** — custom refresh control. Vertical "pull" reveals a Clash Display counter that rolls up (`withTiming` + numerical interpolation). On success, new value "slams" into place with bouncy spring and `✓` fades in. Delta shows `+1.2K` in success green for 1.5s.
95	5. **Card interactions (CampaignCard)** — on press, the card drops its hard shadow (translate `3px,3px` → `0,0`) and moves `translateX/Y(2px,2px)` to simulate a physical press. Spring.soft.
96	6. **Eligibility check animation** — tap "Apply" → criteria animate in a staggered list (each row fades + slides 8px from left, 60ms stagger). Passing conditions show a green check drawing in (SVG stroke-dashoffset, 280ms). Failing conditions shake once and fade to muted.
97	7. **Tab bar** — active tab icon morphs from outline to solid (two SVGs crossfaded, 180ms). Active indicator is a 4px bar under the icon (spring.snappy).
98	8. **FlatList entries** — on first mount, cards stagger in 40ms apart (translateY 16 → 0, opacity 0 → 1). Skip for subsequent renders.
99	
100	**Performance:** all worklets, `runOnUI` for imperative runs. No `Animated` API. Gestures via `react-native-gesture-handler` + Reanimated v3.

file.content:
# Marketify — UI/UX Design Document

*Mobile app for the creator marketing industry. Expo + React Native + Reanimated + Supabase.*

---

## 1. Design Language

### 1.1 Chosen Style

**Neubrutalism** as the primary language, with disciplined restraint for mobile comfort.

**Why it fits Marketify:**
- The marketplace/directory pattern (Vibrant & Block-based) is the top match; Neubrutalism is its most purposeful expression — bold borders, hard offset shadows, flat pop colors.
- The pattern rewards scannability — hard black outlines on CampaignCards make bounties feel like physical "cards on a board."
- WCAG AAA by construction (high contrast), Excellent performance — critical for mid-tier Android.
- Differentiated. TikTok is dark-glassmorphic, Instagram is quiet gradient, every campaign-management tool (Grin, Aspire) is corporate-blue SaaS. Neubrutalism carves out a clear creator-native identity.

**Disciplines to keep it mobile-friendly (not a desktop stunt):**
- Borders are 2px, not 4px.
- Shadows are 3px offset max, never 6–8px.
- Corner radius is 12px on cards, 999px on pills — not 0. Pure 0 feels brittle on touch.
- No intentionally "broken" layouts. Brutalism's anti-design aesthetic is skipped in favor of its craft elements only.

### 1.2 Color Palette

Base palette anchored on the **"Creator Economy Platform"** colors (pink + orange CTA), with semantic statuses added.

| Role | Token | Hex | Usage |
|---|---|---|---|
| Primary | `--primary` | `#EC4899` | Brand, active tab icon, primary surfaces, logo mark |
| Primary Soft | `--primary-soft` | `#FDF2F8` | Primary-tinted backgrounds, selected chip bg |
| Primary Deep | `--primary-deep` | `#831843` | Primary text on soft bg, pressed states |
| Accent (CTA) | `--cta` | `#F97316` | All primary action buttons, "Apply", "Submit", "Create Campaign" |
| Accent Deep | `--cta-deep` | `#9A3412` | CTA pressed state |
| Ink (text) | `--ink` | `#0F172A` | All body and heading text, hard borders |
| Ink-70 | `--ink-70` | `#475569` | Secondary text, captions |
| Ink-40 | `--ink-40` | `#94A3B8` | Tertiary / disabled text |
| Surface | `--surface` | `#FFFFFF` | Card bodies |
| Canvas | `--canvas` | `#FFF9F2` | App background (warm off-white — matches orange CTA undertone) |
| Hairline | `--hairline` | `#E5E7EB` | Dividers inside cards |
| **Success (approved)** | `--success` | `#16A34A` | Approved submissions, approved applications, eligible checks |
| Success Soft | `--success-soft` | `#DCFCE7` | Approved pill bg |
| **Danger (rejected)** | `--danger` | `#DC2626` | Rejected submissions, rejected applications |
| Danger Soft | `--danger-soft` | `#FEE2E2` | Rejected pill bg, ineligibility warning |
| **Warning (pending)** | `--warning` | `#F59E0B` | Pending status, waiting-for-review states |
| Warning Soft | `--warning-soft` | `#FEF3C7` | Pending pill bg |
| **Cancelled** | `--cancelled` | `#6B7280` | Auto-cancelled applications |
| Cancelled Soft | `--cancelled-soft` | `#F3F4F6` | Cancelled pill bg |
| Shadow | `--shadow` | `#0F172A` | Neubrutalist hard shadow (always ink, never tinted) |

**Dark mode:** deferred for MVP. Neubrutalism translates cleanly (invert canvas → `#0B0B0F`, keep shadow as a white 1px border with a `rgba(0,0,0,0.4)` offset), but it doubles visual QA.

### 1.3 Font Pairing

**"Startup Bold"** — `Clash Display` for headings, `Satoshi` for body. Both via `expo-font` (Fontshare CDN or self-host). Fallbacks: `Outfit` for headings, `Rubik` for body.

| Role | Font | Weight | Size (mobile) |
|---|---|---|---|
| Display (screen titles, campaign titles) | Clash Display | 600–700 | 28 / 22 |
| Heading | Clash Display | 600 | 20 |
| Subhead | Satoshi | 600 | 17 |
| Body | Satoshi | 500 | 15 |
| Caption | Satoshi | 500 | 13 |
| Micro (pills, chip labels) | Satoshi | 700 | 11 (uppercase, `letter-spacing: 0.08em`) |
| Monospace (URLs, handles @xxx) | JetBrains Mono | 500 | 14 |

**Usage rules:**
- Display font is *only* for titles and hero metric numbers. Never stack two Clash Display elements in the same card.
- User-generated content (campaign descriptions) bumps to 16px.
- Line-height: 1.55 body, 1.15 display.
- Line-length: max 65 characters → content width caps at ~340px on a 390px screen.

### 1.4 Motion Language (Reanimated)

**Spring presets** (define in `animations/springs.ts`):
```
soft:    { damping: 20, stiffness: 180, mass: 1 }   // cards, chips
snappy:  { damping: 18, stiffness: 260, mass: 1 }   // tab switch, pill transitions
bouncy:  { damping: 12, stiffness: 220, mass: 1 }   // status change celebration
```

**Timing presets:**
```
micro:  150ms  ease-out     // press feedback
short:  220ms  ease-out     // enter/exit
medium: 320ms  cubic-bezier(0.2, 0.8, 0.2, 1)  // screen transitions
```

**Motion rules:**
1. **Screen transitions** — stack push uses native `slide_from_right` (expo-router default). Modals use `modal` presentation with a spring slide-up (snappy).
2. **Press feedback** — every pressable scales to 0.97 on `pressIn` (spring.soft), returns on `pressOut`. Use `Pressable` + `useAnimatedStyle` with `useSharedValue`. Never use `TouchableOpacity`.
3. **Status change (pending → approved/rejected)** — StatusPill morphs color with a 320ms color interpolation + one-shot bouncy scale (1 → 1.12 → 1). Approved gets confetti-lite: 8 small squares in primary/cta/success burst upward from the pill, fade over 800ms. Rejected gets a gentle left-right shake (3 cycles, ±4px, 120ms each). Both respect `prefers-reduced-motion`.
4. **Pull-to-refresh on metrics** — custom refresh control. Vertical "pull" reveals a Clash Display counter that rolls up (`withTiming` + numerical interpolation). On success, new value "slams" into place with bouncy spring and `✓` fades in. Delta shows `+1.2K` in success green for 1.5s.
5. **Card interactions (CampaignCard)** — on press, the card drops its hard shadow (translate `3px,3px` → `0,0`) and moves `translateX/Y(2px,2px)` to simulate a physical press. Spring.soft.
6. **Eligibility check animation** — tap "Apply" → criteria animate in a staggered list (each row fades + slides 8px from left, 60ms stagger). Passing conditions show a green check drawing in (SVG stroke-dashoffset, 280ms). Failing conditions shake once and fade to muted.
7. **Tab bar** — active tab icon morphs from outline to solid (two SVGs crossfaded, 180ms). Active indicator is a 4px bar under the icon (spring.snappy).
8. **FlatList entries** — on first mount, cards stagger in 40ms apart (translateY 16 → 0, opacity 0 → 1). Skip for subsequent renders.

**Performance:** all worklets, `runOnUI` for imperative runs. No `Animated` API. Gestures via `react-native-gesture-handler` + Reanimated v3.

content.content:
# TikTok Scraper API Summary

## Actor ID/Slug
- **ID**: `clockworks~tiktok-scraper`

## API Endpoints
- **Run Actor**: `POST https://api.apify.com/v2/acts/clockworks~tiktok-scraper/runs?token=<YOUR_API_TOKEN>`
- **Run Synchronously**: `POST https://api.apify.com/v2/acts/clockworks~tiktok-scraper/run-sync-get-dataset-items?token=<YOUR_API_TOKEN>`

## Input Schema Fields
Based on the example provided:
- `hashtags` (array) - Target hashtags for scraping
- `resultsPerPage` (integer) - Results per page limit
- `maxFollowersPerProfile` (integer) - Maximum followers threshold
- `maxFollowingPerProfile` (integer) - Maximum following threshold
- `commentsPerPost` (integer) - Comments to extract per post
- `topLevelCommentsPerPost` (integer) - Top-level comments limit
- `maxRepliesPerComment` (integer) - Replies per comment limit
- `proxyCountryCode` (string) - Geographic proxy specification

## Output Dataset Fields
The documentation mentions the actor extracts:
- Profile data (followers, following counts, usernames)
- Video metadata (URLs, share counts, heart counts)
- Music-related data
- Hashtag information
- Creator/author details

**Note**: Complete input/output schema details weren't fully specified in the provided content.

input.prompt: Extract the full input schema JSON for this actor. List all input fields including profiles, profileScrapeSections, profileSorting, excludePinnedPosts, resultsPerPage. How do you scrape a single profile's videos sorted by newest, and what fields are returned (playCount, diggCount, videoMeta, etc.)?

content.content:
# TikTok Scraper Input Schema

## Key Input Fields

The scraper accepts these primary inputs:

- **profiles**: Array of usernames/IDs to scrape
- **resultsPerPage**: Number of videos per profile (1-1,000,000, default: 1)
- **profileScrapeSections**: Select sections like "videos" or "reposts"
- **profileSorting**: Options include "latest," "popular," or "oldest"
- **excludePinnedPosts**: Boolean to filter pinned content

## Scraping a Single Profile

To retrieve one profile's videos sorted newest-first:

1. Input the username in **profiles**
2. Set **profileSorting** to `"latest"`
3. Specify desired **resultsPerPage** count

## Output Fields

The schema documentation references extracted data including:

- Video engagement metrics (playCount, diggCount/hearts)
- User information (profiles, followers)
- **videoMeta**: Video metadata
- Music/sound details
- Timestamps and descriptions

The actual response structure is specified in the API reference documentation, which isn't fully detailed in the input schema page shown. For complete field documentation, consult the "API" tab in the actor's full documentation.

input.prompt: Extract the API input schema for this actor. What is the actor ID/slug? How do you scrape a single Instagram profile's follower count and recent reels/videos with view counts? Extract the resultsType options (posts, details, stories, reels), and the typical input for "details" type (which returns profile info including followerCount), and for "posts" type (which returns recent videos with videoPlayCount). Also include the sync and async endpoint URLs.

content.content:
# Instagram Scraper API Details

## Actor ID/Slug
`apify~instagram-scraper` (or `apify/instagram-scraper`)

## Available resultsType Options
Based on the documentation, the supported result types include:
- `posts` - Recent posts with metrics
- `details` - Profile information
- `stories` - Instagram stories
- `reels` - Video reels

## Single Profile Follower Count & Recent Reels

To retrieve a profile's follower metrics and recent videos, use:

**Input for "details" type:**
```json
{
  "resultsType": "details",
  "directUrls": ["https://www.instagram.com/USERNAME/"]
}
```
This returns "profile info including followerCount" per the documentation.

**Input for "posts" type:**
```json
{
  "resultsType": "posts",
  "directUrls": ["https://www.instagram.com/USERNAME/"],
  "resultsLimit": 10
}
```
This retrieves recent videos with `videoPlayCount` metrics.

## API Endpoints

**Asynchronous Run:**
```
POST https://api.apify.com/v2/acts/apify~instagram-scraper/runs?token=<YOUR_API_TOKEN>
```

**Synchronous Run (Returns Data):**
```
POST https://api.apify.com/v2/acts/apify~instagram-scraper/run-sync-get-dataset-items?token=<YOUR_API_TOKEN>
```

The synchronous endpoint supports both POST and GET methods for data retrieval.

input.prompt: Extract all input fields for the apify/instagram-scraper actor. Focus on: directUrls, username, resultsType (valid values), resultsLimit, addParentData, searchType, searchLimit. What's the shape of output for resultsType=details (fields: followersCount, followsCount, postsCount, etc.)? What's the shape of output for resultsType=posts with a reel (fields: videoPlayCount, videoViewCount, likesCount, commentsCount, type)?

content.content:
# Instagram Scraper Input Fields

## Key Input Parameters

**directUrls** (array, optional): "Add one or more Instagram URLs to scrape." Format must match content type (`/p/` for posts, `/reel/` for reels, `/username/` for profiles).

**resultsType** (string, optional): "Posts returns a feed of content. Profile, hashtag, or place details returns metadata about the page."
- Valid values: `posts`, `details`, `comments`, `reels`, `mentions`, `stories`
- Default: `posts`

**resultsLimit** (integer, optional): Constrains results per URL (minimum 1, maximum 50 for comments).

**searchType** (string, optional): Specifies search scope
- Valid values: `hashtag`, `profile`, `place`, `user`
- Default: `hashtag`

**searchLimit** (integer, optional): Number of search results (1-250).

**addParentData** (boolean, optional): Adds dataSource field labeling results as `profile` or `hashtag`. Default: false.

## Output Shapes

The document **does not specify** output field structures for:
- `resultsType=details` (follower counts, bio data, etc.)
- Reel-specific fields (videoPlayCount, videoViewCount, etc.)

Only input schema documentation is provided; output schema details are unavailable in the source material.

Based on the search results, I found information about the clockworks/tiktok-scraper output fields you're asking about:

The scraper outputs fields including `authorMeta.name`, `diggCount`, `shareCount`, `playCount`, `commentCount`, `collectCount`, and `videoMeta.duration`. Here are some concrete examples from the results:

**Example 1:**
For a video by "bruniela_", the output includes: "diggCount": 5344, "shareCount": 701, "playCount": 55700, "commentCount": 24, "collectCount": 291, "videoMeta.duration": 16

**Example 2:**
Another example shows: "authorMeta.name": "tom.cashman", "diggCount": 465100, "shareCount": 8397, "playCount": 3500000, "commentCount": 1072, "videoMeta.duration": 26

Regarding the `fans` field specifically, the authorMeta object includes fields like "fans": 30900000 (for example, Gordon Ramsay's follower count).

The scraper also captures additional metadata fields like `musicMeta` (music name, author, whether it's original), `createTimeISO`, and `webVideoUrl` for each video entry.

Based on the search results, I found information about the Apify Instagram Scraper's output fields, particularly related to follower counts and reel video play counts.

## Output Fields Overview

The Instagram Scraper extracts number of followers, URLs, bio, posts, likes, counts, related profiles, captions, and highlight reels. For reels specifically, the scraper can extract caption, timestamp, transcript, hashtags, mentions, tagged users, comments, likes, shares, views, duration, and downloaded video.

## Field Examples

The search results show that the scraper uses field names like `followersCount`. A sample output includes fields such as "url", "username", "profilePicture", "postsCount", "followersCount", "followsCount", "fullName", "isBusinessAccount", "threads", and "bio".

For reel-specific metrics, the Instagram Hashtag Scraper scrapes caption, author username, likes, comments count, play count, video URL, hashtags, and posting date. Additionally, the Instagram Reel Scraper provides engagement metrics including likes, comments, shares, video plays, views, and duration.

Exact field names are defined by the Actor's JSON schema—always verify on the Input tab.

For the most detailed and current JSON schema examples, you may want to visit the official Apify Actor page directly at apify.com/apify/instagram-scraper, where the Input and Output schema documentation is typically available.

content.content:
146	## 3. Apify Integration (context7-verified)
147	
148	**Source:** `/apify/apify-client-js` — [`apify-client` NPM package docs](https://github.com/apify/apify-client-js/blob/master/docs/index.md).
149	
150	### 3a. Actor Catalog
151	
152	| Purpose | Apify Actor | Input | Output (fields we care about) |
153	|---|---|---|---|
154	| TikTok profile + last N videos (single call) | `clockworks/tiktok-profile-scraper` *(verify exact slug at impl)* | `{ profiles: ["<handle>"], resultsPerPage: 10 }` | `authorMeta.fans` (followers), `authorMeta.following`, `authorMeta.heart` (total likes), `authorMeta.video` (post count), `playCount` per video |
155	| Instagram follower count (lightweight) | `apify/instagram-follower-count-scraper` *(verify slug)* | `{ usernames: ["<handle>"] }` | `followersCount`, `followsCount`, `postsCount` |
156	| Instagram last 10 reels + view counts | `apify/instagram-reel-scraper` *(verify slug)* | `{ username: "<handle>", resultsLimit: 10 }` | `videoViewCount` (or `playsCount`), `likesCount`, `commentsCount`, `timestamp` per reel |
157	
158	**Note:** exact actor slugs to be confirmed at implementation time; the Apify marketplace catalog evolves. The shape of the integration (client → actor run → dataset → parse → update snapshot) is the same regardless.
159	
160	### 3b. Client Pattern
161	
162	```ts
163	import { ApifyClient } from 'apify-client';
164	const apify = new ApifyClient({ token: Deno.env.get('APIFY_TOKEN')! });
165	
166	// synchronous short-run (used on first signup — user is waiting)
167	const { defaultDatasetId } = await apify
168	  .actor('clockworks/tiktok-profile-scraper')
169	  .call({ profiles: [handle], resultsPerPage: 10 }, { waitSecs: 60 });
170	const { items } = await apify.dataset(defaultDatasetId).listItems();
171	
172	// async run (used for cron refresh — don't block)
173	const run = await apify
174	  .actor('clockworks/tiktok-profile-scraper')
175	  .start({ profiles: [handle], resultsPerPage: 10 }, {
176	    webhooks: [{
177	      eventTypes: ['ACTOR.RUN.SUCCEEDED', 'ACTOR.RUN.FAILED'],
178	      requestUrl: `${SUPABASE_URL}/functions/v1/webhooks-apify`,
179	      payloadTemplate: JSON.stringify({ social_link_id: '<id>', run_id: '{{runId}}', status: '{{status}}', dataset_id: '{{defaultDatasetId}}' }),
180	    }],
181	  });
182	```
183	
184	### 3c. Metric Derivation
185	
186	- **TikTok `follower_count`** = `items[0].authorMeta.fans` (or equivalent, verify on first sample).
187	- **TikTok `avg_views_last_10`** = mean of `playCount` across up to 10 video items from the actor's dataset.
188	- **TikTok `total_likes`**, `video_count`, `is_verified` = respective fields on `authorMeta`.
189	- **IG `follower_count`** = `items[0].followersCount` from the follower-count scraper.
190	- **IG `avg_views_last_10`** = mean of `videoViewCount` (fallback `playsCount`) across reel-scraper results.
191	- **IG `media_count`** = `postsCount` from the follower-count scraper.
192	
193	A single IG refresh = **two actor runs** (follower scraper + reel scraper). TikTok = one. Budget accordingly.
194	
195	### 3d. Cost & Rate Management
196	
197	- Every actor run costs Apify credits (pricing varies by actor; typical TikTok profile scrape ≈ $0.01–0.05, IG reel scrape similar). Budget per-user per-month in product analytics.
198	- **Hard throttle:** `metrics/refresh` server-side limit 1 per 6h per `(user, platform)`. `429` before we even call Apify.
199	- **Daily cron** picks up to N stale links per day, capped at a configurable budget.
200	- Set Apify run timeout to 120s; if actor hangs, we fail-close the snapshot (`status=failed`) and retry on next cron window.
201	
202	### 3e. Webhook Security
203	
204	- Apify webhooks carry a shared secret in header (`Apify-Webhook-Secret` or HMAC — verify in impl). Edge Function rejects requests without it.
205	- Webhook endpoint is idempotent: if the same `run_id` is delivered twice, we no-op the second.
206	
207	### 3f. Rendering Submitted Videos
208	
209	Unchanged from v0.1 — oEmbed for both:
210	- TikTok: `GET https://www.tiktok.com/oembed?url=<share_url>` → `html`, `thumbnail_url`.

## 3. Apify Integration (context7-verified)

**Source:** `/apify/apify-client-js` — [`apify-client` NPM package docs](https://github.com/apify/apify-client-js/blob/master/docs/index.md).

### 3a. Actor Catalog

| Purpose | Apify Actor | Input | Output (fields we care about) |
|---|---|---|---|
| TikTok profile + last N videos (single call) | `clockworks/tiktok-profile-scraper` *(verify exact slug at impl)* | `{ profiles: ["<handle>"], resultsPerPage: 10 }` | `authorMeta.fans` (followers), `authorMeta.following`, `authorMeta.heart` (total likes), `authorMeta.video` (post count), `playCount` per video |
| Instagram follower count (lightweight) | `apify/instagram-follower-count-scraper` *(verify slug)* | `{ usernames: ["<handle>"] }` | `followersCount`, `followsCount`, `postsCount` |
| Instagram last 10 reels + view counts | `apify/instagram-reel-scraper` *(verify slug)* | `{ username: "<handle>", resultsLimit: 10 }` | `videoViewCount` (or `playsCount`), `likesCount`, `commentsCount`, `timestamp` per reel |

**Note:** exact actor slugs to be confirmed at implementation time; the Apify marketplace catalog evolves. The shape of the integration (client → actor run → dataset → parse → update snapshot) is the same regardless.

### 3b. Client Pattern

```ts
import { ApifyClient } from 'apify-client';
const apify = new ApifyClient({ token: Deno.env.get('APIFY_TOKEN')! });

// synchronous short-run (used on first signup — user is waiting)
const { defaultDatasetId } = await apify
  .actor('clockworks/tiktok-profile-scraper')
  .call({ profiles: [handle], resultsPerPage: 10 }, { waitSecs: 60 });
const { items } = await apify.dataset(defaultDatasetId).listItems();

// async run (used for cron refresh — don't block)
const run = await apify
  .actor('clockworks/tiktok-profile-scraper')
  .start({ profiles: [handle], resultsPerPage: 10 }, {
    webhooks: [{
      eventTypes: ['ACTOR.RUN.SUCCEEDED', 'ACTOR.RUN.FAILED'],
      requestUrl: `${SUPABASE_URL}/functions/v1/webhooks-apify`,
      payloadTemplate: JSON.stringify({ social_link_id: '<id>', run_id: '{{runId}}', status: '{{status}}', dataset_id: '{{defaultDatasetId}}' }),
    }],
  });
```

### 3c. Metric Derivation

- **TikTok `follower_count`** = `items[0].authorMeta.fans` (or equivalent, verify on first sample).
- **TikTok `avg_views_last_10`** = mean of `playCount` across up to 10 video items from the actor's dataset.
- **TikTok `total_likes`**, `video_count`, `is_verified` = respective fields on `authorMeta`.
- **IG `follower_count`** = `items[0].followersCount` from the follower-count scraper.
- **IG `avg_views_last_10`** = mean of `videoViewCount` (fallback `playsCount`) across reel-scraper results.
- **IG `media_count`** = `postsCount` from the follower-count scraper.

A single IG refresh = **two actor runs** (follower scraper + reel scraper). TikTok = one. Budget accordingly.

### 3d. Cost & Rate Management

- Every actor run costs Apify credits (pricing varies by actor; typical TikTok profile scrape ≈ $0.01–0.05, IG reel scrape similar). Budget per-user per-month in product analytics.
- **Hard throttle:** `metrics/refresh` server-side limit 1 per 6h per `(user, platform)`. `429` before we even call Apify.
- **Daily cron** picks up to N stale links per day, capped at a configurable budget.
- Set Apify run timeout to 120s; if actor hangs, we fail-close the snapshot (`status=failed`) and retry on next cron window.

### 3e. Webhook Security

- Apify webhooks carry a shared secret in header (`Apify-Webhook-Secret` or HMAC — verify in impl). Edge Function rejects requests without it.
- Webhook endpoint is idempotent: if the same `run_id` is delivered twice, we no-op the second.

### 3f. Rendering Submitted Videos

Unchanged from v0.1 — oEmbed for both:
- TikTok: `GET https://www.tiktok.com/oembed?url=<share_url>` → `html`, `thumbnail_url`.

## 3. Apify Integration

**Sources:**
- `apify-client` NPM package — [github.com/apify/apify-client-js](https://github.com/apify/apify-client-js)
- TikTok Scraper actor page — [apify.com/clockworks/tiktok-scraper](https://apify.com/clockworks/tiktok-scraper)
- Instagram Scraper actor page — [apify.com/apify/instagram-scraper](https://apify.com/apify/instagram-scraper)

### 3a. Actor Catalog (v1)

We use **two actors only**, consolidating all metric fetches:

| Platform | Actor Slug | REST slug (with `~`) | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| TikTok | `clockworks/tiktok-scraper` | `clockworks~tiktok-scraper` | Profile + last N videos in a single run |
| Instagram | `apify/instagram-scraper` | `apify~instagram-scraper` | Profile details **and** posts/reels — two runs per refresh (different `resultsType`) |

**Actor endpoints** (both actors expose the same surface):

- Sync (blocking, returns dataset items): `POST https://api.apify.com/v2/acts/<slug>/run-sync-get-dataset-items?token=<APIFY_TOKEN>`
- Async (returns run, webhook-completed): `POST https://api.apify.com/v2/acts/<slug>/runs?token=<APIFY_TOKEN>`

In practice we use the `apify-client` JS SDK (`.actor(slug).call()` for sync, `.actor(slug).start()` for async) — it wraps both endpoints.

### 3b. TikTok — `clockworks/tiktok-scraper`

**Input (profile scrape):**
```json
{
  "profiles": ["<handle>"],
  "resultsPerPage": 10,
  "profileScrapeSections": ["videos"],
  "profileSorting": "latest",
  "excludePinnedPosts": true,
  "shouldDownloadVideos": false,
  "shouldDownloadCovers": false,
  "shouldDownloadSubtitles": false
}
```

Notes:
- `profileSorting: "latest"` returns newest videos first — required so `avg_views_last_10` is the **recent** trailing window, not all-time popular.
- `excludePinnedPosts: true` avoids skewing the average with a single mega-viral pinned post.
- Download flags default to `true` on Apify; we override to `false` to cut run cost (we never need the media bytes).

**Output** — one row per video, with the author profile **denormalized onto every row**. Relevant fields:

| Path | Meaning | Maps to |
|---|---|---|
| `authorMeta.name` | handle | verification vs. input |
| `authorMeta.fans` | follower count | `follower_count` |
| `authorMeta.following` | following count | (stored for profile, not eligibility) |
| `authorMeta.heart` | total lifetime likes across videos | (stored) |
| `authorMeta.video` | total post count | `video_count` |
| `authorMeta.verified` | blue check | `is_verified` |
| `playCount` | **view count for this video** | contributes to `avg_views_last_10` |
| `diggCount` | like count for this video | (stored) |
| `shareCount`, `commentCount`, `collectCount` | engagement | (stored) |
| `videoMeta.duration` | seconds | (stored) |
| `createTimeISO`, `webVideoUrl` | timestamps / permalink | (stored) |

**Metric derivation (TikTok):**
- `follower_count = items[0].authorMeta.fans`
- `avg_views_last_10 = mean(items[i].playCount for i in 0..min(10, len(items)))`
- `video_count = items[0].authorMeta.video`

**One run per refresh.**

### 3c. Instagram — `apify/instagram-scraper`

The Instagram actor is a **multi-mode scraper**; what it returns depends on `resultsType`. For our use case we need two modes per refresh:

**Run 1 — profile details (follower count):**
```json
{
  "directUrls": ["https://www.instagram.com/<handle>/"],
  "resultsType": "details",
  "resultsLimit": 1,
  "addParentData": false
}
```

Output (one row, keyed on username):

| Field | Meaning | Maps to |
|---|---|---|
| `username` | handle | verification |
| `followersCount` | follower count | `follower_count` |
| `followsCount` | following count | (stored) |
| `postsCount` | total posts | `media_count` |
| `fullName`, `biography`, `profilePicUrl` | profile chrome | (stored) |
| `isBusinessAccount` | is Business/Creator | (stored; informational only — **not required**) |
| `verified` | blue check | `is_verified` |

**Run 2 — recent reels (view counts):**
```json
{
  "directUrls": ["https://www.instagram.com/<handle>/"],
  "resultsType": "posts",
  "resultsLimit": 10,
  "addParentData": false
}
```

`resultsType: "posts"` returns the profile's recent feed (posts + reels interleaved). We filter client-side to `type === "Video"` entries and use their view counts.

Output (one row per post) — relevant fields:

| Field | Meaning | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `type` | `"Image"` \| `"Video"` \| `"Sidecar"` | filter to `"Video"` |
| `videoPlayCount` | Instagram's "plays" metric | primary signal |
| `videoViewCount` | view metric (sometimes null on older posts) | fallback |
| `likesCount`, `commentsCount` | engagement | (stored) |
| `videoDuration` | seconds | (stored) |
| `caption`, `hashtags`, `mentions`, `timestamp`, `url`, `shortCode` | post meta | (stored) |

**Metric derivation (Instagram):**
- `follower_count = details[0].followersCount`
- `media_count = details[0].postsCount`
- `avg_views_last_10 = mean(p.videoPlayCount ?? p.videoViewCount for p in posts where p.type === "Video")` — skip the profile entirely if there are fewer than 1 video posts (set `avg_views_last_10 = null`, surface in UI as "Not enough video posts to compute").

**Alternative:** `resultsType: "reels"` is documented for this actor and returns reels directly. We default to `"posts"` because (a) a profile that posts images + reels still yields reel data, (b) in empirical testing `"reels"` sometimes returns empty for newer accounts. If `"posts"` yields 0 videos across 10 results, fall back to `"reels"` for one additional run.

**Two runs per IG refresh.** Budget: 1 run = TikTok refresh, 2 runs = IG refresh.

### 3d. Client Pattern

```ts
import { ApifyClient } from 'apify-client';
const apify = new ApifyClient({ token: Deno.env.get('APIFY_TOKEN')! });

// --- synchronous short-run (used on first signup — user is waiting) ---
const tiktok = await apify
  .actor('clockworks/tiktok-scraper')
  .call(
    {
      profiles: [handle],
      resultsPerPage: 10,
      profileScrapeSections: ['videos'],
      profileSorting: 'latest',
      excludePinnedPosts: true,
      shouldDownloadVideos: false,
      shouldDownloadCovers: false,
    },
    { waitSecs: 60 },
  );
const { items: tiktokItems } = await apify.dataset(tiktok.defaultDatasetId).listItems();

// --- async run with webhook (used for cron refresh and initial IG scrapes) ---
const run = await apify
  .actor('apify/instagram-scraper')
  .start(
    {
      directUrls: [`https://www.instagram.com/${handle}/`],
      resultsType: 'details',
      resultsLimit: 1,
    },
    {
      webhooks: [{
        eventTypes: ['ACTOR.RUN.SUCCEEDED', 'ACTOR.RUN.FAILED', 'ACTOR.RUN.TIMED_OUT', 'ACTOR.RUN.ABORTED'],
        requestUrl: `${SUPABASE_URL}/functions/v1/webhooks-apify`,
        payloadTemplate: JSON.stringify({
          social_link_id: '<id>',
          scrape_mode: 'ig_details',       // or 'ig_posts' | 'tiktok_profile'
          run_id: '{{resource.id}}',
          status: '{{resource.status}}',
          dataset_id: '{{resource.defaultDatasetId}}',
        }),
        headersTemplate: JSON.stringify({
          'X-Apify-Webhook-Secret': Deno.env.get('APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET')!,
        }),
      }],
    },
  );
```

**Signup flow (user waiting):** Use sync `.call(input, { waitSecs: 60 })` for the *first* scrape per handle. If the actor finishes inside 60s, populate the snapshot inline; if it doesn't, the `waitSecs` limit returns a `RUNNING` run and the webhook completes it later. UI shows "Fetching your metrics..." until either returns.

**Cron refresh / manual refresh:** Always async (`.start()` with webhook). The refresh endpoint queues work and returns `202 Accepted` immediately.

### 3e. Metric Derivation — Summary

| Denormalized field on `creator_profiles` | TikTok source | Instagram source |
|---|---|---|
| `tiktok_follower_count` | `authorMeta.fans` | — |
| `tiktok_avg_views_last_10` | `mean(playCount[:10])` | — |
| `tiktok_video_count` | `authorMeta.video` | — |
| `tiktok_is_verified` | `authorMeta.verified` | — |
| `ig_follower_count` | — | `details[0].followersCount` |
| `ig_avg_views_last_10` | — | `mean(videoPlayCount ?? videoViewCount)` across `type === "Video"` posts |
| `ig_media_count` | — | `details[0].postsCount` |
| `ig_is_verified` | — | `details[0].verified` |

Stored fully in `metric_snapshots.raw_payload` (jsonb) for forensics and future backfills — if we later need `diggCount` trends we don't need to re-scrape.

### 3f. Cost & Rate Management

- Every actor run costs Apify credits. Published pricing at time of writing: TikTok Scraper ≈ $0.40 / 1k videos (so ~$0.004 per 10-video run), Instagram Scraper ≈ $2.30 / 1k results. Budget conservatively at ~$0.01 per TikTok refresh and ~$0.02 per IG refresh (details + 10 posts).
- **Hard throttle:** `metrics/refresh` server-side limit 1 per 6h per `(user, platform)`. `429` before we even call Apify.
- **Daily cron** picks up to N stale links per day, capped at a configurable `APIFY_DAILY_BUDGET_USD`.
- Set Apify run timeout (via `timeoutSecs` input to `.start()`) to 120s; if actor hangs, we fail-close the snapshot (`status=failed`) and retry on next cron window.
- Memory: default `memoryMbytes` is fine for both actors (1024 MB); don't override.

### 3g. Webhook Security

- We attach `X-Apify-Webhook-Secret: <random token>` via `headersTemplate` on every webhook registration. Edge Function rejects requests without a matching secret (constant-time compare).
- Webhook endpoint is idempotent: `metric_snapshots.apify_run_id` has a partial unique index, so redelivery of the same `run_id` no-ops on the second write.
- Webhook event types registered: `ACTOR.RUN.SUCCEEDED`, `ACTOR.RUN.FAILED`, `ACTOR.RUN.TIMED_OUT`, `ACTOR.RUN.ABORTED`. All four resolve the snapshot (either `fresh` or `failed`).
