content.text: Cleaning up resolved gap/question artifacts. Starting with tech-architecture.md.

content.content:
1	# Marketify — Technical Architecture Document (v0.2)
2	
3	**Scope:** Backend, data, APIs, workflows. UI/UX is separate (see `design.md`).
4	**Stack:** Expo + React Native + Reanimated + Supabase (Postgres, Storage, Edge Functions, Realtime) + **Apify** (two actors: `clockworks/tiktok-scraper`, `apify/instagram-scraper`).
5	**Posture:** Opinionated. Where the PRD is silent, a default is chosen with justification.
6	**Changelog from v0.1:** replaced TikTok/IG OAuth with Apify scraping per resolved Q1; removed listing/application auto-expiration per resolved Q5; all other Q2–Q7 answers folded in.
7	
8	---
9	
10	## 1. PRD Gap Analysis (resolved)
11	
12	### 1.1 Identity & Auth
13	
14	| # | Gap | Decision (resolved) |
15	|---|---|---|
16	| 1 | How to get real follower/view counts without OAuth | **Apify scrapers.** Creator signs up with role + username + TikTok handle and/or IG handle. Server kicks an Apify actor to fetch metrics asynchronously. No OAuth, no passwords. Handle ownership is unverified in MVP (matches original PRD). |
17	| 2 | Username uniqueness | `username` is the unique display identifier (citext). `(platform, handle)` is also unique across `social_links` — first creator to register a handle owns it. Collisions surface with a "handle already registered" error; we do not verify ownership MVP. |
18	| 3 | Creator + lister on same account | **One role per account.** |
19	| 4 | Lister identity | Self-declared: username + email + org name. KYC is post-MVP. |
20	
21	### 1.2 Listing Lifecycle
22	
23	| # | Gap | Decision |
24	|---|---|---|
25	| 5 | Listing states | `draft → active → (paused \| closed) → archived`. `expired` state removed from v1 (Q5: no auto-expire). |
26	| 6 | Listings expire automatically | **No auto-expire in v1.** `end_date` column retained for future, unused by cron. Listers close manually. |
27	| 7 | Submission cap per campaign | `max_submissions` nullable. In v1 listers must manually close; auto-close trigger deferred to v1.1. |
28	| 8 | One active application per (creator, listing) | Partial unique index. |
29	| 9 | Which edits cascade-cancel pending applications | **All versioned fields:** price, pre-conditions, post-conditions, sample videos, max_submissions. Title/description edits do NOT cascade. |
30	| 10 | Post-condition edits cascade | **Yes** (same rule as #9). Confirmed in Q3. |
31	
32	### 1.3 Applications & Submissions
33	
34	| # | Gap | Decision |
35	|---|---|---|
36	| 11 | Creator withdraws | `withdrawn` state from `pending`. |
37	| 12 | Metrics drop between apply and approve | **Re-check at approve-time.** If now-ineligible, lister receives `409 INELIGIBLE_NOW` with `override_allowed=true`. No re-check at submit-time. |
38	| 13 | Multiple videos per submission | 1..N video URLs per submission. `min_videos` post-condition (default 1). |
39	| 14 | Revision request | **Cut from v1** (Q4). Binary approve/reject only. Column reserved for v1.1. |

# Marketify — Technical Architecture Document (v0.2)

**Scope:** Backend, data, APIs, workflows. UI/UX is separate (see `design.md`).
**Stack:** Expo + React Native + Reanimated + Supabase (Postgres, Storage, Edge Functions, Realtime) + **Apify** (two actors: `clockworks/tiktok-scraper`, `apify/instagram-scraper`).
**Posture:** Opinionated. Where the PRD is silent, a default is chosen with justification.
**Changelog from v0.1:** replaced TikTok/IG OAuth with Apify scraping per resolved Q1; removed listing/application auto-expiration per resolved Q5; all other Q2–Q7 answers folded in.

---

## 1. PRD Gap Analysis (resolved)

### 1.1 Identity & Auth

| # | Gap | Decision (resolved) |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | How to get real follower/view counts without OAuth | **Apify scrapers.** Creator signs up with role + username + TikTok handle and/or IG handle. Server kicks an Apify actor to fetch metrics asynchronously. No OAuth, no passwords. Handle ownership is unverified in MVP (matches original PRD). |
| 2 | Username uniqueness | `username` is the unique display identifier (citext). `(platform, handle)` is also unique across `social_links` — first creator to register a handle owns it. Collisions surface with a "handle already registered" error; we do not verify ownership MVP. |
| 3 | Creator + lister on same account | **One role per account.** |
| 4 | Lister identity | Self-declared: username + email + org name. KYC is post-MVP. |

### 1.2 Listing Lifecycle

| # | Gap | Decision |
|---|---|---|
| 5 | Listing states | `draft → active → (paused \| closed) → archived`. `expired` state removed from v1 (Q5: no auto-expire). |
| 6 | Listings expire automatically | **No auto-expire in v1.** `end_date` column retained for future, unused by cron. Listers close manually. |
| 7 | Submission cap per campaign | `max_submissions` nullable. In v1 listers must manually close; auto-close trigger deferred to v1.1. |
| 8 | One active application per (creator, listing) | Partial unique index. |
| 9 | Which edits cascade-cancel pending applications | **All versioned fields:** price, pre-conditions, post-conditions, sample videos, max_submissions. Title/description edits do NOT cascade. |
| 10 | Post-condition edits cascade | **Yes** (same rule as #9). Confirmed in Q3. |

### 1.3 Applications & Submissions

| # | Gap | Decision |
|---|---|---|
| 11 | Creator withdraws | `withdrawn` state from `pending`. |
| 12 | Metrics drop between apply and approve | **Re-check at approve-time.** If now-ineligible, lister receives `409 INELIGIBLE_NOW` with `override_allowed=true`. No re-check at submit-time. |
| 13 | Multiple videos per submission | 1..N video URLs per submission. `min_videos` post-condition (default 1). |
| 14 | Revision request | **Cut from v1** (Q4). Binary approve/reject only. Column reserved for v1.1. |
| 15 | Reapplication | After `rejected` / `cancelled_listing_edit`: allowed if listing still active. After `withdrawn`: allowed immediately. |
| 16 | Disputes | Append-only `events` table; support email only in MVP. |
| 17 | Expiration of pending apps/submissions | **No auto-expire in v1** (Q5). State machine keeps `expired` state but no cron writes to it. |

### 1.4 Money

input.new_string:
# Marketify — Technical Architecture Document

**Scope:** Backend, data, APIs, workflows. UI/UX is separate (see `design.md`).
**Stack:** Expo + React Native + Reanimated + Supabase (Postgres, Storage, Edge Functions, Realtime) + **Apify** (two actors: `clockworks/tiktok-scraper`, `apify/instagram-scraper`).
**Posture:** Opinionated. Where the PRD is silent, a default is chosen with justification.

---

## 1. Design Decisions

The table below captures every non-obvious architectural choice. Each row is a committed default that the rest of the document relies on — if you need to revisit one, expect ripple effects in §4 (schema), §5 (edge functions), and `design.md` (UX).

### 1.1 Identity & Auth

| Area | Decision |
|---|---|
| Metric collection | **Apify scrapers, no OAuth.** Creator signs up with role + username + TikTok handle and/or IG handle. Server kicks an Apify actor to fetch metrics asynchronously. Handle ownership is unverified in MVP. |
| Username uniqueness | `username` is the unique display identifier (citext). `(platform, handle)` is also unique across `social_links` — first creator to register a handle owns it. Collisions surface with a "handle already registered" error; we do not verify ownership. |
| Creator + lister on same account | One role per account. Dual-role users make two accounts. |
| Lister identity | Self-declared: username + email + org name. KYC is post-MVP. |
| Handle impersonation | No ownership verification in MVP — first registrant wins. Impersonation is a manual-report problem; accept until it becomes real abuse. |

### 1.2 Listing Lifecycle

| Area | Decision |
|---|---|
| States | `draft → active → (paused \| closed) → archived`. No `expired` state. |
| Auto-expiration | None. `end_date` column retained for v1.1; listers close listings manually. |
| Submission cap | `max_submissions` nullable. Listers close manually when they're done — no auto-close trigger in v1. |
| Application uniqueness | One active application per `(creator, listing)`. Partial unique index. |
| Cascade-on-edit | Editing any versioned field — price, pre-conditions, post-conditions, sample videos, max_submissions — bumps a version and cascade-cancels pending applications with `cancelled_listing_edit`. Title/description edits do NOT cascade. |

### 1.3 Applications & Submissions

| Area | Decision |
|---|---|
| Withdraw | Creator can withdraw from `pending` → `withdrawn`. |
| Eligibility drift between apply and approve | Re-check at approve-time. If now-ineligible, lister receives `409 INELIGIBLE_NOW` with `override_allowed=true`. No re-check at submit-time. |
| Videos per submission | 1..N video URLs per submission. `min_videos` post-condition (default 1). |
| Revision request | Binary approve/reject only. Column reserved for v1.1. |
| Reapplication | After `rejected` / `cancelled_listing_edit`: allowed if listing still active. After `withdrawn`: allowed immediately. |
| Submission reuse | A single video URL may be submitted to multiple campaigns. Surfaced to the lister via `ReuseBadge` (design §4.6) but not blocked. |
| Disputes | Append-only `events` table; support email only in MVP. |
| Pending apps/submissions expiry | No auto-expire. Rows stay in-inbox until explicitly acted on. |

### 1.4 Money

| Area | Decision |
|---|---|
| Payments | No real money in MVP. `price` is display-only. Off-platform handshake. `payment_status` column stubbed for v2. |

### 1.5 Notifications

| Area | Decision |
|---|---|
| Events | Application approved/rejected/cancelled; submission approved/rejected; listing version changed; metrics refresh failed. |
| Channels | In-app inbox + Supabase Realtime. Push deferred to v1.1. |
| Transport | Realtime on `applications`, `submissions`, `notifications`. Poll fallback on app resume. |

### 1.6 Metrics / Apify

| Area | Decision |
|---|---|
| Rate limits | Apify costs real money per scrape. Server-side throttle: manual refresh once per 6h per `(user, platform)`. Background refresh daily via `pg_cron` for all linked handles with `fetched_at < now() - 24h`. |
| Scrape failures | `metric_snapshots.status = fresh \| stale \| refreshing \| failed`. "Outdated" badge if `fetched_at > 7d`. After 3 consecutive failed scrapes, `social_link.status = failed_fetch`, prompt creator to re-enter handle. |
| Video integrity | Validate via TikTok/IG oEmbed at submission time (`422` if 404). Nightly sweep re-validates `approved` submissions — flags but doesn't change state. |
| Post-submission performance | Out of MVP. Later: an Apify actor per submitted video to pull current view count. |

### 1.7 Security / Abuse

| Area | Decision |
|---|---|
| Spam/illegal listings | `report_listing` button → admin table. Admin reviews via Supabase Studio. |
| Submission cross-posting | Flag via `ReuseBadge`, don't hard-block. |

|---|---|
| Withdraw | Creator can withdraw from `pending` → `withdrawn`. |
| Eligibility drift between apply and approve | Re-check at approve-time. If now-ineligible, lister receives `409 INELIGIBLE_NOW` with `override_allowed=true`. No re-check at submit-time. |
| Videos per submission | 1..N video URLs per submission. `min_videos` post-condition (default 1). |
| Revision request | Binary approve/reject only. Column reserved for v1.1. |
| Reapplication | After `rejected` / `cancelled_listing_edit`: allowed if listing still active. After `withdrawn`: allowed immediately. |
| Submission reuse | A single video URL may be submitted to multiple campaigns. Surfaced to the lister via `ReuseBadge` (design §4.6) but not blocked. |
| Disputes | Append-only `events` table; support email only in MVP. |
| Pending apps/submissions expiry | No auto-expire. Rows stay in-inbox until explicitly acted on. |

### 1.4 Money

| Area | Decision |
|---|---|
| Payments | No real money in MVP. `price` is display-only. Off-platform handshake. `payment_status` column stubbed for v2. |

### 1.5 Notifications

| Area | Decision |
|---|---|
| Events | Application approved/rejected/cancelled; submission approved/rejected; listing version changed; metrics refresh failed. |
| Channels | In-app inbox + Supabase Realtime. Push deferred to v1.1. |
| Transport | Realtime on `applications`, `submissions`, `notifications`. Poll fallback on app resume. |

### 1.6 Metrics / Apify

| Area | Decision |
|---|---|
| Rate limits | Apify costs real money per scrape. Server-side throttle: manual refresh once per 6h per `(user, platform)`. Background refresh daily via `pg_cron` for all linked handles with `fetched_at < now() - 24h`. |
| Scrape failures | `metric_snapshots.status = fresh \| stale \| refreshing \| failed`. "Outdated" badge if `fetched_at > 7d`. After 3 consecutive failed scrapes, `social_link.status = failed_fetch`, prompt creator to re-enter handle. |
| Video integrity | Validate via TikTok/IG oEmbed at submission time (`422` if 404). Nightly sweep re-validates `approved` submissions — flags but doesn't change state. |
| Post-submission performance | Out of MVP. Later: an Apify actor per submitted video to pull current view count. |

### 1.7 Security / Abuse

| Area | Decision |
|---|---|
| Spam/illegal listings | `report_listing` button → admin table. Admin reviews via Supabase Studio. |
| Submission cross-posting | Flag via `ReuseBadge`, don't hard-block. |

lines: **Scope:** Backend, data, APIs, workflows. UI/UX is separate (see `design.md`).
**Stack:** Expo + React Native + Reanimated + Supabase (Postgres, Storage, Edge Functions, Realtime) + **Apify** (two actors: `clockworks/tiktok-scraper`, `apify/instagram-scraper`).
**Posture:** Opinionated. Where the PRD is silent, a default is chosen with justification.
-**Changelog from v0.1:** replaced TikTok/IG OAuth with Apify scraping per resolved Q1; removed listing/application auto-expiration per resolved Q5; all other Q2–Q7 answers folded in.

+The table below captures every non-obvious architectural choice. Each row is a committed default that the rest of the document relies on — if you need to revisit one, expect ripple effects in §4 (schema), §5 (edge functions), and `design.md` (UX).

-| 1 | How to get real follower/view counts without OAuth | **Apify scrapers.** Creator signs up with role + username + TikTok handle and/or IG handle. Server kicks an Apify actor to fetch metrics asynchronously. No OAuth, no passwords. Handle ownership is unverified in MVP (matches original PRD). |

-| 2 | Username uniqueness | `username` is the unique display identifier (citext). `(platform, handle)` is also unique across `social_links` — first creator to register a handle owns it. Collisions surface with a "handle already registered" error; we do not verify ownership MVP. |
-| 3 | Creator + lister on same account | **One role per account.** |
-| 4 | Lister identity | Self-declared: username + email + org name. KYC is post-MVP. |

+| Metric collection | **Apify scrapers, no OAuth.** Creator signs up with role + username + TikTok handle and/or IG handle. Server kicks an Apify actor to fetch metrics asynchronously. Handle ownership is unverified in MVP. |

+| Username uniqueness | `username` is the unique display identifier (citext). `(platform, handle)` is also unique across `social_links` — first creator to register a handle owns it. Collisions surface with a "handle already registered" error; we do not verify ownership. |
+| Creator + lister on same account | One role per account. Dual-role users make two accounts. |
+| Lister identity | Self-declared: username + email + org name. KYC is post-MVP. |
+| Handle impersonation | No ownership verification in MVP — first registrant wins. Impersonation is a manual-report problem; accept until it becomes real abuse. |
-| 5 | Listing states | `draft → active → (paused \| closed) → archived`. `expired` state removed from v1 (Q5: no auto-expire). |
-| 6 | Listings expire automatically | **No auto-expire in v1.** `end_date` column retained for future, unused by cron. Listers close manually. |
-| 7 | Submission cap per campaign | `max_submissions` nullable. In v1 listers must manually close; auto-close trigger deferred to v1.1. |
-| 8 | One active application per (creator, listing) | Partial unique index. |
-| 9 | Which edits cascade-cancel pending applications | **All versioned fields:** price, pre-conditions, post-conditions, sample videos, max_submissions. Title/description edits do NOT cascade. |
-| 10 | Post-condition edits cascade | **Yes** (same rule as #9). Confirmed in Q3. |
+| States | `draft → active → (paused \| closed) → archived`. No `expired` state. |

+| Application uniqueness | One active application per `(creator, listing)`. Partial unique index. |

+| Cascade-on-edit | Editing any versioned field — price, pre-conditions, post-conditions, sample videos, max_submissions — bumps a version and cascade-cancels pending applications with `cancelled_listing_edit`. Title/description edits do NOT cascade. |
-| 12 | Metrics drop between apply and approve | **Re-check at approve-time.** If now-ineligible, lister receives `409 INELIGIBLE_NOW` with `override_allowed=true`. No re-check at submit-time. |

-| 19 | Events + channels | Events: application approved/rejected/cancelled; submission approved/rejected; listing version changed; metrics refresh failed. Channels: in-app inbox + Realtime. Push deferred v1.1. |

## 8. MVP Scope Cuts (resolved)

1. **No payment** — `price` is display-only (Q2).
2. **No OAuth** — Apify scraping (Q1).
3. **No IG Business/Creator requirement** — Apify works on personal accounts too.
4. **No post-submission performance tracking** — v2 "Marketify Analytics".
5. **No lister dashboard analytics** — counts only.
6. **No admin moderation console** — reports → table, admin via Supabase Studio.
7. **No revision-request flow** — binary approve/reject (Q4).
8. **No push notifications** — in-app + Realtime only (v1.1).
9. **No auto-expire** for applications, submissions, or listings — manual close only (Q5).
10. **No auto-close-on-max-submissions** — v1.1.
11. **Sample videos: URL only** — no Storage upload in v1.
12. **No blocklists** — report-only.
13. **No in-app chat** — out of scope.
14. **No dark mode** — v2.
15. **No handle ownership verification** — first-to-register wins (tracked in §1.27).
16. **One role per account** (Q6).

---

## Appendix A: Resolved Questions

All seven open questions from v0.1 are resolved:

1. **Q1 (Apify):** Use Apify scrapers instead of platform OAuth. Two actors: `clockworks/tiktok-scraper` (one run per TikTok refresh) and `apify/instagram-scraper` (two runs per IG refresh: `resultsType=details` + `resultsType=posts`). No OAuth, no Business/Creator requirement. Handle is unverified in MVP.
2. **Q2:** No real money in MVP. `price` is display-only.
3. **Q3:** All versioned field edits cascade-cancel pending applications — including post-condition edits.
4. **Q4:** Revision-request flow cut from v1. Binary approve/reject only.
5. **Q5:** No auto-expire on applications, submissions, or listings. Only metric `stale` transitions run on cron.
6. **Q6:** One role per account.
7. **Q7:** Neubrutalism design direction confirmed (see `design.md`).

---

**Ready for build.** Step 1 is the migration from §4.7; step 2 is Apify client + webhook receiver edge function; step 3 is signup flows. See `product-plan.md` §6 for the full build sequence.

-15. **No handle ownership verification** — first-to-register wins (tracked in §1.27).
-All seven open questions from v0.1 are resolved:

-1. **Q1 (Apify):** Use Apify scrapers instead of platform OAuth. Two actors: `clockworks/tiktok-scraper` (one run per TikTok refresh) and `apify/instagram-scraper` (two runs per IG refresh: `resultsType=details` + `resultsType=posts`). No OAuth, no Business/Creator requirement. Handle is unverified in MVP.
-2. **Q2:** No real money in MVP. `price` is display-only.
-3. **Q3:** All versioned field edits cascade-cancel pending applications — including post-condition edits.
-4. **Q4:** Revision-request flow cut from v1. Binary approve/reject only.
-5. **Q5:** No auto-expire on applications, submissions, or listings. Only metric `stale` transitions run on cron.
**Ready for build.** Step 1 is the migration from §4.7; step 2 is Apify client + webhook receiver edge function; step 3 is signup flows. See `product-plan.md` §6 for the full build sequence.

**Self-affirm vs review checkbox mapping.** The Submission Composer (creator, §4.4) and Submission Review (lister, §4.6) render the same `ConditionChecklist` with different `mode` props:

- `mode='self'` (composer) → two-state: `☐` unchecked / `☑` checked. Every row starts unchecked; the creator affirms each one. No `✗` red state — a creator who can't honestly affirm a row just leaves it unchecked, and the client-side validator blocks submit until all rows are checked.

content.content:
1	# Marketify — Polished Product Plan
2	
3	*Synthesis of the initial PRD, the UI/UX design analysis (`design.md`), and the technical architecture analysis (`tech-architecture.md`). All seven founding product decisions have been resolved. This is the canonical plan; source docs hold authoritative detail.*
4	
5	---
6	
7	## 0. TL;DR
8	
9	A mobile marketplace (Expo + React Native + Supabase) where creators claim bounties from companies.
10	
11	- **Two roles**, chosen at onboarding (no dual-role accounts in v1).
12	- **Creators** sign up with a username + at least one social handle (TikTok and/or Instagram). **No OAuth** — we pull public profile data via **Apify scrapers** (`clockworks/tiktok-scraper` and `apify/instagram-scraper`). The app trusts whatever handle the creator types; handle ownership is unverified in v1.
13	- **Listers** sign up with username + email + org name.
14	- Listers post campaigns with **pre-conditions** (follower/view thresholds) and **post-conditions** (content rules).
15	- Creators see only eligible campaigns by default, apply, get approved, film, submit a video URL, get reviewed.
16	
17	**Design language:** Disciplined Neubrutalism — hard shadows, 2px ink borders, pink/orange creator-economy palette, Reanimated-driven micro-interactions.
18	
19	**No payments in v1.** `price_cents` is display-only; the creator–lister payment handshake happens off-platform.
20	
21	---
22	
23	## 1. The Core User Loop
24	
25	```
26	CREATOR                                   LISTER
27	────────                                  ──────
28	Sign up with username + handle(s)         Sign up with username + email
29	        │                                         │
30	(Apify scrapes profile                            ↓
31	 in background ~30s)                      Create campaign (5-step wizard)
32	        │                                         │
33	        ↓                                         ↓
34	Browse feed ←─────────────────── Publish → status=active
35	   │  (filter: eligible only — default)
36	   ↓
37	Tap campaign → eligibility evaluated live
38	   │
39	   ↓ (if eligible)
40	Apply (optional pitch) ─────────────────► Lister inbox (Realtime)
41	                                             │
42	                                             ↓
43	                              Approve / Reject application
44	   (Realtime push to creator) ◄──────────────┘
45	   │
46	   ↓ (if approved)
47	Film → paste video URL ─────────────────► Lister review (embedded video + post-condition checklist)
48	                                             │
49	                                             ↓
50	                              Approve / Reject submission
51	   (Realtime push to creator) ◄──────────────┘
52	```
53	
54	Two cross-cutting mechanics the PRD surfaced:
55	
56	- **Listing versioning + auto-cancel.** Editing price, pre-conditions, post-conditions, sample videos, or `max_submissions` bumps a version and cascades `cancelled_listing_edit` to all pending applications, with notifications.
57	- **Metrics refresh.** Creators can't edit their numbers. Pulled via Apify on sign-up, refreshed daily by cron, or manually pulled once per 6 hours per platform via pull-to-refresh.
58	
59	---
60	
61	## 2. Identity Model — Apify, not OAuth
62	
63	The PRD's original ask was *"no auth — users just select a role and create an account with a username and social media links."* Initially that looked infeasible because TikTok and Instagram APIs both require OAuth (TikTok needs the `user.info.stats` scope; IG needs a Business/Creator Graph API token) to expose follower and view counts.
64	
65	**Resolution (Q1 = Apify):** we skip the platform APIs entirely and scrape public profile pages via Apify.
66	
67	- **Creators** sign up with: username + at least one of `tiktok_handle`, `instagram_handle`. No password, no platform consent, no Business/Creator account conversion, no server-side OAuth token management.
68	- A custom JWT signed by our edge function is the session. No Supabase Auth.
69	- At signup, we kick three Apify runs (TikTok profile, IG details, IG posts/reels) with a 60s `waitSecs` — the common case is that metrics populate before the user finishes the next screen.
70	- **Trust model:** handles are *unverified.* A creator could type a handle they don't own, and we would scrape and display that account's metrics against their profile. In v1 we accept this risk because (a) it doesn't give them money — `price` is display-only — and (b) listers re-check eligibility at approval time and see the actual submitted video's author before approving. Verification can ship in v2 via a TikTok/IG "post this code in your bio" challenge if abuse emerges.
71	
72	**Trade-off with listers:** listers trust the displayed metrics when reviewing applications. If a creator spoofs a popular handle, they pass the numeric pre-check but the lister still judges the *submitted video* — the fraud is self-limiting. We surface handles in monospace (`@xxx`) so listers can tap through to verify before approving.
73	
74	---
75	
76	## 3. Scope — What Ships in v1
77	
78	### 3.1 IN (build this first)
79	
80	**Foundations**

# Marketify — Polished Product Plan

*Synthesis of the initial PRD, the UI/UX design analysis (`design.md`), and the technical architecture analysis (`tech-architecture.md`). All seven founding product decisions have been resolved. This is the canonical plan; source docs hold authoritative detail.*

---

## 0. TL;DR

A mobile marketplace (Expo + React Native + Supabase) where creators claim bounties from companies.

- **Two roles**, chosen at onboarding (no dual-role accounts in v1).
- **Creators** sign up with a username + at least one social handle (TikTok and/or Instagram). **No OAuth** — we pull public profile data via **Apify scrapers** (`clockworks/tiktok-scraper` and `apify/instagram-scraper`). The app trusts whatever handle the creator types; handle ownership is unverified in v1.
- **Listers** sign up with username + email + org name.
- Listers post campaigns with **pre-conditions** (follower/view thresholds) and **post-conditions** (content rules).
- Creators see only eligible campaigns by default, apply, get approved, film, submit a video URL, get reviewed.

**Design language:** Disciplined Neubrutalism — hard shadows, 2px ink borders, pink/orange creator-economy palette, Reanimated-driven micro-interactions.

**No payments in v1.** `price_cents` is display-only; the creator–lister payment handshake happens off-platform.

---

## 1. The Core User Loop

```
CREATOR                                   LISTER
────────                                  ──────
Sign up with username + handle(s)         Sign up with username + email
        │                                         │
(Apify scrapes profile                            ↓
 in background ~30s)                      Create campaign (5-step wizard)
        │                                         │
        ↓                                         ↓
Browse feed ←─────────────────── Publish → status=active
   │  (filter: eligible only — default)
   ↓
Tap campaign → eligibility evaluated live
   │
   ↓ (if eligible)
Apply (optional pitch) ─────────────────► Lister inbox (Realtime)
                                             │
                                             ↓
                              Approve / Reject application
   (Realtime push to creator) ◄──────────────┘
   │
   ↓ (if approved)
Film → paste video URL ─────────────────► Lister review (embedded video + post-condition checklist)
                                             │
                                             ↓
                              Approve / Reject submission
   (Realtime push to creator) ◄──────────────┘
```

Two cross-cutting mechanics the PRD surfaced:

- **Listing versioning + auto-cancel.** Editing price, pre-conditions, post-conditions, sample videos, or `max_submissions` bumps a version and cascades `cancelled_listing_edit` to all pending applications, with notifications.
- **Metrics refresh.** Creators can't edit their numbers. Pulled via Apify on sign-up, refreshed daily by cron, or manually pulled once per 6 hours per platform via pull-to-refresh.

---

## 2. Identity Model — Apify, not OAuth

The PRD's original ask was *"no auth — users just select a role and create an account with a username and social media links."* Initially that looked infeasible because TikTok and Instagram APIs both require OAuth (TikTok needs the `user.info.stats` scope; IG needs a Business/Creator Graph API token) to expose follower and view counts.

**Resolution (Q1 = Apify):** we skip the platform APIs entirely and scrape public profile pages via Apify.

- **Creators** sign up with: username + at least one of `tiktok_handle`, `instagram_handle`. No password, no platform consent, no Business/Creator account conversion, no server-side OAuth token management.
- A custom JWT signed by our edge function is the session. No Supabase Auth.
- At signup, we kick three Apify runs (TikTok profile, IG details, IG posts/reels) with a 60s `waitSecs` — the common case is that metrics populate before the user finishes the next screen.
- **Trust model:** handles are *unverified.* A creator could type a handle they don't own, and we would scrape and display that account's metrics against their profile. In v1 we accept this risk because (a) it doesn't give them money — `price` is display-only — and (b) listers re-check eligibility at approval time and see the actual submitted video's author before approving. Verification can ship in v2 via a TikTok/IG "post this code in your bio" challenge if abuse emerges.

**Trade-off with listers:** listers trust the displayed metrics when reviewing applications. If a creator spoofs a popular handle, they pass the numeric pre-check but the lister still judges the *submitted video* — the fraud is self-limiting. We surface handles in monospace (`@xxx`) so listers can tap through to verify before approving.

---

## 3. Scope — What Ships in v1

### 3.1 IN (build this first)

**Foundations**

Verification can ship in v2 via a TikTok/IG "post this code in your bio" challenge if abuse emerges.

**Trade-off with listers:** listers trust the displayed metrics when reviewing applications. If a creator spoofs a popular handle, they pass the numeric pre-check but the lister still judges the *submitted video* — the fraud is self-limiting. We surface handles in monospace (`@xxx`) so listers can tap through to verify before approving.

135	## 4. Key Technical Decisions — Resolved
136	
137	All seven founding questions now have final answers. Driver detail is in `tech-architecture.md` §3 (Apify) and Appendix A (resolved questions).
138	
139	| # | Decision | Value | Source |
140	|---|---|---|---|
141	| 1 | **Metric source** | Apify scrapers: `clockworks/tiktok-scraper` (1 run) and `apify/instagram-scraper` (2 runs: `resultsType=details` + `resultsType=posts`). No OAuth, no Business/Creator requirement. | Q1 = C |
142	| 2 | **Versioned fields** | `price`, `currency`, `max_submissions`, pre-conditions, **post-conditions**, sample videos. Editing any of these bumps a version and cascades `cancelled_listing_edit` to pending apps. | Q2 = A |
143	| 3 | **Role cardinality** | One role per account. Creator-listers make two accounts. | Q3 = A |
144	| 4 | **Revision flow** | Cut from v1 — approve or reject, no "request changes." Reserved column in schema for v1.1. | Q4 = A |
145	| 5 | **Auto-expiration** | None. Applications and submissions stay in their inbox until explicitly acted on. Listings only close when the lister closes them or `end_date` passes. | Q5 = C |
146	| 6 | **Submission re-use** | A single video URL may be submitted to multiple campaigns. Flagged in the lister's review UI (*"This video was also submitted to 2 other campaigns"*) but not blocked. | Q6 = A |
147	| 7 | **Eligibility re-check** | Re-evaluated at **lister approve-time**, not at submission-time. Prevents metric-gaming; approval locks the creator in at that moment's numbers. | Q7 = A |
148	
149	**Supporting architectural calls** (derived, not voted):
150	
151	- Denormalized creator metrics on `creator_profiles` (`tiktok_follower_count`, `ig_avg_views_last_10`, etc.) — populated by an `AFTER INSERT` trigger on `metric_snapshots`, scoped by `scrape_mode`.
152	- Generated columns `listings.min_followers_tiktok` / `min_followers_instagram` — btree-indexed for O(log n) feed filtering.
153	- `metric_snapshots.scrape_mode` enum (`tiktok_profile` | `ig_details` | `ig_posts`) — lets a single Apify webhook resolve into the correct subset of denormalized fields. IG's two runs land independently.
154	- Manual refresh throttle: 1 per 6h per `(user, platform)` — `429` returned from edge function before any Apify call.
155	
156	---
157	
158	## 5. Design System Summary
159	
160	Full details in `docs/design.md`. Highlights:
161	
162	- **Style:** Neubrutalism (hard shadows, 2px ink borders, 12px card radius, 999px pill radius).
163	- **Palette:** Pink primary `#EC4899`, Orange CTA `#F97316`, warm canvas `#FFF9F2`, four semantic status colors with soft-bg variants.
164	- **Typography:** Clash Display (display/heading), Satoshi (body/caption), JetBrains Mono (URLs/handles).
165	- **Motion:** Reanimated 3 worklets. Three spring presets (soft/snappy/bouncy). Press-collapse shadows on every pressable. Celebration burst on approval. Shake on rejection. `useReducedMotion` respected everywhere.
166	- **Icons:** Lucide React Native only. No emoji.
167	- **Tabs:** 4 per role. Creator = Discover / Applied / Submitted / Profile. Lister = Home / Campaigns / Inbox / Profile. Lister has a FAB for create-campaign.
168

## 4. Key Technical Decisions — Resolved

All seven founding questions now have final answers. Driver detail is in `tech-architecture.md` §3 (Apify) and Appendix A (resolved questions).

| # | Decision | Value | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Metric source** | Apify scrapers: `clockworks/tiktok-scraper` (1 run) and `apify/instagram-scraper` (2 runs: `resultsType=details` + `resultsType=posts`). No OAuth, no Business/Creator requirement. | Q1 = C |
| 2 | **Versioned fields** | `price`, `currency`, `max_submissions`, pre-conditions, **post-conditions**, sample videos. Editing any of these bumps a version and cascades `cancelled_listing_edit` to pending apps. | Q2 = A |
| 3 | **Role cardinality** | One role per account. Creator-listers make two accounts. | Q3 = A |
| 4 | **Revision flow** | Cut from v1 — approve or reject, no "request changes." Reserved column in schema for v1.1. | Q4 = A |
| 5 | **Auto-expiration** | None. Applications and submissions stay in their inbox until explicitly acted on. Listings only close when the lister closes them or `end_date` passes. | Q5 = C |
| 6 | **Submission re-use** | A single video URL may be submitted to multiple campaigns. Flagged in the lister's review UI (*"This video was also submitted to 2 other campaigns"*) but not blocked. | Q6 = A |
| 7 | **Eligibility re-check** | Re-evaluated at **lister approve-time**, not at submission-time. Prevents metric-gaming; approval locks the creator in at that moment's numbers. | Q7 = A |

**Supporting architectural calls** (derived, not voted):

- Denormalized creator metrics on `creator_profiles` (`tiktok_follower_count`, `ig_avg_views_last_10`, etc.) — populated by an `AFTER INSERT` trigger on `metric_snapshots`, scoped by `scrape_mode`.
- Generated columns `listings.min_followers_tiktok` / `min_followers_instagram` — btree-indexed for O(log n) feed filtering.
- `metric_snapshots.scrape_mode` enum (`tiktok_profile` | `ig_details` | `ig_posts`) — lets a single Apify webhook resolve into the correct subset of denormalized fields. IG's two runs land independently.
- Manual refresh throttle: 1 per 6h per `(user, platform)` — `429` returned from edge function before any Apify call.

---

## 5. Design System Summary

Full details in `docs/design.md`. Highlights:

- **Style:** Neubrutalism (hard shadows, 2px ink borders, 12px card radius, 999px pill radius).
- **Palette:** Pink primary `#EC4899`, Orange CTA `#F97316`, warm canvas `#FFF9F2`, four semantic status colors with soft-bg variants.
- **Typography:** Clash Display (display/heading), Satoshi (body/caption), JetBrains Mono (URLs/handles).
- **Motion:** Reanimated 3 worklets. Three spring presets (soft/snappy/bouncy). Press-collapse shadows on every pressable. Celebration burst on approval. Shake on rejection. `useReducedMotion` respected everywhere.
- **Icons:** Lucide React Native only. No emoji.
- **Tabs:** 4 per role. Creator = Discover / Applied / Submitted / Profile. Lister = Home / Campaigns / Inbox / Profile. Lister has a FAB for create-campaign.

---

input.new_string:
## 4. Key Technical Decisions

Detail lives in `tech-architecture.md` §1 (design decisions) and §3 (Apify integration).

| Area | Decision |
|---|---|
| **Metric source** | Apify scrapers: `clockworks/tiktok-scraper` (1 run per TikTok refresh) and `apify/instagram-scraper` (2 runs per IG refresh: `resultsType=details` + `resultsType=posts`). No OAuth, no Business/Creator requirement. |
| **Versioned fields** | `price`, `currency`, `max_submissions`, pre-conditions, post-conditions, sample videos. Editing any of these bumps a version and cascades `cancelled_listing_edit` to pending applications. |
| **Role cardinality** | One role per account. Dual-role users make two accounts. |
| **Revision flow** | Binary approve/reject. No "request changes" in v1; column reserved for v1.1. |
| **Auto-expiration** | None. Applications and submissions stay in their inbox until acted on. Listings close only when the lister closes them. |
| **Submission re-use** | A single video URL may be submitted to multiple campaigns. Surfaced to the lister via `ReuseBadge` ("also submitted to N other campaigns") but never blocked. |
| **Eligibility re-check** | Re-evaluated at lister approve-time, not at submission-time. Prevents metric-gaming; approval locks the creator in at that moment's numbers. Lister sees `OverrideEligibilityDialog` if the applicant drifted out of eligibility between apply and approve. |

**Supporting architectural calls:**

- Denormalized creator metrics on `creator_profiles` (`tiktok_follower_count`, `instagram_avg_views_last_10`, etc.) — populated by a `BEFORE INSERT` trigger on `metric_snapshots`, scoped by `scrape_mode`, serialized with `pg_advisory_xact_lock`.
- Trigger-maintained `listings.min_followers_tiktok` / `min_followers_instagram` cache columns, btree-indexed for O(log n) feed filtering. Plain `integer` columns (not `GENERATED ALWAYS AS`) because the source lives in another table.
- `metric_snapshots.scrape_mode` enum (`tiktok_profile` | `ig_details` | `ig_posts`) — lets a single Apify webhook resolve into the correct subset of denormalized fields. IG's two runs land independently and can partially succeed.
- Manual refresh throttle: 1 per 6h per `(user, platform)` — `429` returned from edge function before any Apify call.

11. **Submission composer + review.** Creator pastes URL → oEmbed validation → submit. Lister embeds video (WebView), checks post-conditions, approves/rejects. Status change animations wired.
12. **Notifications + polishing pass.** In-app inbox, bell badge, deep-link routing, listing-edit cascade UX (*"cancel 12 apps?"* modal), empty states, error states, reduced-motion audit.

**Target velocity:** a solo dev with this stack can land steps 1–4 in week 1, 5–7 in week 2, 8–10 in week 3, 11–12 in week 4. Four-week MVP is realistic.

---

## 7. Resolved Decisions Ledger

All seven founding questions are locked. Summarized below; full reasoning lives in `tech-architecture.md` §Appendix A.

| # | Question | Chosen | In effect |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | How do we get real follower/view counts with no OAuth? | **C — Apify scrapers** | `clockworks/tiktok-scraper` + `apify/instagram-scraper` (two modes); no OAuth, no Business/Creator requirement |
| 2 | Which fields trigger listing versioning? | **A** | Price, max_submissions, pre-conds, post-conds, sample videos all bump |
| 3 | Can one account be both creator and lister? | **A — one role per account** | Schema enforces single role; duals make two accounts |
| 4 | Revision flow on submissions? | **A — cut from v1** | Approve/reject only; column reserved for v1.1 |
| 5 | Auto-expire pending applications/submissions? | **C — no auto-expire** | Inbox stays open until explicit action |
| 6 | Submission re-use across campaigns? | **A — allowed, flagged not blocked** | Lister sees "also submitted to N others" chip |
| 7 | Eligibility re-check timing? | **A — at approve-time** | Server re-evaluates against current metrics before lister approves |

Any future pivots (e.g. handle verification flow, revision-request, push notifications) should be documented here as an addendum row rather than silently changing source docs.

---

- **`docs/design.md`** — full UI/UX language, screen catalog, wireframes, component inventory, motion spec.
- **`docs/tech-architecture.md`** — PRD gap analysis, state machines, Apify integration specs (actor catalog, input/output schemas, webhook wiring, cost management), complete Supabase schema + RLS + triggers + cron, edge-function spec sheet, eligibility engine, sequence diagrams, v1 scope cuts, resolved-question appendix.
- **`docs/product-plan.md`** (this file) — synthesis + decisions + build sequence.

All three are living docs. If a build-time decision forces a change, update the relevant source doc and add a row to §7 here if it alters a founding answer.

content.text:
## Summary

**Applied all 19 reviewer follow-ups:**

### Tech-architecture.md
- **Schema reconciliation** (B7): added `listings.category`, `social_links.handle_confirmed_at`, `submissions.override_by_user_id` + `override_reason` (with check constraint), `submission_reuse_view`, partial index for category+price feed filter
- **RLS DDL inlined** (B1): replaced the v0.1 stub with full `create policy` blocks for all 14 tables, including the previously-ghosted `submission_videos` policies and an integration test plan
- **POST /applications race fix** (B2): documented `SELECT FOR UPDATE` on listings + `409 LISTING_VERSION_CHANGED` error code
- **Version-bump ordering contract** (B3): documented the 6-step edge-function sequence; added `v_affected_version <> v_current` early-return in `refresh_listing_thresholds` so partial condition reads are impossible
- **Webhook status guard** (B4): terminal-state transition only from `refreshing`; added `metric_snapshots_stuck_idx` partial index
- **revoked_sessions cache** (B5): TTL dropped 15→5 min, force-reload on LISTEN reconnect, added `trg_revoked_sessions_notify` trigger
- **active_pending_applications_count maintainer** (B6): new `trg_maintain_pending_apps_counter`
- **cron-refresh-metrics overlap guard** (B8): `pg_try_advisory_xact_lock` on a reserved key
- Reset `social_links.fail_count = 0` on success snapshots

### Design.md
- **Rejected CTA filter** (B1): now references `listings.category` + `listings.price_cents` (real schema names)
- **AddHandleSheet spec** (B2): full state machine (input → dispatching → scraping → partial → fresh/failed), interaction with §5.6 confirmation, backend contract, wireframe
- **/onboarding/metrics wireframe** (B3): new §4.0 with per-run status rows, 5 completion rules, photo-only IG handling, full-error state, 90s "still working" copy
- **Self-affirm vs review checklist mapping** (B4), **shake gesture clarification** (B5), **deep-link back-stack per-row** (B6), **tab-bar stroke-weight toggle** (B7), **Dynamic Type 130% cap** (B8), **tiered rate-limit toast format** (B9), **EmptyState illustration registry keys** (B10)

### Cleanup (across all three docs)
- Dropped `v0.1`/`v0.2` versioning and changelog, collapsed "PRD Gap Analysis (resolved)" into "Design Decisions", removed the Appendix A resolved-questions list, stripped `(Q1)`–`(Q7)` tags throughout, retitled "Resolved Decisions Ledger" to plain "Key Technical Decisions", retitled the product plan from "Polished Product Plan" to "Product Plan".

All three documents now read as forward-looking specs rather than a working record of resolved debate. Ready for dev.

content.text:
Review this session for learnings about working with Claude Code in this codebase. Update CLAUDE.md with context that would help future Claude sessions be more effective.

## Step 1: Reflect

What context was missing that would have helped Claude work more effectively?
- Bash commands that were used or discovered
- Code style patterns followed
- Testing approaches that worked
- Environment/configuration quirks
- Warnings or gotchas encountered

## Step 2: Find CLAUDE.md Files

```bash
find . -name "CLAUDE.md" -o -name ".claude.local.md" 2>/dev/null | head -20
```

Decide where each addition belongs:
- `CLAUDE.md` - Team-shared (checked into git)
- `.claude.local.md` - Personal/local only (gitignored)

## Step 3: Draft Additions

**Keep it concise** - one line per concept. CLAUDE.md is part of the prompt, so brevity matters.

Format: `<command or pattern>` - `<brief description>`

Avoid:
- Verbose explanations
- Obvious information
- One-off fixes unlikely to recur

## Step 4: Show Proposed Changes

For each addition:

```
### Update: ./CLAUDE.md

**Why:** [one-line reason]

\`\`\`diff
+ [the addition - keep it brief]
\`\`\`
```

## Step 5: Apply with Approval

Ask if the user wants to apply the changes. Only edit files they approve.


ARGUMENTS: Create an optimized Claude.md file with instructions on how to interact with this codebase and the documentation throughout development and reach the end result seamlessly with official anthropic-claude best practices.
Let me check for existing CLAUDE.md files first.

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user is building **Marketify** — an Expo + React Native + Reanimated + Supabase mobile marketplace where creators claim bounties from companies (listers). This conversation continued from a prior summarized session where 32 reviewer-flagged issues had been fixed. The specific requests in this session were:
   
   a) **(Continued from prior):** Complete Task #6 — apply the remaining design.md fixes (Fixes 13-14 must-fix + 8 nice-to-haves).
   
   b) **Second review pass:** "again, pass the 3 finalized documents you wrote down back to the UI subagent and tech subagent and make them analyze/review the documents-specs, get their approval before we start development."
   
   c) **Apply follow-up fixes:** "apply the fixes" — address all 19 follow-ups (UI: 3 must-fix + 7 nice-to-haves; Tech: 8 follow-ups) from the second review round.
   
   d) **Cleanup:** "Cleanup the old information from the plan files (liked resolved gap issues)" — strip out Q1-Q7 question tags, v0.1/v0.2 versioning, "resolved" parentheticals, the "PRD Gap Analysis (resolved)" framing, and the "Appendix A: Resolved Questions" — so the docs read as forward-looking specs rather than a historical debate record.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Expo + React Native + Reanimated v3 (worklets, spring presets, `useReducedMotion`, `useFocusEffect`)
   - Supabase: Postgres + Edge Functions (Deno) + Realtime + Storage + pg_cron
   - Custom JWT auth (sub=user_id, role, session_id) — no Supabase Auth
   - Apify via `apify-client` with two actors: `clockworks/tiktok-scraper` (1 run per TT refresh) and `apify/instagram-scraper` (2 runs per IG refresh: `resultsType=details` + `resultsType=posts`)
   - `scrape_mode` enum (`tiktok_profile` | `ig_details` | `ig_posts`)
   - `pg_advisory_xact_lock` with `hashtextextended` for race-free trigger concurrency
   - Trigger-maintained cache columns (since GENERATED ALWAYS AS can't reference other tables)
   - Listing versioning with cascade-cancel via `version_bump_reason` ephemeral column
   - Webhook idempotency via partial unique on `apify_run_id`
   - Dual-secret rotation (`APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET` + `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET_PREVIOUS`)
   - LISTEN/NOTIFY for revoked_sessions invalidation
   - Neubrutalism design language (hard shadows, 2px ink borders, pink #EC4899 + orange CTA #F97316 on canvas #FFF9F2)
   - oEmbed for TikTok + Instagram video rendering
   - Dynamic Type / font scaling (capped at 130%)
   - Dispatching parallel agents skill for reviewer workflow

3. Files and Code Sections:
   
   - **`/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/docs/tech-architecture.md`**
     - Added `listings.category` column (default 'other') for feed taxonomy
     - Added partial index: `create index listings_category_price_idx on public.listings (category, price_cents) where status = 'active';`
     - Added `social_links.handle_confirmed_at timestamptz` for §5.6 first-scrape confirmation
     - Added `submissions.override_by_user_id uuid references public.users(id)` + `override_reason text` + check constraint:
       ```sql
       constraint submissions_override_requires_approved
         check ((override_by_user_id is null and override_reason is null)
                or status = 'approved')
       ```
     - Added `submission_reuse_view` — aggregates other submissions reusing the same video external_id per platform
     - Added `metric_snapshots_stuck_idx` partial index for janitor cron: `create index metric_snapshots_stuck_idx on public.metric_snapshots (fetched_at) where status = 'refreshing';`
     - **Inlined full RLS policy DDL** (replacing the v0.1 pointer stub) — wrote `create policy` statements for all 14 tables including the previously-ghosted `submission_videos` policies and a cross-tenant integration test plan (6 test cases)
     - Added **SELECT FOR UPDATE** contract to `POST /applications` + new error code `409 LISTING_VERSION_CHANGED`
     - Documented 6-step ordering contract in `request_listing_version_bump()` comments (insert versions → insert conditions → insert samples → swap current_version_id last)
     - Updated `refresh_listing_thresholds()` with early-return: `if v_affected_version <> v_current then return coalesce(new, old); end if;`
     - Updated webhook receiver flow with guarded UPDATE: `update public.metric_snapshots set status = :status_next ... where apify_run_id = :run_id and status = 'refreshing';`
     - Added reset: `On success: reset social_links.fail_count = 0 so the consecutive-failure counter only reflects uninterrupted failures.`
     - Updated `revoked_sessions` cache: TTL 15→5 min, force-reload on LISTEN reconnect, added:
       ```sql
       create or replace function app_private.notify_revoked_session()
       returns trigger language plpgsql as $
       begin
         perform pg_notify('revoked_sessions_invalidate', new.session_id::text);
         return new;
       end $;
       ```
     - Added `trg_maintain_pending_apps_counter` — tracks INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE on applications for `active_pending_applications_count`
     - Added `pg_try_advisory_xact_lock(hashtextextended('cron-refresh-metrics', 0))` overlap guard to cron-refresh-metrics
     - **Cleanup:** removed `**Changelog from v0.1:** ...` from header; retitled "## 1. PRD Gap Analysis (resolved)" → "## 1. Design Decisions" with simplified 2-col tables; removed Q1-Q7 tags throughout; removed "## Appendix A: Resolved Questions" entirely; replaced "## 8. MVP Scope Cuts (resolved)" → "## 8. MVP Scope Cuts"; removed "Unchanged from v0.1 —" prefix
   
   - **`/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/docs/design.md`**
     - Added 3 components to §6 inventory: `ReuseBadge`, `OverrideEligibilityDialog`, `MetricStaleIndicator`
     - Added `AddHandleSheet` component row to inventory
     - Added new **§4.0 "Onboarding — Fetching Metrics (Creator)"** with full wireframe, per-run status rows (dispatching/refreshing/fresh/failed), 5 completion rules including photo-only IG case, full-error state wireframe, 90s "still working" copy
     - Added **§4.2 AddHandleSheet inline flow** — complete state machine: `input → dispatching → scraping → partial → fresh|failed`, with wireframe, backend contract (`POST /social-links`), interaction with §5.6 confirmation card
     - Added **§5.3 rejected-creator escape**: "Find a similar bounty" primary CTA using `listings.category` + `listings.price_cents` with ±30% price band via `listings_category_price_idx`
     - Added **§5.6 "Is this you?"** first-scrape handle confirmation card
     - Added §2.5 age-bucketed nudge banners (7d/5d/14d thresholds)
     - Added §3.2 Inbox tab = cross-campaign lens over §2.3 campaign-scoped routes
     - Added §4.6 wireframe additions: ReuseBadge header chip + OverrideEligibilityDialog with typed OVERRIDE confirmation
     - Softened §1.2 AAA contrast claim (pink #EC4899 is ~3.3:1, AA-only; use `--primary-deep` #831843 for body-size text)
     - Added §1.4 motion rule 3: deep-link celebration anchor with MMKV `celebration_seen_for_submission_id` gate
     - Added §1.4 motion rule 8: stagger skip on screen re-entry via `useFocusEffect`-tracked `hasMountedOnce` ref
     - Added §5.2 cascade copy warning (no re-notify for creators cancelled by prior edits)
     - Added self-affirm vs review 3-state checkbox mode mapping
     - Clarified shake gesture: "one shake gesture made of 3 oscillation cycles (±4px each, 120ms per half-cycle, ~720ms total)"
     - Added back-stack seed column to deep-link table (per-row spine rules)

     - Added Dynamic Type 130% cap via `Text.defaultProps.maxFontSizeMultiplier = 1.3`
     - Added **tiered rate-limit toast format**: ≥1h → "Try again in Xh Ym."; 60s≤t<1h → "Try again in Xm."; <60s → "Try again in a few seconds."
     - Added `EmptyStateIllustrationKey` registry with 9 ship-in-repo SVG keys
     - Added 2 new pre-delivery checklist items (Dynamic Type + EmptyState registry)
     - **Cleanup:** removed all `(Q6)` and `(Q7)` tags from ReuseBadge/OverrideEligibilityDialog descriptions
   
   - **`/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/docs/product-plan.md`**
     - **Cleanup:** retitled `# Marketify — Polished Product Plan` → `# Marketify — Product Plan`; reworked opening subtitle; rewrote "## 2. Identity Model — Apify, not OAuth" to drop "Resolution (Q1 = Apify)" framing; retitled "## 4. Key Technical Decisions — Resolved" → "## 4. Key Technical Decisions" with 2-col table dropping "Source" column; removed entire "## 7. Resolved Decisions Ledger" section; renumbered §8 → §7 "Reference Artifacts"; updated closing line

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **Reviewers caught design↔schema disagreement**: design.md referenced `category`, `price_per_submission`, `override_by_user_id`, `override_reason`, `handle_confirmed_at`, `submission_reuse_view` — none of which existed in schema. **Fix:** added all of these to tech-architecture.md schema (kept canonical name `price_cents`, updated design copy to match).
   - **RLS policy DDL was stubbed** (`"(Full policy DDL as in v0.1 §4.7, minus the oauth_states policies)"`) — not enforceable. **Fix:** inlined complete `create policy` statements for all 14 tables with integration test plan.
   - **POST /applications race with bump_listing_version** — concurrent insert could pin stale version. **Fix:** documented `SELECT FOR UPDATE` on listings row + `409 LISTING_VERSION_CHANGED` error.
   - **Version-bump partial-read race** — per-row `refresh_listing_thresholds` could see half the conditions during multi-condition edits. **Fix:** ordering contract (insert conditions against non-current version, swap last) + trigger early-returns when affected version isn't current.
   - **Webhook retry could clobber fresh snapshot** — retried ABORTED after SUCCEEDED. **Fix:** guarded UPDATE only transitions from `refreshing` (terminal states stay put).
   - **revoked_sessions 15-min TTL** was security-material for custom auth. **Fix:** dropped to 5 min + force-reload on LISTEN reconnect + NOTIFY trigger.
   - **active_pending_applications_count was declared orphan** (no maintainer trigger). **Fix:** added `trg_maintain_pending_apps_counter`.
   - **cron-refresh-metrics could overlap itself** during Apify slowdowns. **Fix:** `pg_try_advisory_xact_lock` guard.
   - **User feedback on reviewer flow:** user wants full reviewer sign-off before dev — emphasized this twice ("get their approval before we start development"). **Adaptation:** dispatched reviewers in parallel both times and clearly relayed APPROVED WITH FOLLOW-UPS verdicts.
   - **Tool loaded.** prompts for ToolSearch deferred tools — loaded TaskUpdate/TaskList when reminded.

5. Problem Solving:
   - **Reconciled design↔schema mismatch** identified independently by both reviewers (UI-B1 and Tech-B7). Added columns to schema rather than softening design copy.
   - **Established version-bump ordering contract** to eliminate partial condition reads during multi-row edits — relies on the invariant that `current_version_id` swap is atomic and last.
   - **Solved webhook idempotency under Apify retry semantics** via terminal-state guard (first terminal signal wins; retries are 200 OK no-ops).
   - **Dispatched 2 parallel reviewer agents** in one message using the dispatching-parallel-agents pattern; both returned APPROVED WITH FOLLOW-UPS (neither blocked development).
   - **Consolidated cleanup** across three files: retained the decisions themselves but stripped the Q-tagged historical framing, version numbers, gap-analysis headers, and resolved-questions appendix.

6. All user messages:
   - (Implicit continuation from prior summarized session — was completing Task #6 design.md fixes)
   - "again, pass the 3 finalized documents you wrote down back to the UI subagent and tech subagent and make them analyze/review the documents-specs, get their approval before we start development."

   - "CRITICAL: Respond with TEXT ONLY. Do NOT call any tools. ..." (current summary request)

7. Pending Tasks:

The final recommendation to the user was: "All three documents now read as forward-looking specs rather than a working record of resolved debate. Ready for dev."

Then attempted to mark Task #6 completed (which returned "Task not found" — already marked complete). Produced a detailed summary of all 19 follow-ups + the cleanup pass for the user.

9. Optional Next Step:

skills.name: superpowers:dispatching-parallel-agents
skills.content:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/bulutyazgan/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/superpowers/5.0.7/skills/dispatching-parallel-agents

# Dispatching Parallel Agents

## Overview

You delegate tasks to specialized agents with isolated context. By precisely crafting their instructions and context, you ensure they stay focused and succeed at their task. They should never inherit your session's context or history — you construct exactly what they need. This also preserves your own context for coordination work.

When you have multiple unrelated failures (different test files, different subsystems, different bugs), investigating them sequentially wastes time. Each investigation is independent and can happen in parallel.

**Core principle:** Dispatch one agent per independent problem domain. Let them work concurrently.

## When to Use

```dot
digraph when_to_use {
    "Multiple failures?" [shape=diamond];
    "Are they independent?" [shape=diamond];
    "Single agent investigates all" [shape=box];
    "One agent per problem domain" [shape=box];
    "Can they work in parallel?" [shape=diamond];
    "Sequential agents" [shape=box];
    "Parallel dispatch" [shape=box];

    "Multiple failures?" -> "Are they independent?" [label="yes"];
    "Are they independent?" -> "Single agent investigates all" [label="no - related"];
    "Are they independent?" -> "Can they work in parallel?" [label="yes"];
    "Can they work in parallel?" -> "Parallel dispatch" [label="yes"];
    "Can they work in parallel?" -> "Sequential agents" [label="no - shared state"];
}
```

**Use when:**
- 3+ test files failing with different root causes
- Multiple subsystems broken independently
- Each problem can be understood without context from others
- No shared state between investigations

**Don't use when:**
- Failures are related (fix one might fix others)
- Need to understand full system state
- Agents would interfere with each other

## The Pattern

### 1. Identify Independent Domains

Group failures by what's broken:
- File A tests: Tool approval flow
- File B tests: Batch completion behavior
- File C tests: Abort functionality

Each domain is independent - fixing tool approval doesn't affect abort tests.

### 2. Create Focused Agent Tasks

Each agent gets:
- **Specific scope:** One test file or subsystem
- **Clear goal:** Make these tests pass
- **Constraints:** Don't change other code
- **Expected output:** Summary of what you found and fixed

### 3. Dispatch in Parallel

```typescript
// In Claude Code / AI environment
Task("Fix agent-tool-abort.test.ts failures")
Task("Fix batch-completion-behavior.test.ts failures")
Task("Fix tool-approval-race-conditions.test.ts failures")
// All three run concurrently
```

### 4. Review and Integrate

When agents return:
- Read each summary
- Verify fixes don't conflict
- Run full test suite
- Integrate all changes

## Agent Prompt Structure

Good agent prompts are:
1. **Focused** - One clear problem domain
2. **Self-contained** - All context needed to understand the problem
3. **Specific about output** - What should the agent return?

```markdown
Fix the 3 failing tests in src/agents/agent-tool-abort.test.ts:

1. "should abort tool with partial output capture" - expects 'interrupted at' in message
2. "should handle mixed completed and aborted tools" - fast tool aborted instead of completed
3. "should properly track pendingToolCount" - expects 3 results but gets 0

These are timing/race condition issues. Your task:

1. Read the test file and understand what each test verifies
2. Identify root cause - timing issues or actual bugs?
3. Fix by:
   - Replacing arbitrary timeouts with event-based waiting
   - Fixing bugs in abort implementation if found
   - Adjusting test expectations if testing changed behavior

Do NOT just increase timeouts - find the real issue.

Return: Summary of what you found and what you fixed.
```

## Common Mistakes

**❌ Too broad:** "Fix all the tests" - agent gets lost
**✅ Specific:** "Fix agent-tool-abort.test.ts" - focused scope

**❌ No context:** "Fix the race condition" - agent doesn't know where
**✅ Context:** Paste the error messages and test names

**❌ No constraints:** Agent might refactor everything
**✅ Constraints:** "Do NOT change production code" or "Fix tests only"

**❌ Vague output:** "Fix it" - you don't know what changed
**✅ Specific:** "Return summary of root cause and changes"

## When NOT to Use

**Related failures:** Fixing one might fix others - investigate together first
**Need full context:** Understanding requires seeing entire system
**Exploratory debugging:** You don't know what's broken yet
**Shared state:** Agents would interfere (editing same files, using same resources)

## Real Example from Session

**Scenario:** 6 test failures across 3 files after major refactoring

**Failures:**
- agent-tool-abort.test.ts: 3 failures (timing issues)
- batch-completion-behavior.test.ts: 2 failures (tools not executing)
- tool-approval-race-conditions.test.ts: 1 failure (execution count = 0)

**Decision:** Independent domains - abort logic separate from batch completion separate from race conditions

**Dispatch:**
```
Agent 1 → Fix agent-tool-abort.test.ts
Agent 2 → Fix batch-completion-behavior.test.ts
Agent 3 → Fix tool-approval-race-conditions.test.ts
```

**Results:**
- Agent 1: Replaced timeouts with event-based waiting
- Agent 2: Fixed event structure bug (threadId in wrong place)
- Agent 3: Added wait for async tool execution to complete

**Integration:** All fixes independent, no conflicts, full suite green

**Time saved:** 3 problems solved in parallel vs sequentially

## Key Benefits

1. **Parallelization** - Multiple investigations happen simultaneously
2. **Focus** - Each agent has narrow scope, less context to track
3. **Independence** - Agents don't interfere with each other
4. **Speed** - 3 problems solved in time of 1

## Verification

After agents return:
1. **Review each summary** - Understand what changed
2. **Check for conflicts** - Did agents edit same code?
3. **Run full suite** - Verify all fixes work together
4. **Spot check** - Agents can make systematic errors

## Real-World Impact

From debugging session (2025-10-03):
- 6 failures across 3 files
- 3 agents dispatched in parallel
- All investigations completed concurrently
- All fixes integrated successfully
- Zero conflicts between agent changes

## plugin:context7:context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

content:
<EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>
You have superpowers.

**Below is the full content of your 'superpowers:using-superpowers' skill - your introduction to using skills. For all other skills, use the 'Skill' tool:**

---
name: using-superpowers
description: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions
---

<SUBAGENT-STOP>
If you were dispatched as a subagent to execute a specific task, skip this skill.
</SUBAGENT-STOP>

<EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>
If you think there is even a 1% chance a skill might apply to what you are doing, you ABSOLUTELY MUST invoke the skill.

IF A SKILL APPLIES TO YOUR TASK, YOU DO NOT HAVE A CHOICE. YOU MUST USE IT.

This is not negotiable. This is not optional. You cannot rationalize your way out of this.
</EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>

## Instruction Priority

Superpowers skills override default system prompt behavior, but **user instructions always take precedence**:

1. **User's explicit instructions** (CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, AGENTS.md, direct requests) — highest priority
2. **Superpowers skills** — override default system behavior where they conflict
3. **Default system prompt** — lowest priority

If CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, or AGENTS.md says "don't use TDD" and a skill says "always use TDD," follow the user's instructions. The user is in control.

## How to Access Skills

**In Claude Code:** Use the `Skill` tool. When you invoke a skill, its content is loaded and presented to you—follow it directly. Never use the Read tool on skill files.

**In Copilot CLI:** Use the `skill` tool. Skills are auto-discovered from installed plugins. The `skill` tool works the same as Claude Code's `Skill` tool.

**In Gemini CLI:** Skills activate via the `activate_skill` tool. Gemini loads skill metadata at session start and activates the full content on demand.

**In other environments:** Check your platform's documentation for how skills are loaded.

## Platform Adaptation

Skills use Claude Code tool names. Non-CC platforms: see `references/copilot-tools.md` (Copilot CLI), `references/codex-tools.md` (Codex) for tool equivalents. Gemini CLI users get the tool mapping loaded automatically via GEMINI.md.

# Using Skills

## The Rule

**Invoke relevant or requested skills BEFORE any response or action.** Even a 1% chance a skill might apply means that you should invoke the skill to check. If an invoked skill turns out to be wrong for the situation, you don't need to use it.

```dot
digraph skill_flow {
    "User message received" [shape=doublecircle];
    "About to EnterPlanMode?" [shape=doublecircle];
    "Already brainstormed?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke brainstorming skill" [shape=box];
    "Might any skill apply?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke Skill tool" [shape=box];
    "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'" [shape=box];
    "Has checklist?" [shape=diamond];
    "Create TodoWrite todo per item" [shape=box];
    "Follow skill exactly" [shape=box];
    "Respond (including clarifications)" [shape=doublecircle];

    "About to EnterPlanMode?" -> "Already brainstormed?";
    "Already brainstormed?" -> "Invoke brainstorming skill" [label="no"];
    "Already brainstormed?" -> "Might any skill apply?" [label="yes"];
    "Invoke brainstorming skill" -> "Might any skill apply?";

    "User message received" -> "Might any skill apply?";
    "Might any skill apply?" -> "Invoke Skill tool" [label="yes, even 1%"];
    "Might any skill apply?" -> "Respond (including clarifications)" [label="definitely not"];
    "Invoke Skill tool" -> "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'";
    "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'" -> "Has checklist?";
    "Has checklist?" -> "Create TodoWrite todo per item" [label="yes"];
    "Has checklist?" -> "Follow skill exactly" [label="no"];
    "Create TodoWrite todo per item" -> "Follow skill exactly";
}
```

## Red Flags

These thoughts mean STOP—you're rationalizing:

| Thought | Reality |
|---------|---------|
| "This is just a simple question" | Questions are tasks. Check for skills. |
| "I need more context first" | Skill check comes BEFORE clarifying questions. |
| "Let me explore the codebase first" | Skills tell you HOW to explore. Check first. |
| "I can check git/files quickly" | Files lack conversation context. Check for skills. |
| "Let me gather information first" | Skills tell you HOW to gather information. |
| "This doesn't need a formal skill" | If a skill exists, use it. |
| "I remember this skill" | Skills evolve. Read current version. |
| "This doesn't count as a task" | Action = task. Check for skills. |
| "The skill is overkill" | Simple things become complex. Use it. |
| "I'll just do this one thing first" | Check BEFORE doing anything. |
| "This feels productive" | Undisciplined action wastes time. Skills prevent this. |
| "I know what that means" | Knowing the concept ≠ using the skill. Invoke it. |

## Skill Priority

When multiple skills could apply, use this order:

1. **Process skills first** (brainstorming, debugging) - these determine HOW to approach the task
2. **Implementation skills second** (frontend-design, mcp-builder) - these guide execution

"Let's build X" → brainstorming first, then implementation skills.
"Fix this bug" → debugging first, then domain-specific skills.

## Skill Types

**Rigid** (TDD, debugging): Follow exactly. Don't adapt away discipline.

**Flexible** (patterns): Adapt principles to context.

The skill itself tells you which.

## User Instructions

Instructions say WHAT, not HOW. "Add X" or "Fix Y" doesn't mean skip workflows.


</EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>

content.text:
No existing CLAUDE.md. Project is pre-development — only the three spec docs exist. Here's a proposed team-shared CLAUDE.md tuned to what we learned this session.

### Proposed: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/CLAUDE.md`

**Why:** Preserves spec-first workflow, critical invariants, and the design↔schema sync rule that bit us this session.

```markdown
# Marketify

Expo + React Native (Reanimated v3) + Supabase marketplace where creators claim bounties from listers.

## Project state

Pre-development. **The three docs in `docs/` are canonical** — treat them as the spec, not a draft:
- `docs/product-plan.md` — what we're building and why
- `docs/design.md` — UX, wireframes, component inventory, motion rules
- `docs/tech-architecture.md` — schema, RLS, edge functions, cron, invariants

Build sequence (from product-plan.md §7): migration → Apify client + webhook receiver → signup → feed → eligibility → application → submission composer → notifications.

## Working with the docs

- **Docs are forward-looking specs.** No `v0.x`, no "resolved" tags, no gap-analysis framing. Keep it that way.
- **Design ↔ schema must stay in sync.** Any column/view referenced in `design.md` must exist in `tech-architecture.md`. When they drift, add to schema — don't soften design.
- **Use parallel reviewer agents before big changes.** Dispatch one UI-focused and one tech-focused `general-purpose` agent in a single message; demand explicit APPROVED / APPROVED-WITH-FOLLOW-UPS / BLOCKED verdicts.
- Canonical column name: `listings.price_cents` (not `price_per_submission`).

## Stack

- **Mobile:** Expo, React Native, Reanimated v3 (worklets, `useReducedMotion`, `useFocusEffect`), MMKV
- **Backend:** Supabase — Postgres, Edge Functions (Deno), Realtime, Storage, pg_cron
- **Auth:** custom JWT (`sub=user_id, role, session_id`). Not Supabase Auth.
- **Metrics:** Apify via `apify-client`. Actors: `clockworks/tiktok-scraper` (1 run/TT), `apify/instagram-scraper` (2 runs/IG: `details` + `posts`).
- **Video embeds:** oEmbed (TikTok + Instagram).

## Invariants — do not violate

- **Version-bump ordering:** insert new version → insert conditions/samples against it → swap `current_version_id` **last**. Triggers early-return if `affected_version ≠ current_version`.
- **Webhook idempotency:** `metric_snapshots` UPDATE only transitions from `status = 'refreshing'`. Terminal states stay put — retries become 200 OK no-ops. Partial unique on `apify_run_id`.
- **POST /applications:** `SELECT FOR UPDATE` on the listings row; mismatched version → `409 LISTING_VERSION_CHANGED`.
- **revoked_sessions cache:** 5-min TTL, force-reload on LISTEN reconnect, `pg_notify('revoked_sessions_invalidate', session_id)` on insert.
- **Concurrency guards:** `pg_try_advisory_xact_lock(hashtextextended(key, 0))` for cron overlaps and race-prone triggers.
- **Dual-secret rotation:** `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET` + `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET_PREVIOUS` both accepted during rotation window.

## Design system

- Neubrutalism: canvas `#FFF9F2`, pink `#EC4899` (AA-only, use `--primary-deep` `#831843` for body text), orange CTA `#F97316`, 2px ink borders, hard shadows.
- Dynamic Type capped at 130% (`Text.defaultProps.maxFontSizeMultiplier = 1.3`).
- Tab bar uses stroke-weight toggle (1.5 → 2.5 + fills) — Lucide has no filled variants.
- Shake gesture: 3 oscillations, ±4px, 120ms/half-cycle, ~720ms total.
- Rate-limit toast tiers: ≥1h "Xh Ym" / 60s–1h "Xm" / <60s "a few seconds".
- Stagger animations skip on screen re-entry via `useFocusEffect` + `hasMountedOnce` ref.

## When writing code (once dev starts)

- No Supabase Auth helpers — custom JWT flow.
- Edge functions live under `supabase/functions/<name>/index.ts` (Deno).
- Migrations: one concern per file, numbered. Never edit a shipped migration.
- RLS on every user-facing table. Cross-tenant integration tests required.
- Trigger-maintained cache columns (Postgres `GENERATED ALWAYS AS` can't reference other tables).
```
